blocks:
سُورة الأعراف

مكّيّة وهي 206 آية

سورة الأعراف

مكية عدد آيها مائتان وست آيات بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) المص ** (1) Alif. Lām. Mīm. Ṣād. The discussion regarding its interpretation has already passed in the beginning of Sūrat al-Baqarah. And in al-Ma‘ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a tradition: And المص ** means: I am Allah, the All-Powerful (المقتدر), the Truthful (الصادق). And in it [i.e., al-Ma‘ani] and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man from Banu Umayyah, who was a zindiq (زنديق), came to him and said to him regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in His Book: المص —what did He intend by this? And what is in it regarding the lawful (الحَلال) and the unlawful (الحرام)? And what is in it that benefits the people? He [Imam al-Sadiq] said: So, he became angry at that and said: "Hold your tongue, woe betide you! The Alif (الألف) is one (1), the Lām (اللّام) is thirty (30), the Mīm (الميم) is forty (40), and the Ṣād (الصاد) is ninety (90). How much do you have with you?" The man said: "One hundred and sixty-one (161)." He [Imam al-Sadiq] said: "When the year one hundred and sixty-one (161) passes, the dominion of your companions will cease." He said: So, he observed. When the one hundred and sixty-first year passed, on the Day of Ashura (يوم عاشوراء), the Black-Banners (المسودة) (١) entered Kufa (الكوفة) and their dominion vanished.

(٢) كِتابٌ أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ فَلا يَكُنْ فِي صَدْرِكَ حَرَجٌ مِنْهُ لِتُنْذِرَ بِهِ وَذِكْرى لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٢) (A Book sent down to you—so let there be no constraint in your breast on its account—that you may warn thereby, and as an admonition to the faithful.)

كِتابٌ ** (A Book) — it is a Book sent down to you. فَلا يَكُنْ فِي صَدْرِكَ حَرَجٌ مِنْهُ ** (so let there be no constraint in your breast on its account) — a constriction (ضِيقٌ) regarding its conveyance (تَبْلِيغِهِ).

It was said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم feared the denial of his people and their turning away from accepting his saying. And when he intended it, his breast would become constricted (يَضِيقُ صَدْرُهُ) regarding the performance (الأَدَاءِ) and would not be expanded for it. So Allah secured him by means of this Ayah and commanded Him to abandon caring about them (مُبَالَاتِهِمْ).

لِتُنْذِرَ بِهِ ** (that you may warn thereby): meaning, it was sent down to you for your warning by means of it. وَذِكْرى ** (and as an admonition): and as a reminder (تَذْكِيرًا). لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (to the faithful.)

(٣) اتَّبِعُوا ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (3) Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, meaning the Quran and the revelation (الوحي). وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءَ and do not follow any masters besides Him. (Do not follow) the devils (شياطين) among mankind (الإِنسِ) and jinn (الجنّ), lest they carry you toward whims (الأهواءِ) and innovations (البدع) and lead you astray from the religion of Allah and from that which you were commanded to follow. قَلِيلاً ما تَذَكَّرُونَ ** Little is it that you take admonition! You take admonition (تَذَكَّرُونَ) only a little admonition (تذكراً قليلاً). And it was read with a light dhāl (خفيفة الذال) (as تَذْكُرُونَ), and (it was read as) يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (in the third person plural), and (it was read) in the third person (بالغيبة) as an address to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

footnote (١) المسوِّدة بكسر الواو أي لابسي السّواد ومنه الحديث فدخلت علينا المسوّدة يعني أصحاب الدعوة العباسية لأنّهم كانوا يلبسون ثياباً سوداء وعيسى ابن موسى أوّل من لبس لباس العبّاسيين من العلويّين استحوذ عليهم الشياطين وأغمرهم لباس الجاهلية. (1) المسوِّدة (with a kasrah on the wāw) means those who wear black (لابسي السّواد). From this is the Hadith: "So al-Musawwadah entered upon us," meaning the proponents of the Abbasid call (أصحاب الدعوة العباسية), because they used to wear black garments (ثياباً سوداء). And ʿĪsā ibn Mūsā was the first among the ʿAlawites (العلويّين) to wear the clothing of the Abbasids (لباس العبّاسيين). The devils (الشياطين) gained mastery over them, and the clothing of the Age of Ignorance (لباس الجاهلية) overwhelmed them.

(٤) وَكَمْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ ** (4) وَكَمْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ ** And many a town أَهْلَكْناها فَجاءَها ** أَهْلَكْناها فَجاءَها ** then its people were overtaken بَأْسُنا ** بَأْسُنا ** by Our punishment (عذابنا) بَياتاً ** بَياتاً ** while sleeping at night (بآيتين), like the people of Lot أَوْ هُمْ قائِلُونَ (١) أَوْ هُمْ قائِلُونَ (١) or while they were resting at midday (قائلين نصف النهار), like the people of Shu'ayb meaning He seized them while they were heedless (غفلة) and secure (أمن), and during times of ease (دعة) and rest (استراحة).

وَكَمْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْناها فَجاءَها بَأْسُنا بَياتاً أَوْ هُمْ قائِلُونَ (٤) (And how many a town We destroyed! Our might came upon it by night, or while they were resting at noon.)

The Almighty's saying: (٥) فَما كانَ دَعْواهُمْ إِذْ جاءَهُمْ بَأْسُنا إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ (So their cry, when Our might overtook them, was only to say: 'Indeed, we have been wrongdoers!')

[The meaning of] فَما كانَ دَعْواهُمْ is what they used to claim regarding their religion, or their supplication and seeking help, when Our might overtook them, [it was] except their confession of its falsehood and of their wrongdoing in what they were upon, and their regret over what had occurred from them.

(6) فَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ ** (6) We will surely question those to whom [apostles] were sent, meaning the nations, concerning their acceptance of the message and their response to the Messengers. وَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** and We will surely question the apostles. meaning the Prophets, concerning the conveyance of the message with which they were entrusted.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a الحديث: The Messengers will be made to stand, and they will be questioned concerning the conveyance of the messages with which they were entrusted to their nations. They will inform that they have indeed conveyed that to their nations. And the nations will be questioned, but they will deny, just as Allah said: فَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَنَسْئَلَنَّ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. We will surely question those to whom [apostles] were sent, and We will surely question the apostles. The الحديث—its entirety has already passed in سورة النساءِ (Chapter of Women) during the exegesis of: فَكَيْفَ إِذا جِئْنا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ. How will it be when We bring a witness from every nation?

(٧) فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (7) We shall surely narrate to them (We shall narrate) to the Messengers and those to whom they were sent, what occurred from them (the people). بِعِلْمٍ ** (We shall narrate) with knowledge, being fully aware of their outward (الظاهِرة) and inward (الباطنة) states. **وَما كُنَّا غائِبِينَ ** and We were not absent. (We were not absent) from them, nor from their deeds and their states. The purpose of the questioning is to rebuke and establish the proof against them, and the increase in the joy of those rewarded through praise upon them, and the grief of those punished by the manifestation of their vile deeds.

(٨) وَالْوَزْنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُ ** (8) And the weighing on that day will be the truth. meaning the weighing of deeds and the distinction between their lightness and their preponderance. Al-Qummi said: The recompense is based on the deeds: if good, then good; and if evil, then evil. He said: And this is His saying: فَمَنْ ثَقُلَتْ ** (the Ayah). فَمَنْ ثَقُلَتْ مَوازِينُهُ ** So as for those whose scales are heavy, (meaning) his good deeds (حسناته). It is the plural of موزون (that which is weighed). In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He only means the good deeds (الحسنات). The good deeds (الحسنات) and the evil deeds (السيئات) are weighed. The good deeds (الحسنات) are the heaviness of the scale (ثقل الميزان), and the evil deeds (السيئات) are the lightness of the scale (خفة الميزان). And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is the fewness of the good deeds (الحسنات) and their abundance. فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** it is they who will be the felicitous. (meaning) those who attain salvation (النجاة) and reward (الثواب).

footnote (١) The Almighty's saying: **وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلاً ** (and a better resting place). This is derived from *القائلة* (midday rest), which is seeking shelter/rest (*استكنان*) at midday (*وقت نصف النهار*). And in the *Tafsir*, it is stated that the Day of Resurrection will not reach midday (*لا ينتصف النهار*) until the people of Paradise settle in Paradise and the people of the Fire settle in the Fire.

(9) وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوازِينُهُ فَأُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (9) But as for those whose scales are light, they are the ones who have ruined their souls, by wasting the sound nature (الفطرة السليمة) upon which they were created, and by committing that which exposed them to the punishment. بِما كانُوا بِآياتِنا يَظْلِمُونَ because they used to wrong Our signs. so they deny instead of affirming belief (التصديق). And al-Qummi said: they reject the Imams (الأئمة).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked, "Are not deeds weighed?" He said: "No, because deeds are not bodies (أجساماً); rather, they are the description (صفة) of what they did. Only he who is ignorant of the number of things and does not know their heaviness or lightness needs to weigh a thing, and nothing is hidden from Allah." He was asked: "Then what is the meaning of the Balance (الميزان)?" He said: "Justice (العدل)." He was asked: "Then what is its meaning in His Book: فَمَنْ ثَقُلَتْ مَوازِينُهُ (Then as for those whose scales are heavy)?" He said: "It means: those whose deeds outweigh."

I say: The secret of this is that the balance (ميزان) of everything is the standard (المعيار) by which the worth (قدر) of that thing is known. Thus, the balance of people on the Day of Resurrection is that by which the worth and value (قيمته) of every human being is weighed, according to his belief (عقيدته), character (خلقه), and deed, so that every soul may be recompensed for what it has earned (لِتُجْزى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِما كَسَبَتْ). And that is none other than the Prophets and the Successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them), for it is through them, through following their laws (شرايعهم), tracing their footsteps (آثارهم), or abandoning them, and through proximity to their conduct (سيرتهم) or distance from it, that the measure of people and the worth of their good deeds (حسناتهم) and evil deeds (سيئاتهم) are known. Thus, the balance of every nation is the Prophet of that nation, the Successor (وصيّ) of its Prophet, and the Law (الشريعة) which He brought. فَمَنْ ثَقُلَتْ (Then as for those whose scales are heavy)—meaning, whose good deeds (حسناتهم) are heavy and numerous—فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ (they are the felicitous ones). وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ (But as for those whose scales are light)—meaning, whose good deeds are few—فَأُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (they are the ones who have ruined their souls) due to the injustice (بظلمهم) they committed against them, by denying the Prophets and the Successors (الأوصياء) or by not following them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq: He was asked concerning the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَنَضَعُ الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ لِيَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ (We shall set up the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection). He said: "They are the Prophets and the Successors (الأوصياء) (peace be upon them)."

And in another narration: "We are الْمَوازِينَ الْقِسْطَ (the scales of justice)." We have already verified the meaning of the Balance (الميزان) and the manner of weighing deeds, and we have reconciled the conflicting narrations (الأخبار المتعارضة) and sayings (الأقوال) regarding this in a manner that leaves nothing further to be desired, in our book entitled Mizan al-Qiyamah (ميزان القيامة). It is a good book, unmatched, I believe. May He grant success to whoever is worthy of studying and understanding it, if Allah wills.

(١٠) وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ ** (10) Certainly We established you in the land, and made in it livelihoods for you. Little do you thank. The Almighty's saying: وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (10) Certainly We established you in the land, مَكَّنَّاكُمْ (We established you) by enabling you to dwell in it, cultivate it, and dispose of its affairs, وَجَعَلْنا لَكُمْ فِيها مَعايِشَ تَعيشون بها (and made in it livelihoods for you, by which you live). قَلِيلاً ما تَشْكُرُونَ (Little do you thank) regarding what We created for you.

(١١) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْناكُمْ ثُمَّ صَوَّرْناكُمْ. (11) Certainly We created you, then We fashioned you. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for خَلَقْناكُمْ ** (We created you), it refers to the sperm-drop (نطفة), then the clinging mass (علقة), then the chewed lump (مضغة), then bone (عظماً), then flesh (لحماً). And as for صَوَّرْناكُمْ ** (We fashioned you), it refers to the eye, the nose, the ears, the mouth, the hands, and the feet—the forms of this and its like. Then He made the ugly (الدميم), the handsome (الوسيم), the large-bodied (الجسيم), the tall (الطويل), the short (القصير), and their likes.

I say: The restriction in the narration to explaining the creation and fashioning of the Children of Adam (بني آدم) does not negate the verse's inclusion of Adam, for He created him from clay (طيناً) unformed (غير مصوّر), and then He fashioned him. Thus, the narration does not contradict the remainder of the verse: ثُمَّ قُلْنا لِلْمَلائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ ** (then We said to the angels, 'Prostrate before Adam')—that is, after the creation and fashioning of Adam—فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ لَمْ يَكُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ ** (So they prostrated, but not Iblis; he was not among those who prostrated)—meaning, among those who prostrated to Adam.

(١٢) قالَ ما مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ ** (12) He said, 'What prevented you from prostrating meaning, from prostrating (أن تسجد). The particle لا (lā) is added in such instances to emphasize the meaning of the verb it precedes, similar to لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ ** (Lest he should know). And in this is an alert that the object of reproach is the abandonment of prostration. It is also said that the one prevented from something is compelled toward its opposite, so it is as if He said: "What compelled you toward **أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ قالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْهُ خَلَقْتَنِي مِنْ نارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُ مِنْ طِينٍ ** that you should not prostrate when I commanded you?' He said, 'I am better than him. You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.'

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Iblis measured (قاس) himself against Adam and said: You created me from fire, and You created him from clay. Had he measured the essence (الجوهر) from which Allah created Adam against the fire, that essence would have been more luminous (نوراً) and radiant (ضياءً) than the fire. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The angels used to assume that Iblis was one of them, but it was in Allah's knowledge that he was not. So, He brought forth the zealotry (الحمية) that was within him, and he said: You created me from fire, and You created him from clay.

In al-Kafi, al-Ihtijaj, and al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Abu Hanifa entered upon him, and he said to him: "O Abu Hanifa, I have been informed that you use analogical reasoning (تقيس)." He replied: "Yes, I use analogical reasoning (أقيس)." He said: "Do not use analogical reasoning (لا تقس), for the first one who used analogical reasoning (قاس) was Iblis when he said: You created me from fire, and You created him from clay. So, he measured (قاس) what was between fire and clay. Had he measured the luminosity (نورية) of Adam against the luminosity (نورية) of the fire, he would have known the superiority of what was between the two lights (النورين) and the purity (صفاء) of one over the other."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a long tradition: The first disobedience (معصية) that appeared was the egoism (الأنانية) of the accursed Iblis when Allah commanded His angels to prostrate to Adam, and they prostrated, but the accursed one refused to prostrate. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ما مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ (What prevented you from prostrating) (the rest of the Ayah). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, expelled him from His proximity (جواره), cursed him, and named him the outcast (رجيماً). And He swore by His Might that no one shall use analogical reasoning (يقيس) in his religion except that He will couple him with his enemy Iblis in the lowest level (أسفل درك) of the Fire.

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): Iblis lied; Allah did not create him except from clay. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ ناراً (Who has appointed for you fire from the green tree). Allah created him from that tree, and the origin of the tree is from clay.

(١٣) قالَ فَاهْبِطْ مِنْها ** (13) He said, 'Get down from it, (meaning) from the station (المنزلة) that you hold in the heaven and among the company (زمرة) of the angels. فَما يَكُونُ لَكَ (meaning) it is not proper (يصحّ) for you أَنْ تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيها ** and disobey, for it is the place of the humble (الخاشع) and the obedient (المطيع). It is said that in this is an alert (تنبيه) that arrogance (التكبر) is not fitting (لا يليق) for the inhabitants of Paradise (أهل الجنة), and that He, the Exalted, only expelled him and brought him down due to arrogance, not merely due to his disobedience. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever humbles himself before Allah, Allah raises him, and whoever is arrogant, Allah abases him." فَاخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ ** (13 cont.) 'So get out! You are indeed one of the abased.' (meaning) one of those whom Allah, the Exalted, has humiliated (أهانه) because of his arrogance.

(١٤) قالَ أَنْظِرْنِي إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ ** (14) He said, 'Respite me until the day they are resurrected.' Grant me respite until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), so do not cause me to die and do not hasten my punishment. (١٥) قالَ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنْظَرِينَ ** (15) He said, 'You are indeed among the reprieved.' Allah answered him regarding what he asked for concerning the respite (الإِمهال), but He did not answer him regarding what he asked for concerning its limit, because Allah says in another place: فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنْظَرِينَ إِلى يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ ** (Indeed, you are among the reprieved until the day of the known time.) And that is the first blast (النفخة الأولى), while the Day of Resurrection (يوم البعث والقيامة) is the second blast (النفخة الثانية). In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Iblis will die between the first and the second blast (النفخة الأولى والثانية). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He granted him respite until the day our Qā’im (قائمنا) is resurrected therein. The two narrations (الخبران) will come in Surat al-Hijr, if Allah the Almighty wills. And in His granting of his request is the testing of the servants (العباد) and exposing them to reward by opposing him.

(16) The Almighty's saying: قالَ فَبِما أَغْوَيْتَنِي ** (He said, 'Now that You have led me astray) meaning, because of Your leading me astray, which is His imposing upon him what caused him to fall into error (الغي). And he did not remain firm (يثبت) as the angels remained firm, for when Allah commanded him to prostrate, arrogance (الأنفة) carried him to disobey Him. لَأَقْعُدَنَّ لَهُمْ صِراطَكَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ ** (I will surely lie in wait for them on Your straight path.) I will surely strive hard in leading them astray until they become corrupt because of me, just as I became corrupt because of them, by lying in wait for them on the path of Islam, just as highway robbers (القطاع) lie in wait on the road to cut it off from passersby.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Path (الصراط) here is Ali (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): O Zurarah, he only aimed (عَمَدَ) for you and your companions, as for the others, He has finished with them.

And in a narration by al-Ayyashi from him (peace be upon him): He only aimed (صَمَدَ) (1).

(17) ثُمَّ لَآتِيَنَّهُمْ مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ وَعَنْ أَيْمانِهِمْ وَعَنْ شَمائِلِهِمْ ** (Then I will come at them from before them and from behind them, and from their right and from their left.) from all four directions collectively. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ثُمَّ لَآتِيَنَّهُمْ مِنْ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (Then I will come at them from before them) means that I will make the matter of the Hereafter trivial for them. وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ** (and from behind them) I will command them to amass wealth and to be stingy with it, withholding it from due rights (الحقوق), so that it remains for their heirs. وَعَنْ أَيْمانِهِمْ ** (and from their right) I will corrupt the matter of their religion for them by adorning misguidance (الضلالة) and beautifying doubt (الشبهة). وَعَنْ شَمائِلِهِمْ ** (and from their left) by making pleasures beloved to them and allowing desires (الشهوات) to overcome their hearts (2). And al-Qummi narrated something close to this with a simpler explanation. وَلا تَجِدُ أَكْثَرَهُمْ شاكِرِينَ ** (and You will not find most of them grateful.) (meaning) obedient. He said this as a conjecture (تظنّناً) based on His, the Glorified, saying: "And certainly Iblis found his conjecture to be true concerning them."

(١٨) قالَ اخْرُجْ مِنْها مَذْؤُماً ** (18) He said, 'Get out of it, disgraced, meaning (مذموماً), derived from (ذامه) when He censured him. مَدْحُوراً ** banished. meaning expelled (مطروداً). لَمَنْ تَبِعَكَ I will surely fill hell with you and all those who follow you مِنْهُمْ The (اللام) in it is for preparing the oath (القسم), and its answer is: لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنْكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** I will surely fill hell with you and all those who follow you meaning, from you and from them, so the second person plural pronoun (المخاطب) is used predominantly.

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding the Almighty's saying: فَاخْرُجْ مِنْها فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ لَعْنَتِي إِلى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ ** So get out of it, for you are an outcast, and My curse shall be on you till the Day of Retribution. Iblis said: "O Lord, how can this be, when You are the Just One Who does not commit injustice? Has the reward for my deed been nullified?" He said: "No, but ask Me for whatever you wish concerning the affairs of the world as a reward for your deed, and I shall grant it to you." The first thing he asked for was remaining until the Day of Retribution (يوم الدين). Allah said: "I have granted it to you." He said: "Grant me authority over the children of Adam." He said: "I have granted you authority." He said: "Make me flow through them like blood flows in the veins." He said: "I have made you flow." He said: "No child is born to them except that two are born to me; I see them, but they do not see me; and I take shape for them in any form I wish." He said: "I have granted it to you." He said: "O Lord, increase me." He said: "I have made homes (أوطاناً) for you and your progeny in their chests." He said: "O Lord, that is enough for me." Iblis then said: "By Your might, I will surely lead them astray," up to His saying: شاكِرِينَ ** grateful ones. [The narrator] said to him [Imam al-Sadiq]: "May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), by what means did Iblis merit from Allah that He granted him what He granted him?" He replied: "For something that was done by him for which Allah thanked Him." It was asked: "And what was it that was done by him, may I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك)?" He said: "Two units of prayer (ركعتين) which he performed in the heaven over four thousand years."

footnote (١) يصمد في الحوائج يقصد والصمد القصد يقال صمده يصمده صمداً.

footnote (٢) قيل المعنى من قبل دنياهم وآخرتهم ومن جهة حسناتهم وسيئاتهم **عن ابن عبّاس** وتلخيصه إنّي ازين لهم الدنيا وأخوفهم بالفقر وأقول لهم لا جنّة ولا نار ولا بعث ولا حساب واثبطهم عن الحسنات واشغلهم عنها واحبّب إليهم السّيئات وأحثهم عليها** قال ابن عبّاس** وانما لم يقل من فوقهم لأن فوقهم جهة نزول الرّحمة من السماء فلا سبيل له الى ذلك ولم يكن من تحت أرجلهم لأن الإتيان به يوحش انتهى وانما دخلت من في القدام والخلف وعن في اليمين والشمال لأن في القدّام والخلف معنى طلب النّهاية وفي اليمين والشمال الانحراف عن الجهة.

(١٩) وَيا آدَمُ ** (19) And O Adam وقلنا And We said: يا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ فَكُلا مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتُما وَلا تَقْرَبا هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (O Adam, dwell with your wife in paradise, and eat from wherever you wish, but do not approach this tree, lest you should be among the wrongdoers.) قد مضى تفسيرها في سورة البقرة. Its exegesis (تفسيرها) has already passed in Surah Al-Baqarah.

(٢٠) فَوَسْوَسَ لَهُمَا الشَّيْطانُ ** (So Satan tempted them) The difference between وسوس إليه and وسوس له is that the first means the origin of the hidden sound (الصوت الخفي). لِيُبْدِيَ لَهُما ** (so that he might expose to them) so that he might make manifest to them. ما وُورِيَ (١) (what was concealed from them) (1) covered. عَنْهُما مِنْ سَوْآتِهِما ** (of their shameful parts) their private parts (عوراتهما). It was said that they could not see them on themselves, nor could one of them see them on the other. وَقالَ ما نَهاكُما رَبُّكُما عَنْ هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةِ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونا ** (and he said, 'Your Lord has not forbidden you from this tree except lest you become) lest you become two angels (مَلَكَيْنِ) or that you become among the immortal ones (الْخالِدِينَ).

(٢١) وَقاسَمَهُما إِنِّي لَكُما لَمِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ. (And he swore to them both: 'I am indeed one of your well-wishers.') He swore to them both: إِنِّي لَكُما لَمِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ. (I am indeed one of your well-wishers.)

(٢٢) فَدَلَّاهُما ** So he guided them to eating from the tree. He (the author) drew attention by this to the fact that he (Iblis) caused them to descend thereby from a high degree (درجة عالية) to a low rank (رتبة سافلة), for al-tadliyah (التدلية) and al-idhlā' (الإِدلاء) is the sending down of a thing from above to below. بِغُرُورٍ ** by what he deluded them with through the oath, for they both thought that no one would swear by Allah falsely. فَلَمَّا ذاقَا الشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُما سَوْآتُهُما ** So when they found its taste, while beginning to eat from it, the punishment seized them, and their clothing fell away from them, and their private parts (عوراتهما) became manifest to them.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Their shameful parts (سوآتهما) were not manifest to them, so فَبَدَتْ (they became manifest), meaning they were internal (داخلة).

وَطَفِقا يَخْصِفانِ (١) And they began patching (2) and sticking leaf upon leaf عَلَيْهِما مِنْ وَرَقِ الْجَنَّةِ (themselves with the leaves of paradise), covering their shameful parts (سوآتهما) with them.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah settled him (Adam) in Paradise and permitted it to him, except for the tree, it was because Allah created a creation that does not endure except through command and prohibition (الأمر والنهي), sustenance (الغذاء), clothing (اللباس), shelter (الأكنان) (3), and marriage (التناكح). And he (Adam) does not perceive what benefits him from what harms him except through divine instruction (بالتوقيف). So Iblis came to him and said to him: "If you both eat from this tree which Allah forbade you from, you will become two angels (ملكين) and remain in Paradise forever. And if you do not eat from it, He will expel you from Paradise." And he swore to them both that he was a well-wisher (ناصح) for them. So Adam (peace be upon him) accepted his word, and they both ate from the tree. And the matter was as Allah recounted: بَدَتْ لَهُما سَوْآتُهُما (their shameful parts became manifest to them), and the clothing of Paradise which Allah had clothed them with fell away from them, and they began to cover themselves مِنْ وَرَقِ الْجَنَّةِ (with the leaves of paradise).

وَناداهُما رَبُّهُما أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُما عَنْ تِلْكُمَا الشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُلْ لَكُما إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ لَكُما عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ ** (So he misled them with delusion. So when they tasted the tree, their shameful parts became manifest to them, and they began to cover themselves with the leaves of paradise. And their Lord called out to them: 'Did I not forbid you from that tree, and tell you that Satan is your manifest enemy?') This is a reproach (عتاب) for violating the prohibition and a reprimand (توبيخ) for being deceived by the enemy's word.

(٢٣) قالا رَبَّنا ظَلَمْنا أَنْفُسَنا وَإِنْ لَمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنا وَتَرْحَمْنا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ. (23) They said, 'Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.' (٢٤) قالَ اهْبِطُوا بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ وَلَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتاعٌ إِلى حِينٍ ** (24) He said, 'Get down, being enemies of one another! For you there shall be a dwelling place on the earth and enjoyment for a while.' The interpretation of these verses, along with the complete story, has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

(1) Meaning, they affix some of it upon some [other part] so that they may cover their nakedness with it, derived from الخصف (al-khaṣf), which is the joining of one thing to another and affixing it to it (الإلصاق به). (2) رقع الثوب (He patched the garment) means he repaired it with patches (الرقاع). (3) الكن (al-kinn), with a kasrah (بالكسر), is the protection (وقا) and covering (ستره) of everything, like الكنة (al-kinnah) and الكنان (al-kinān), both with a kasrah (بكسرهما). And the house (البيت), its plural is أكنان (aknān) and اكنة (akinah).

(٢٥) قالَ فِيها تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيها تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْها تُخْرَجُونَ ** (25) He said, 'In it you shall live, and in it you shall die, and from it you shall be brought forth.' (This refers) to the recompense (الجزاء), for the recompense (للجزاء). And it was read with the fath of the tā' (بفتح التاءِ).

(٢٦) يا بَنِي آدَمَ ** (26) O Children of Adam! In al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them عليهما السلام: They said: It is general (عامة). قَدْ أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكُمْ لِباساً يُوارِي سَوْآتِكُمْ ** We have certainly sent down to you a garment to cover your private parts, and it suffices you from stitching (خصف) leaves (الورق). وَرِيشاً ** and rich adornment; with which you adorn yourselves. And al-rīsh (الريش) is that with which one adorns himself. It is borrowed (استعير) from the feathers (ريش) of the bird, because it is its garment (لباسه) and its adornment (زينته). وَلِباسُ (١) التَّقْوى ** but the garment of Godwariness (is) the fear (خشية) of Allah. ذلِكَ خَيْرٌ ** is the best. And libās (لباس) was read with the nasb (بالنّصب).

Al-Qummi said: The garment of Godwariness (لِباسُ التَّقْوى) is white clothes (ثياب البياض).

And from al-Baqir عليه السلام: As for the garment (اللباس), it is the clothes (الثياب) that you wear. And as for the rich adornment (الرياش), it is the possessions (المتاع) and wealth (المال). And as for the garment of Godwariness (لِباسُ التَّقْوى), it is chastity (العفاف). Indeed, the chaste person (العفيف) does not have his private parts (عورة) exposed, even if he is naked (عارياً) of clothes (الثياب). And the wicked person (الفاجر) has his private parts exposed (بادي العورة), even if he is clothed (كاسياً) in clothes (الثياب). ذلِكَ خَيْرٌ ** (That is best). He says: And chastity (العفاف) is best.

ذلِكَ ** That —meaning the sending down (انزال) of the garment (اللباس)— مِنْ آياتِ اللهِ ** is one of Allah's signs, which indicates His favor (فضله) and His mercy (رحمته). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ** that they may take admonition. so they recognize His blessing (نعمته), or they take heed (يتعظون) and thus abstain (فيتورعون) from foul deeds (القبائح).

(٢٧) يا بَنِي آدَمَ لا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ الشَّيْطانُ ** (27) O Children of Adam! Do not let Satan tempt you He should not test you by preventing you from entering Paradise through his misleading (إغواء). The meaning is a prohibition against following him and being tempted by him, كَما أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُمْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ يَنْزِعُ عَنْهُما لِباسَهُما لِيُرِيَهُما سَوْآتِهِما just as he expelled your parents from paradise, stripping them of their garments to expose their shameful parts to them. The stripping (النزع) was attributed to him due to his being the cause (لِلتَّسَبُّب). إِنَّهُ يَراكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ** Indeed He sees you—He and his tribe—whence you do not see them. This is a justification for the prohibition and an emphasis on the warning against his temptation (فتنة). وَقَبِيلُهُ ** means his soldiers (جنوده). And in the Hadith: "Verily, Satan flows through the son of Adam like blood flows through him." إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا الشَّياطِينَ أَوْلِياءَ لِلَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** Indeed We have made the devils friends of those who do not have faith. Due to the affinity (التناسب) that exists between them.

(٢٨) وَإِذا فَعَلُوا فاحِشَةً ** (28) And when they commit an indecency An act that is extreme in ugliness, such as idol worship, following an unjust leader (إمام الجور), or circumambulating the House (البيت) naked. قالُوا وَجَدْنا عَلَيْها آباءَنا وَاللهُ أَمَرَنا بِها قُلْ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَأْمُرُ بِالْفَحْشاءِ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ. (28) they say, 'We found our fathers following it, and Allah has enjoined it upon us.' Say, 'Indeed Allah does not enjoin indecency. Do you attribute to Allah what you do not know?' Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] those who worshipped idols, and Allah refuted them. In al-Kafi, narrated muḍmaran (مضمراً), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from a righteous servant (عبد صالح), he said: "Have you seen anyone who claimed that Allah commanded us to commit fornication (الزنا), drink wine (شرب الخمر), or [commit] any of these forbidden acts (المحارم)?" It was said: "No." He said: "What is this indecency (الفاحشة) which they claim Allah commanded them to commit?" It was said: "Allah knows best, and His guardian (وليه)." He said: "This is concerning the unjust leaders (أئمّة الجور). They claimed that Allah commanded them to follow a people whom Allah did not command them to follow. So, Allah refuted that against them and informed [us] that they had spoken lies against Him, and He named that act of theirs an indecency (فاحشة)." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever claims that Allah commands indecency (الفحشاء) has lied against Allah. And whoever claims that good and evil are attributed to Him has lied against Allah."

footnote (١) قوله **وَلِباسُ التَّقْوى ** (1) His saying: And the garment of piety [It means] the fear of Allah (خشية الله). And it is said: good demeanor (السّمت الحسن). And it is said: the garment of war (لباس الحرب). Its nominative case (رفعه) is due to it being the subject (الابتداء), and its predicate (خبره) is ذلك خيراً or خير, and ذلك is its description (صفته). It is as if it were said: And the garment of piety referred to is better (خير). Nafi', Ibn 'Amir, and Al-Kisa'i read وَلِباسَ (with naṣb - accusative) as an ‘aṭf (conjunction) upon لِباساً.

(٢٩) قُلْ أَمَرَ رَبِّي بِالْقِسْطِ ** (29) Say, 'My Lord has enjoined justice, (meaning) with justice (العدل) and uprightness (الاستقامة). وَأَقِيمُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ ** (and set your faces upright) (meaning) turn towards His worship, being upright, not deviating to anything else; or, set them (your faces) towards the Qiblah عِنْدَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ ** (at every place of worship), (meaning) at every time of prostration (سجود), or at every place of prostration (سجود), which is the prayer (الصلوة).

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This (verse) concerns the Qiblah. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): (The phrase) "places of worship (مساجد)" refers to those that were newly established (محدثة), so they were commanded to set their faces towards the direction (شطر) of the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام).

And al-Ayyashi narrated similar to the two narrations, and added to the first one: "There is no worship of idols (عبادة الأوثان) in it, purely (خالصاً) and sincerely (مخلِصاً)." And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding: عِنْدَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ ** (at every place of worship), meaning the Imams (الأئمّة). وَادْعُوهُ ** (and supplicate Him), (meaning) and worship Him, مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (dedicating your worship to Him). (meaning) the obedience (الطاعة), for indeed your return (مصيركم) is to Him. كَما بَدَأَكُمْ ** (Just as He originated you), (meaning) just as He created you initially (ابتداءً), **تَعُودُونَ ** (you will return). (meaning) by His bringing you back, and He will recompense you for your deeds.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: He created them, when He created them, as believers (مؤمناً) and disbelievers (كافراً), wretched (شقيّاً) and felicitous (سعيداً). Likewise, they will return on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) as guided (مهتد) and astray (ضالّ).

(٣٠) فَرِيقاً هَدى (He guided a group) by granting them success (وَفَّقَهُمْ) for faith (للإيمان). وَفَرِيقاً حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الضَّلالَةُ (and another group deserved to be misguided) meaning abandonment (الخذلان), since they did not accept the guidance (الهدى), so they went astray (فَضَلُّوا). إِنَّهُمُ اتَّخَذُوا الشَّياطِينَ أَوْلِياءَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (Indeed, they have taken the devils as patrons instead of Allah) —they obeyed them in what they commanded them with— وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ. (and they suppose that they are guided.)

Al-Qummi (says): And it is as if this is the completion of the previous narration. They are the Qadariyyah (القدرية) who say there is no pre-decree (لا قدر) and claim that they are capable of guidance (الهدى) and misguidance (الضلال), and that this belongs to them: if they wish, they are guided, and if they wish, they go astray. They are the Magians (مجوس) of this Ummah (الأمّة). The enemies of Allah have lied; the Will (المشيئة) and the Power (القدرة) belong to Allah. As He originated them, so shall they return. Whoever He created wretched (شقيّاً) on the day He created him, thus shall he return to Him; and whoever He created felicitous (سعيداً) on the day He created him, thus shall he return to Him felicitous. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The wretched (الشقيّ) is he who is wretched in his mother's womb, and the felicitous (السّعيد) is he who is felicitous in his mother's womb."

In al-Ilal, narrated from him عليه السلام regarding: إِنَّهُمُ اتَّخَذُوا الشَّياطِينَ أَوْلِياءَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (Indeed, they have taken the devils as patrons instead of Allah)—He means leaders (أئمّة) other than the Imams (أئمّة) of the Truth (الحقّ).

(٣١) يا بَنِي آدَمَ خُذُوا زِينَتَكُمْ عِنْدَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ ** (O Children of Adam! Take your adornment to every mosque) Al-Qummi said: Regarding the two Eids and the Friday prayer (الجمعة), one should perform ghusl (يغتسل) and wear white clothes (ثياباً بيضاً). And it is also narrated: Combing (المشط) before every prayer (صلوة). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means during the two Eids and the Friday prayer (الجمعة). And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That is, خُذُوا ** your clothes (ثيابكم) with which you adorn yourselves for the prayer (الصّلوة) on Fridays (الجمعات) and Eids (الأعياد). And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It refers to the clothes (الثياب). And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the outer garments (الأردية) during the two Eids and the Friday prayer (الجمعة). And in al-Jawami' and al-Ayyashi: Al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them both), when he stood up for prayer (الصلوة), would wear his finest clothes. When he was asked about this, he said: "Indeed, Allah is beautiful and loves beauty, so I adorn myself for my Lord," and he recited the verse (الْآيَةَ). And in al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Among that is combing (التمشّط) before every prayer (صلوة). And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it. And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse (الآية): "Comb your hair, for combing (التمشّط) brings sustenance (الرزق), beautifies the hair, fulfills the need in the seminal fluid (ماءِ الصّلب), and cuts phlegm (البلغم)." And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would comb beneath his beard forty times and pass over it seven times, and he would say: "Indeed, it increases sustenance (الرّزق) and cuts phlegm (البلغم)." And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse (الآية): He said: Ghusl (الغسل) when meeting every Imam (إمام). And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): It means the Imams (peace be upon them). And it was said: It is an command to wear clothes (الثياب) during the prayer (الصّلوة) and the circumambulation (الطّواف), as they used to circumambulate naked, saying: "We do not worship in clothes (ثياب) in which we have sinned." Al-Qummi (narrated) that some people used to circumambulate the House (البيت) naked—the men by day and the women by night—so Allah commanded them to wear clothes (الثياب). And they used not to eat except a meager sustenance (قوتاً), so Allah commanded them to eat and drink and not to be extravagant (يسرفوا). I say: Meaning, during the days of their Hajj (حجهم), they magnify their Hajj thereby. وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا ** what is good for you وَلا تُسْرِفُوا ** through excess (الإفراط), waste (الإتلاف), transgression toward the unlawful (الحرام), declaring the lawful (الحلال) unlawful, and other such things. It was said (1): Allah has indeed gathered medicine (الطبّ) in half a verse, for He said: كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا وَلا تُسْرِفُوا . This refers to excess (الإفراط) in eating, which is condemned in many narrations. إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ ** He is not pleased with their action. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Do you think that Allah gave to those He gave out of His honor for them, and withheld from those He withheld out of His contempt for them? No. Rather, wealth (المال) is the wealth of Allah; He places it with a man as a trust (ودايع). He permitted them to eat moderately (قصداً), drink moderately, dress moderately, marry moderately, and ride moderately, and to return what is beyond that to the poor among the believers (فقراءِ المؤمنين) and thereby mend their disunity (شعثهم). Whoever does that, what he eats is lawful (حلالاً), what he drinks is lawful, what he rides is lawful, and what he marries is lawful. But whoever transgresses beyond that, it is unlawful (حراماً) for him." Then he recited: وَلا تُسْرِفُوا إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ (and do not be extravagant; indeed, He does not like the extravagant). "Do you think Allah entrusted a man with wealth, granting him (2) the ability to buy a horse for ten thousand dirhams (درهم) when a horse worth twenty dirhams would suffice him, or to buy a slave-girl for a thousand dinars (دينار) when one worth twenty dinars would suffice him?" And He said: لا تُسْرِفُوا إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ (and do not be extravagant; indeed, He does not like the extravagant). And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever asks people for something while he possesses what sustains him for his day, he is one of الْمُسْرِفِينَ.** (the extravagant)."

(٣٢) قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللهِ ** (Say, 'Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah) such as clothing and all other things by which one beautifies oneself, الَّتِي أَخْرَجَ لِعِبادِهِ ** (which He has brought forth for His servants,') from the earth, like cotton, linen, silk (الأبريسم), wool, and jewels, **وَالطَّيِّباتِ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ ** (and the good things of the provision?') meaning the delightful things among foods and drinks. And this is a denial of the prohibition of these things.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) sent Abdullah ibn al-Abbas to Ibn al-Kawwa and his companions, and he (Ibn al-Abbas) was wearing a thin shirt and a fine garment (حُلّة). When they looked at him, they said: "O Ibn Abbas, you are the best among us in our estimation, yet you wear this clothing?" So he said: "This is the first thing I will dispute with you about: قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللهِ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَ لِعِبادِهِ وَالطَّيِّباتِ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ (Say, 'Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good things of the provision?')" And Allah said: خُذُوا زِينَتَكُمْ عِنْدَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ. (Take your adornment at every mosque.)

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): that which is similar in meaning.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Sufyan al-Thawri saw him, and he (the Imam) was wearing beautiful, expensive clothes. So he (Sufyan) said: "By Allah, I shall go to him and rebuke him." So he approached him and said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not wear clothing like this, nor did Ali عليه السلام, nor any of your forefathers." He (the Imam) said to him: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in a time of scarcity (قَتِرٍ مُقْتِرٍ) (1), and He took according to His scarcity and poverty. But the world after that let down its buckets (أرخت عزاليها) (2), and the most deserving of its people are its righteous ones." Then He recited: قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللهِ —the Ayah. "So we are the most deserving of taking from it what Allah has given. However, O Thawri, the garment you see upon me, I only wear it for the people." Then He pulled Sufyan's hand and drew him toward Him, then He lifted the outer garment and brought out a thick garment underneath it, next to His skin. He said: "This I wear for myself, and what you saw is for the people." Then He pulled a garment on Sufyan, the outer layer of which was thick and coarse, and inside that garment was a soft garment. He (the Imam) said: "You wore this outer one for the people, and you wore this (inner one) for yourself, to please it."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was leaning upon one of His companions when Abbad ibn Kathir met Him. He (the Imam) was wearing beautiful silk (مروية) garments. Abbad said: "O Aba Abdillah, you are from the Household of Prophethood, and your father (1) was such-and-such, so why are these silk garments upon you? If only you wore less than these silk garments upon you." He (the Imam) said to him: "Woe unto you, O Abbad! مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللهِ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَ لِعِبادِهِ وَالطَّيِّباتِ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ ** (Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good things of the provision?) Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, when He bestows a blessing upon a servant, loves to see its effect upon him; there is no harm in it. Woe unto you, O Abbad! I am but a piece (بضعة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so do not harm me." And Abbad used to wear two garments of cotton (قطن).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "May Allah set your affairs right. You mentioned that Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) used to wear coarse clothing, wearing a shirt for four dirhams and the like, yet we see fine clothing upon you." He (the Imam) said to him: "Indeed, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) wore that in a time when it was not disapproved of. But if he were to wear the like of that today, he would be singled out (لَشُهِرَ بِهِ). So the best clothing for every time is the clothing of its people, except that when our Qaim (قائمنا) rises, he will wear the clothing of Ali and follow his conduct."

I say: And in another narration from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He explained the coarseness of His food and clothing by saying that Allah has obligated the Imams of Justice to measure themselves by the weak among the people, so that the poverty of the poor does not overwhelm (يَتَبَيَّغ) him (2). قُلْ هِيَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا (Say, 'These are for those who have faith in the life of the world,') primarily (بالأصالة), and the participation of the disbelievers in them is secondary (فَتَبَعٌ). خالِصَةً يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (exclusively on the Day of Resurrection.) Others will not share in them with them. And it was read with the rafa' (nominative case).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): After mentioning the rivers of the earth, He said: "Whatever they irrigate and draw water from belongs to us, and whatever belongs to us belongs to our Shi'a (شيعتنا). And there is nothing of it for our enemy, except what he has usurped. And indeed, our Wali (وليّنا) is in the most expansive state between this and this," meaning between the heaven and the earth. Then He recited this Ayah: قُلْ هِيَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا (Say, 'These are for those who have faith in the life of the world,')—those who have been usurped of it—خالِصَةً (exclusively) for them **يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (on the Day of Resurrection) without usurpation.

And in al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And know, O servants of Allah, that the pious (المتقين) have attained the immediate good and the deferred good. They shared with the people of the world in their world, but the people of the world will not share with them in their Hereafter. Allah permitted for them in the world that which sufficed them and enriched them. Allah said: قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللهِ —the Ayah. They inhabited the world with the best manner in which it is inhabited, and they ate from it with the best manner in which it is eaten. They shared with the people of the world in their world, so they ate with them from the good things they eat, and drank from the good things they drink, and wore the best of what they wear, and inhabited the best of what they inhabit, and married the best of whom they marry, and rode the best of what they ride. And they attained the pleasure of the world along with the people of the world. And tomorrow, they will be the neighbors of Allah, making wishes to Him, and He will grant them what they wish. No supplication of theirs will be rejected, nor will their share of pleasure be diminished. So, O servants of Allah, this is what the one who possesses intellect yearns for." كَذلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (Thus do We elaborate the signs for a people who know.) meaning: Just as We elaborate this ruling, We elaborate the rest of the rulings for them.

footnote (١) (*قَتَرَ*) (*قَتْراً*) and (*قُتُوراً*) from the categories (*بابي*) of (*ضَرَبَ*) and (*قَعَدَ*): He restricted him in expenditure. From this is (*قَتَرَ*) upon his dependents, meaning he restricted them. And (*أَقْتَرَ*) (*إِقْتَاراً*) and (*قَتَّرَ*) (*تَقْتِيراً*) are similar to it.

footnote (٢) In the *Hadith*: "The sky sent forth its *azali* (*عزاليها*)," meaning its mouths (*أفواهها*). And *al-azali* (*العزالي*), with the *fathah* or *kasrah* on the *lam*, is the plural of *al-azla'* (*العزلاء*), like *al-hamra'* (*الحمراء*), which is the mouth of the waterskin (*المزادة*). So His saying, "The sky sent forth its *azali* (*عزاليها*)," means the severity of the rainfall, likened to its descent from the mouths of the waterskin. And similar to this is: "And the world after that let down its buckets (*أرخت عزاليها*)."

footnote (١) "And your father was such-and-such" (*وكان أبوك وكان*) means he was an ascetic (*زاهداً*), and he used to wear coarse clothing, and he was one who abandoned the blessings of the world. He means by his father the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And in some manuscripts, (*قَطْوَتَيْنِ*) appears instead of (*قُطْنٍ*) at the end of the *hadith*, and it is, with the undotted letters (*بالمهملة*), a type of striped cloak (*البرود*) made from it.

footnote (٢) In the *Hadith*: "Indeed, Allah has obligated the Imams of Justice..." (*الخ*) meaning: it is stirred up by it. (*تَبَيَّغَ*) upon him: the matter became confused, and the blood surged and overcame, and the milk became abundant.

قُلْ إِنَّما حَرَّمَ رَبِّيَ الْفَواحِشَ ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَما بَطَنَ وَالْإِثْمَ وَالْبَغْيَ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَأَنْ تُشْرِكُوا بِاللهِ ما لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطاناً وَأَنْ تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ. (33) Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, those that are manifest and those that are hidden, and sin and aggression without right, and that you associate with Allah that for which He has not sent down any authority, and that you attribute to Allah what you do not know.'

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): As for His saying: ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها ** (those that are manifest), it means open fornication (الزّنا المعلن) and the raising of banners which the immoral, indecent women (الفواجر الفواحش) used to raise in the Jahiliyyah (Age of Ignorance). And as for His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَما بَطَنَ ** (and those that are hidden), it means marrying the wives of the fathers (أزواج الآباءِ), because people, before the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his family) was sent, if a man had a wife and died, his son would marry her after him, provided she was not his mother. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, forbade that. And as for الْإِثْمَ ** (and sin), it is wine (الخمر) itself. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said in another place: يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِما إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ (They ask you concerning wine and gambling. Say, 'There is a great sin in them, and some benefits for the people'). As for al-Ithm (الإثم) in the Book of Allah, it is wine (الخمر) and gambling (الميسر), and the sin of both is great.

And al-Ayyashi added: After His saying وَالْمَيْسِرِ ** (and gambling), finally, it is al-Nard (النّرد) (backgammon). He said: "And the sin of both is great." And as for His saying: وَالْبَغْيَ ** (and aggression), it is secret fornication (الزّنا سرّاً).

I say: And perhaps الْفَواحِشَ ** (indecencies) is generalized to everything whose ugliness is excessive, whether manifest or hidden. And الْإِثْمَ ** (sin) is generalized to every sin. And الْبَغْيَ ** (aggression) is interpreted as injustice (الظلم) and arrogance (الكبر). And بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ (without right) is considered an emphasis (تأكيداً). And ما لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطاناً ** (that for which He has not sent down any authority) is a form of mockery (تهكّماً), since it is not permissible for Him to send down a proof (برهاناً) that others should be associated with Him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Quran has an apparent meaning (ظهر) and an inner meaning (بطن). All that Allah has forbidden in the Quran is the apparent meaning, and the inner meaning of that is the Imams of injustice (أئمّة الجور). And all that Allah has permitted in the Book is the apparent meaning, and the inner meaning of that is the Imams of truth (أئمّة الحقّ). وَأَنْ تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** (and that you attribute to Allah what you do not know) means that you fabricate and lie (تتقّولوا وتفتروا).

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Beware of two characteristics in which those who perished, perished: beware of giving people religious rulings (تفتي الناس) based on your opinion (برأيك) and practicing a religion (تدين) based on what you do not know. And in another narration: that you worship Allah (تدين الله) with falsehood (بالباطل) and give people religious rulings based on what you do not know.

In it (al-Khisal) and in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked what the proof (حجّة) of Allah is against the servants. He replied: "That they say what they know and stop short of what they do not know."

In al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in his testament to his son Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah: "O my son, do not say what you do not know, rather, do not say everything that you know."

In al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his family): "Whoever gives religious rulings (أفتى) to people without knowledge, the angels of the heavens and the earth curse him."

(٣٤) وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ** (34) There is a term for every nation. A duration or a time for the descent of death or punishment. فَإِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ ** when their term comes, Their duration has expired or their time has arrived. لا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ ** they shall not defer it by a single hour nor advance it. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) said: It is that which is named for the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) on the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) (1). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) said: The years are counted, then the months are counted, then the days are counted, then the breath (النَّفس) is counted. فَإِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ.** (when their term comes, they shall not defer it by a single hour nor advance it.)

**(٣٥) يا بَنِي آدَمَ إِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ ** (35) O Children of Adam! Should there come to you

The mā (ما) is joined to the conditional in (إن) for emphasis of the meaning of the condition. رُسُلٌ مِنْكُمْ from your kind يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِي فَمَنِ اتَّقى ** the denial (التكذيب) from you وَأَصْلَحَ his deed (عمله) فَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ.**

(who recount My signs to you, then whoever is Godwary, and sets right, there shall be no fear come upon them, nor shall they grieve.)

**وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا عَنْها أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ (٣٦) ** (36) But those who deny Our signs and are disdainful of them—they shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it forever.

It was said that the insertion of the fā’ (الفاءِ) in the first recompense, without the second, is for the sake of emphasis in the promise (الوعد) and leniency in the threat (الوعيد).

(٣٧) فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ ** more heinous in wrongdoing (أشنع ظلماً) مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآياتِهِ ** meaning: [by] saying about Him what He did not say, or denying what He did say. (37) So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah or denies His signs? أُولئِكَ يَنالُهُمْ نَصِيبُهُمْ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (Those—their share of the Book shall reach them) refers to what was written for them of provisions (الأرزاق) and appointed times (الآجال). And according to al-Qummi, meaning: يَنالُهُمْ ** (shall reach them) what is in Our Book concerning the punishments for acts of disobedience (عقوبات المعاصي). حَتَّى إِذا جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنا يَتَوَفَّوْنَهُمْ ** (until, when Our apostles come to them to take away their souls). The word حَتَّى (until) is the limit (غاية) for their receiving their share and fulfilling it completely, meaning: until the time of their death, which is the point after which the speech begins. The intended meaning of the apostles (الرسل) here is the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) and his assistants (أعوانه). قالُوا ** (they will say)—meaning the apostles (الرسل)—أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (Where are those whom you used to invoke besides Allah?) meaning: the deities (الآلهة) whom you used to worship. قالُوا ضَلُّوا عَنَّا ** (They will say, 'They have failed us,') meaning: they have disappeared from us (غابوا عنا). وَشَهِدُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كانُوا كافِرِينَ (and they will bear witness against themselves that they were faithless.) They confessed that they were not upon anything (لم يكونوا على شيءٍ) regarding what they had been practicing.

(٣٨) قالَ meaning Allah Almighty said to them: ادْخُلُوا فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ existing within the company of nations, accompanying them, مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ meaning the disbelievers (كفّار) of the past nations from both types. فِي النَّارِ (in the Fire) relates to ادْخُلُوا ** (Enter). كُلَّما دَخَلَتْ أُمَّةٌ into the Fire لَعَنَتْ أُخْتَها which went astray by following it, حَتَّى إِذَا ادَّارَكُوا (١) فِيها جَمِيعاً meaning they caught up (تداركوا) or followed one another (تلاحقوا) in the Fire.

**قالَ ادْخُلُوا فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ فِي النَّارِ كُلَّما دَخَلَتْ أُمَّةٌ لَعَنَتْ أُخْتَها حَتَّى إِذَا ادَّارَكُوا فِيها جَمِيعاً ** (38) He said, 'Enter the Fire among the nations of jinn and humans that have passed away before you.' Whenever a nation enters, it curses its sister nation, until, when they all overtake one another in it,

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Some of them disown others, and some of them curse others. Some of them wish to argue against (يحجّ) others, hoping for victory (رجاء الفلج) (٢), so that they may escape the magnitude of what has descended upon them. But it is not a time for trial (بلوى), nor testing (اختبار), nor acceptance of an excuse (قبول معذرة), nor is it a time for salvation (نجاة).

قالَتْ أُخْراهُمْ in rank (منزلة), and they are the followers (الأتباع) and the low-ranking (السّفلة), لِأُولاهُمْ in rank (منزلة), meaning concerning them, since the address (الخطاب) is with Allah, not with them, and they are the leaders (القادة) and chiefs (الرؤساء).

**قالَتْ أُخْراهُمْ لِأُولاهُمْ ** the last of them will say to the first of them,

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means the imams of tyranny (أئمة الجور). رَبَّنا هؤُلاءِ أَضَلُّونا they called us to error (الضّلال) and compelled us to it, فَآتِهِمْ عَذاباً ضِعْفاً مِنَ النَّارِ multiplied (مضاعفاً) because they went astray and led others astray (أضلّوا). قالَ لِكُلٍّ ضِعْفٌ ** (He will say, 'For each is double') — As for the leaders (القادة), it is for their disbelief (كفرهم) and their leading others astray (تضليلهم). And as for the followers (الأتباع), it is for their disbelief (كفرهم) and their imitation (تقليدهم). وَلكِنْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ what is decreed for each. And it was recited (وقرء) with the ya' (بالياء) indicating separation (على الانفصال).

**رَبَّنا هؤُلاءِ أَضَلُّونا فَآتِهِمْ عَذاباً ضِعْفاً مِنَ النَّارِ قالَ لِكُلٍّ ضِعْفٌ وَلكِنْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** 'Our Lord! These ones led us astray, so give them a double punishment of the Fire.' He will say, 'For each is double, but you do not know.'

(١) Some of them overtook others (أدرك بعضهم بعضاً), meaning they disputed with Him (خاصمه) and argued with Him (جادله).

(٢) Hoping for al-Falaj (رجاء الفلج), with a harakah (محرّكة), meaning victory (الفوز) and escape (التخلص) from the punishment. Fayafiltu (فيفلتوا) means their minds suddenly fly away (يطر عقلهم بغتة), and they slip (يزلّون), and their planning (تدبيرهم) is stripped away, so they find no way (سبيلاً).

(39) وَقالَتْ أُولاهُمْ لِأُخْراهُمْ ** (39) And the former of them will say to the latter of them Addressing them, فَما كانَ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنا مِنْ فَضْلٍ ** 'You have no superiority over us, They linked their speech to the saying of Allah, glory be to Him, concerning the followers: لِكُلٍّ ضِعْفٌ (for each there is double). Meaning: it has been established that you have no superiority over us, and we and you are equal in misguidance and in deserving the double [punishment] (الضِعْف). فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ ** so taste the punishment because of what you used to earn.' Al-Qummi said: [This is said] gloating (شماتة) over them.

(40) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا عَنْها ** (Indeed, those who deny Our signs and are disdainful of them) meaning: concerning belief in them. لا تُفَتَّحُ لَهُمْ أَبْوابُ السَّماءِ ** (the gates of the sky will not be opened for them) for their supplications and deeds, nor for the descent of blessing upon them, nor for the ascent of their souls when they die.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for the believers, their deeds and their souls are raised to the heaven, and its gates are opened for them. But as for the disbeliever, he ascends with his deed and his soul until, when he reaches the heaven, a caller cries out: "Descend to Sijjin," which is a valley in Hadhramawt (1) called Barhut (2). **وَلا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِياطِ ** (nor will they enter paradise until the camel passes through the eye of the needle.) meaning: they will not enter Paradise until that which never happens occurs: the passing of the camel—which does not pass except through a wide gate—through the eye of the needle. وَكَذلِكَ (And thus), like that terrible recompense, نَجْزِي الْمُجْرِمِينَ (We recompense the guilty).

(٤١) لَهُمْ مِنْ جَهَنَّمَ مِهادٌ وَمِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ غَواشٍ وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ. (41) They shall have a bedding of hellfire, and coverings above them, and thus do We requite the wrongdoers. مِهادٌ ** means a mattress (فراش). وَمِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ غَواشٍ ** means coverings (أغطية). وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ. (٤٢) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ. (42) But those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We task no soul except according to its capacity—they shall be the inhabitants of paradise, and they will remain in it forever. لا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها ** is an interjection (اعتراض) between the subject (المبتدأ) and the predicate (الخبر), intended to encourage the acquisition of the everlasting bliss (النعيم المقيم) with what their capacity encompasses and what is easy for them. أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ.**

footnote (١) حضر موت بضم الميم بلد وقبيلة ويقال هذا حضر موت ويضاف فيقال حضرُ مُوت بضم الراء وان شئت لا تنوّن الثاني والتصغير حضير موت. (1) Hadhramawt (حضر موت) with the dammah on the mīm (بضم الميم) is a country (بلد) and a tribe (قبيلة). It is said: hādhā Hadhramawt (هذا حضر موت). It can also be used in the construct state (يضاف), so it is said: Hadhru Mawt (حضرُ مُوت) with the dammah on the rāʾ (بضم الراء). If you wish, you do not apply tanwīn (لا تنوّن) to the second part. The diminutive form (التصغير) is Hudhayr Mawt (حضير موت). footnote (٢) برهوت محرّكة وبالضمّ بئرا وواد أو بلد. (2) Barhūt (برهوت) with vocalization (محرّكة) and with the dammah (وبالضمّ) is a well (بئرا), a valley (واد), or a country (بلد).

(٤٣) وَنَزَعْنا ما فِي صُدُورِهِمْ مِنْ غِلٍ ** (43) And We shall remove whatever rancour is in their breasts [The rancor was] against their brothers in the world (الدنيا). Thus, their hearts became sound and purified from malice (الحقد), envy (الحسد), and enmity (الشحناء). Nothing remained among them except mutual affection (التعاطف), mercy (التراحم), and love (التوادّ). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Enmity (العداوة) is removed from them, meaning from the believers in Paradise. تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهارُ وَقالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي هَدانا لِهذا وَما كُنَّا لِنَهْتَدِيَ لَوْ لا أَنْ هَدانَا اللهُ ** (43) with rivers running in their vicinity. They will say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who guided us to this. Had Allah not guided us, we would have never been guided. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and the Imams (peace be upon them) will be called, and they will be set up for the people. When their followers (شيعتهم) see them, قالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي هَدانا لِهذا ** (They will say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who guided us to this'). The Ayah refers to the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful and the Imams (peace be upon them) from his progeny. لَقَدْ جاءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنا بِالْحَقِ ** (44) The apostles of our Lord certainly brought the truth.' So we were guided by their guidance (إرشادهم). They say that out of joy (اغتباطاً) and exultation (تبجّحاً) (١), since the knowledge of certainty (علم يقينهم) they held in the world (الدنيا) has become the absolute certainty (عين يقينهم) for them in the Hereafter (الآخرة). وَنُودُوا أَنْ تِلْكُمُ الْجَنَّةُ ** (44) And they will be called out: 'This is paradise, [This call is made] when they see it. أُورِثْتُمُوها بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. which you have inherited because of what you used to do.' In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: There is no one except that he has a dwelling in Paradise and a dwelling in the Fire. As for the disbeliever (الكافر), he inherits the believer's dwelling in the Fire, and the believer inherits the disbeliever's dwelling in Paradise. That is the meaning of His saying: أُورِثْتُمُوها بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ.** (which you have inherited because of what you used to do.)

وَنادى أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ أَصْحابَ النَّارِ أَنْ قَدْ وَجَدْنا ما وَعَدَنا رَبُّنا حَقًّا فَهَلْ وَجَدْتُمْ ما وَعَدَ رَبُّكُمْ حَقًّا ** (44) The inhabitants of paradise will call out to the inhabitants of the Fire, [saying,] 'We have found what our Lord promised us to be true. Have you [too] found what your Lord promised you to be true?' They said this rejoicing in their state, gloating over the inhabitants of the Fire, and causing them regret. And He only did not say "what He promised you" (referring to the inhabitants of the Fire) just as He said ما وَعَدَنا ** (what He promised us), because what distressed them of the promised things was not entirely specific to His promise to them, such as the Resurrection (البعث), the Reckoning (الحساب), and the bliss of Paradise for its inhabitants.

قالُوا نَعَمْ فَأَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌ بَيْنَهُمْ أَنْ ** They will say, 'Yes.' And it was read (قُرِئَ) with anna (أَنَّ) with tashdid (doubling the nūn): لَعْنَةُ اللهِ عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ. (Then an announcer will announce among them: 'The curse of Allah is on the wrongdoers.')

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Kazim, and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon them both): The announcer (المؤذّن) is the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين). And al-Qummi added: He announces an announcement (أذاناً) that all creatures (الخلائق) hear. And in al-Majma' and al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "I am that announcer (المؤذّن)."

footnote (١) *البَجَح* (*al-Bajaḥ*), with the *bāʾ* preceding the *jīm* then the *ḥāʾ*, vocalized, means joy (*الفرح*).

(٤٥) الَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَيَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً ** deviation (زيغاً) and turning away (وميلاً) from what it (the way of Allah) is upon وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ كافِرُونَ. (45) those who bar [others] from the way of Allah, and seek to make it crooked, and who are faithless to the Hereafter. (٤٦) وَبَيْنَهُما حِجابٌ ** meaning, between the two groups (as indicated by His saying: فَضُرِبَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِسُورٍ ** (So a wall will be set up between them)) or between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار) to prevent one from reaching the other. وَعَلَى الْأَعْرافِ ** the heights of the veil (اعراف الحجاب), meaning its highest parts, رِجالٌ ** men from the monotheists (الموحّدين), the knowing (العارفين), the well-known (المعروفين). يَعْرِفُونَ كُلًّا ** from the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار) بِسِيماهُمْ ** by their mark (علامتهم) which Allah has informed them of, because they are among the discerning (المتوسّمين), the people of insight (أهل الفراسة). (46) Between the two there will be a veil, and on the A‘rāf will be men who recognize each of them by their marks.

In al-Majma' and al-Jawami', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "We shall be stationed on the Day of Resurrection between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النار). Whoever helps us, we shall recognize him by his mark (بسيماه) and admit him into Paradise (الجنّة). And whoever hates us, we shall recognize him by his mark (بسيماه) and admit him into the Fire (النّار)."

And in both of them (i.e., al-Majma' and al-Jawami') and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْأَعْرافِ ** are sand dunes (كُثبان) (1) between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار), and the men (الرجال) are the Imams (الأئمّة). The rest of the narration will follow.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "We are عَلَى الْأَعْرافِ ** (on the A‘rāf). We recognize our supporters بِسِيماهُمْ ** (by their marks). And we are الْأَعْرافِ ** (the A‘rāf), those through the path of whose knowledge Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is not known except. And we are الْأَعْرافِ . Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will station us on the Day of Resurrection upon the Bridge (الصراط). So, none shall enter Paradise (الجنة) except those who recognized us and whom we recognized, and none shall enter the Fire (النار) except those who denied us and whom we denied."

And similar to it is found in al-Basair and al-Ihtijaj, except that it states: "We shall be stationed on the Day of Resurrection between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار). So, none shall enter Paradise (الجنّة)..." (the rest of the narration). And it added at the end: "That is because if Allah Almighty had willed, He would have made Himself known to the people so that they would know His limit and approach Him through His gate. But He made us His gates (أبوابه), His Bridge (صراطه), His path (سبيله), and the gate (بابه) through which He is approached."

And al-Ayyashi narrated what is close to it. And narrated from Salman, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم say to Ali (peace be upon him) more than ten times: "O Ali, verily you and the Successors (الأوصياء) after you are the A‘rāf between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار). None shall enter Paradise (الجنّة) except those who recognized you and whom you recognized, and none shall enter the Fire (النار) except those who denied you and whom you denied."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "They are the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them). None shall enter Paradise (الجنّة) except those who recognized them and whom they recognized, and none shall enter the Fire (النار) except those who denied them and whom they denied."

And he (Fayd Kashani) narrated it in al-Majma' also, and in al-Basair narrated from him (Imam al-Baqir) (peace be upon him): "The men (الرجال) are the Imams (الأئمة) from the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them), and الْأَعْرافِ ** (the A‘rāf) is a bridge (صراط) between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار). So, whoever among the believers (المؤمنين) is interceded for by the Imams (الأئمة) from us, he is saved. And whoever they do not intercede for, he falls into it (the Fire)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "We are those men (الرجال), the Imams (الأئمّة) from us. They recognize who enters the Fire (النار) and who enters Paradise (الجنة), just as you recognize in your tribes the man among you who knows who among them is righteous (صالح) or wicked (طالح)." And the narrations concerning this meaning are numerous. And it is added in some of them: "That is because they are the overseers (عرفاء) of the servants. Allah made them known to them when He took the covenants (المواثيق) of obedience from them. So, He placed them in His Book and said: وَعَلَى الْأَعْرافِ رِجالٌ يَعْرِفُونَ كُلًّا بِسِيماهُمْ ** (and on the A‘rāf will be men who recognize each of them by their marks). And they are the witnesses (الشهداء) over the people, and the Prophets (النبيّون) are their witnesses by their taking (1) the covenants of obedience from the servants for them."

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Every nation (أمة) will be judged by the Imam (إمام) of its time. And the Imams (الأئمة) recognize their friends (أولياءهم) and their enemies (أعداءهم) by their marks (بسيماهم). This is His saying: وَعَلَى الْأَعْرافِ رِجالٌ يَعْرِفُونَ كُلًّا بِسِيماهُمْ ** (and on the A‘rāf will be men who recognize each of them by their marks). So, they will give (2) their friends their book in their right hand, and they will pass into Paradise (الجنّة) without reckoning. And they will give their enemies their book in their left hand, and they will pass into the Fire (النار) without reckoning."

And in al-Basair and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the People of the A‘rāf (أصحاب الأعراف), and He said: "They are a people whose good deeds (حسناتهم) and evil deeds (سيئاتهم) are equal, and their deeds fell short of qualifying them (for Paradise). And they are indeed as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about them, and He said: "They are a people whose good deeds (حسناتهم) and evil deeds (سيئاتهم) are equal. If He admits them into the Fire (النار), it is because of their sins (بذنوبهم); and if He admits them into Paradise (الجنّة), it is by His mercy (برحمته)."

And in the narration of al-Ayyashi: "And if Allah admits them into Paradise (الجنّة), it is by His mercy (برحمته); and if He punishes them, He does not wrong them."

I say: There is no contradiction between these two narrations and the preceding narrations, as most people have claimed. This is because these people (whose deeds are equal) will be with the men (الرجال) who are on the A‘rāf, and both groups are the People of the A‘rāf (أصحاب الأعراف). What we have stated is explicitly indicated by the narration in al-Jawami' and al-Qummi concerning the two verses at the end of these verses, for they indicate that the Imams (الأئمة) will be عَلَى الْأَعْرافِ ** (on the A‘rāf) along with the sinners among their followers (شيعتهم) from their time. The reason for applying the term al-A‘rāf (الأعراف) to the Imams (الأئمّة), as mentioned in several preceding narrations, is that if al-A‘rāf (الْأَعْرافِ) is derived from knowledge (المعرفة), then the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء) are the knowing ones (العارفون), the well-known ones (المعروفون), who make Allah and people known to people in this existence (النشأة). And if it is derived from al-ʿUrf (العرف) (1), meaning the high, elevated place (المكان العالي المرتفع), then they are those who, due to the abundance of their knowledge and the intensity of their insight (بصيرتهم), are as if they are in a high, elevated place, looking upon the rest of the people in their ranks (درجاتهم) and their levels (in the Fire) (دركاتهم), distinguishing the fortunate (السّعداء) from the wretched (الأشقياء) based on their knowledge of them, even while they are still in this existence. Likewise, some of their followers (شيعتهم) who followed their path (are like this), as indicated by the narration of Harithah ibn al-Nu‘man, who used to look at the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة) visiting each other in Paradise (الجنّة) and at the people of the Fire (أهل النار) howling in the Fire (النار), while he was still in this world (الدنيا).
