His narration is recorded in al-Kafi. وَنادَوْا ** meaning, the People of the A‘rāf (أصحاب الأعراف) called out—and by them are meant those who were the Imams (peace be upon them) on the A‘rāf, along with the sinners among their followers (شيعتهم) whose good deeds and evil deeds were equal—أَصْحابَ الْجَنَّةِ أَنْ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** meaning, when they look at them, they greet them with peace. لَمْ يَدْخُلُوها وَهُمْ يَطْمَعُونَ.** (and they will call out to the inhabitants of paradise, ‘Peace be to you!’ They will not have entered it, though they are eager.)

(١) The sand gathered (انكثب), meaning it collected. Everything that poured into something has gathered in it (انكثب فيه). From this, the sand dune (الكثيب) is named because it poured into a place and gathered there. The plural is al-Kuthbān (الكثبان), which are hills of sand.

(١) Meaning, by the Prophets (النبيّين) taking the covenants of obedience from the servants for the Imams (peace be upon them).

(٢) The omission of the nūn (النون) from ya‘ṭū (يعطوا) and the subsequent verbs is perhaps due to their being jussive (مجزومة) as an answer to an implied condition, meaning: If they recognize and are judged, then they will give.

(١) Al-ʿUrf (العرف) is the elevated sand and place. Its rā’ (راؤه) is sometimes pronounced with a ḍamma (ضمّ) like al-ʿUrfah (العرفة) with a ḍamma.

(٤٧) وَإِذا صُرِفَتْ أَبْصارُهُمْ تِلْقاءَ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ قالُوا ** (47) And when their eyes are turned toward the inmates of the Fire, they say, seeking refuge in Allah رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** 'Our Lord! Do not put us among the wrongdoing lot.' meaning: in the Fire.

And in al-Majma' (مجمع البيان): that in the recitation (قراءة) of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), [it was]: 'they said, Our Lord, seeking refuge in You (عائذاً بك) from putting us among the wrongdoing lot.'

(٤٨) وَنادى أَصْحابُ الْأَعْرافِ ** (The residents of the Purgatory will call out) —meaning the Imams (الأئمة)— رِجالاً يَعْرِفُونَهُمْ بِسِيماهُمْ ** (to men whom they recognize by their marks) —from the chiefs of the disbelievers (رؤساء الكفّار). قالُوا ما أَغْنى عَنْكُمْ جَمْعُكُمْ وَما كُنْتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (and say, 'Your accumulation [of wealth] did not avail you, nor did your being arrogant.') —in the world, and what you used to be arrogant about, turning away from the truth (عن الحق).

(٤٩) أَهؤُلاءِ الَّذِينَ أَقْسَمْتُمْ لا يَنالُهُمُ اللهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ ** ('Are these the ones concerning whom you swore that Allah will not extend them His mercy?') —This is a continuation of the Imams' saying to those men, and a reference to their followers (شيعتهم) who were with them upon al-A'raf, whom the disbelievers used to despise in the world and swear that Allah would not admit them into Paradise (الجنّة). ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ ** ([They will be told,] 'Enter paradise! You have no fear, nor will you grieve.') —meaning, they turned to their companions and said to them, "Enter it: لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ. (You have no fear, nor will you grieve)."

In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْأَعْرافِ (the Purgatory) are sand dunes (كثبان) between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار). Every Prophet (نبيّ) and every successor (خليفة) of a Prophet stands upon them, along with the sinners (المذنبين) from the people of his time, just as the commander of an army stands with the weak among his soldiers. The virtuous ones (المحسنون) have already preceded them into Paradise. That successor then says to the sinners standing with him: "Look at your virtuous brothers; they have preceded you into Paradise." The sinners then greet them with peace, and that is His saying: سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ لَمْ يَدْخُلُوها وَهُمْ يَطْمَعُونَ (Peace be upon you! They have not entered it, though they are eager [to do so])—that Allah may admit them into it through the intercession (شفاعة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imam عليه السلام. And these people look towards the people of the Fire and say: رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (Our Lord! Do not put us among the wrongdoing lot). And أَصْحابُ الْأَعْرافِ (the residents of the Purgatory)—who are the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the successors (الخلفاء)—call out to رِجالاً (men) from the people of the Fire and the chiefs of the disbelievers (رؤساء الكفّار), saying to them in reproach: ما أَغْنى عَنْكُمْ جَمْعُكُمْ (Your accumulation [of wealth] did not avail you) and your arrogance (واستكباركم). أَهؤُلاءِ الَّذِينَ أَقْسَمْتُمْ لا يَنالُهُمُ اللهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ ('Are these the ones concerning whom you swore that Allah will not extend them His mercy?')—This is a reference for them to the people of Paradise whom the chiefs used to deem weak (يستضعفونهم) and despise (ويحتقرونهن) due to their poverty, and whom they used to dominate (ويستطيلون عليهم) with their worldly possessions, swearing that Allah would not admit them into Paradise. ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ ('Enter paradise!')—The residents of al-A'raf say this to these deemed-weak ones, by command from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), for them to do so: ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ (Enter paradise! You have no fear, nor will you grieve), meaning, neither fearful nor grieving.

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): الْأَعْرافِ (the Purgatory) are sand dunes (كثبان) between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النار). The men (الرجال) are the Imams (peace be upon them), who stand upon al-A'raf with their followers (شيعتهم). The believers (المؤمنون) have already preceded them into Paradise. The Imams then say to their followers who are sinners (أصحاب الذنوب): "Look at your brothers in Paradise; they have preceded you to it without reckoning." And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ لَمْ يَدْخُلُوها وَهُمْ يَطْمَعُونَ (Peace be upon you! They have not entered it, though they are eager [to do so]). Then it is said to them: "Look at your enemies in the Fire." And that is His saying: وَإِذا صُرِفَتْ أَبْصارُهُمْ تِلْقاءَ أَصْحابِ النَّارِ قالُوا رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (And when their eyes are turned toward the residents of the Fire, they say, 'Our Lord! Do not put us among the wrongdoing lot.') وَنادى أَصْحابُ الْأَعْرافِ رِجالاً يَعْرِفُونَهُمْ بِسِيماهُمْ (The residents of the Purgatory will call out to men whom they recognize by their marks) in the Fire. Then قالُوا ما أَغْنى عَنْكُمْ جَمْعُكُمْ (and say, 'Your accumulation [of wealth] did not avail you') in the world وَما كُنْتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (nor did your being arrogant). Then they say to their enemies in the Fire: "These are my followers (شيعتي) and my brothers, concerning whom you used to swear in the world that Allah would not extend them His mercy." Then the Imams say to their followers: ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا أَنْتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ (Enter paradise! You have no fear, nor will you grieve).

(50) وَنادى أَصْحابُ النَّارِ أَصْحابَ الْجَنَّةِ أَنْ أَفِيضُوا عَلَيْنا مِنَ الْماءِ ** (The inmates of the Fire will call out to the inmates of Paradise, 'Pour upon us some water) meaning: pour it, and that is because Paradise is above the Fire. أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ ** (or some of what Allah has provided you.') of the foods and fruits.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both). He said: Indeed, the people of the Fire die thirsty (عطاشاً), and they enter their graves thirsty (عطاشاً), and they enter Hell thirsty (عطاشاً). Then their relatives from Paradise are raised up for them, and they say: أَفِيضُوا عَلَيْنا مِنَ الْماءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ (Pour upon us some water or some of what Allah has provided you).

And from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Day of Calling Out (يوم التّناد) is the day when the people of the Fire call out to the people of Paradise: أَفِيضُوا عَلَيْنا مِنَ الْماءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ حَرَّمَهُما ** ('Pour upon us some water or some of what Allah has provided you.' They will say, 'Allah has forbidden both of them) He has forbidden the drink of Paradise (الجنّة) and its food عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ.** (to the faithless.')

(51) الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَهُمْ ** which was incumbent upon them to adhere to لَهْواً وَلَعِباً وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا ** (those who took their religion for a diversion and a play, and whom the life of the world deceived) So they forbade what they wished and permitted what they wished. فَالْيَوْمَ نَنْساهُمْ كَما نَسُوا لِقاءَ يَوْمِهِمْ هذا ** (So today We will forget them just as they forgot the encounter of this day of theirs). In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام in a tradition: meaning, We abandon them just as they abandoned preparing for the encounter of this day of theirs. And He said: He only recompenses the one who forgot Him and forgot the encounter of his day by making them forget themselves, just as the Almighty said: وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ نَسُوا اللهَ فَأَنْساهُمْ أَنْفُسَهُمْ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ.** (and do not be like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. It is they who are the transgressors.)

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام regarding its interpretation: He means by forgetting (النسيان) that He did not reward them as He rewards His friends (أولياء) who were obedient and mindful in the worldly abode (دار الدنيا) when they believed in Him and His messengers and feared Him in the unseen (الغيب). And the Arabs may say concerning the chapter of forgetting (النسيان): "We have forgotten so-and-so, so he does not remember us," meaning that He does not command any good for them, nor does He mention them with it. وَما كانُوا بِآياتِنا يَجْحَدُونَ ** (and used to impugn Our signs.) And just as they were deniers (منكرين) of Our signs.

(٥٢) وَلَقَدْ جِئْناهُمْ بِكِتابٍ فَصَّلْناهُ ** (Certainly We brought them a Book which We detailed with knowledge, as a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith.) We clarified its meanings, including doctrines (العقائد), rulings (الأحكام), and detailed admonitions (المواعظ مفصّلة). عَلى عِلْمٍ ** Knowing the manner of its detailing, so that it came (١) wisely (حكيماً), as a guidance (هُدىً) and a mercy (وَرَحْمَةً) for a people who have faith (لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ).

(٥٣) هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ ** (Do they await anything but its interpretation? The day its interpretation comes, those who forgot it before will say, 'Certainly the apostles of our Lord brought the truth. Are there any intercessors for us, so that they may intercede for us, or can we be sent back, so that we may act differently from what we used to do?' They have certainly ruined their souls, and what they used to fabricate has failed them.) Do they await (هل ينتظرون)? إِلَّا تَأْوِيلَهُ ** That to which its affair leads, which is the clarification of its truthfulness by the manifestation of what it spoke of regarding the promise (الوعد) and the threat (الوعيد). يَوْمَ يَأْتِي تَأْوِيلُهُ ** It was said: the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And al-Qummi said: That is upon the rising of the Riser (قيام القائم) and the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ نَسُوهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ They abandoned it with the abandonment of one who forgets (تركوه ترك الناسي). قَدْ جاءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنا بِالْحَقِ ** It has become clear that they came with the truth (بالحقّ). فَهَلْ لَنا مِنْ شُفَعاءَ فَيَشْفَعُوا لَنا Today (اليوم). أَوْ نُرَدُّ ** To the world (إلى الدنيا). فَنَعْمَلَ غَيْرَ الَّذِي كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ قَدْ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ By expending their deeds in disbelief (بصرف أعمالهم في الكفر). وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** It vanished from them (بطل عنهم) and did not benefit them.

(٥٤) **إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (54) Indeed your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days.

Al-Qummi said: "In six periods (أوقات)."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Had He willed to create them in less than the blink of an eye, He would have created them. But He made deliberation (الأناة) and gentleness (المداراة) an example for His trustees (أمنائه) and an establishment of the proof (الحجّة) upon His creation.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was capable of creating them in the blink of an eye, but He, the Mighty and Majestic, created them **فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (in six days) so that He might manifest to the angels what He creates from them, thing after thing, so that they may infer the existence of Allah the Almighty from the occurrence of what occurs, time after time.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah created goodness (الخير) on Sunday, and He would not create evil (الشرّ) before goodness. On Sunday and Monday, He created the earths (الأرضين); He created their provisions (أقواتها) on Tuesday; He created the heavens (السماوات) on Wednesday and Thursday; and He created their provisions on Friday. That is His saying, the Almighty: خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ (He created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days).

I say: This verse, which includes His saying وَما بَيْنَهُما ** (and what is between them), is only found in Surat al-Furqan and Surat al-Sajdah, which follows Luqman. It is understood from this verse, and from this narration and similar ones that have been reported of this kind, that what is between them (ما بَيْنَهُما) is also included in the intended meaning of the verse we are currently interpreting.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created the world (الدنيا) فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (in six days), then He separated (اختزلها) (1) them from the days of the year, and the year is three hundred and fifty-four days.

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah the Almighty created the year as three hundred and sixty days, and خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (He created the heavens and the earth in six days), so He reserved (حجزها) (2) them from the three hundred and sixty days, thus the year is three hundred and fifty-four days. (The narration continues.)

In al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to it. If it is said that the days are only measured and distinguished by the movement of the celestial sphere (الفلك), then how were the heavens and the earth created during the distinguished days before their distinction? We say: The basis (مناط) for the distinction and measurement of the days is only the movement of the Highest Celestial Sphere (الفلك الأعلى), not the seven heavens (السماوات السبع). And what was created during the distinguished days are only the seven heavens, the earth, and what is between them, but not what is above them. And this does not necessitate a void (خلاء), due to the precedence of the water (الماء) from which everything was created over everything else.

And let it be known that this verse and similar narrations are among the ambiguous verses (المتشابهات) whose interpretation (تأويلها) rests with those firmly grounded in knowledge (الراسخين في العلم).

**ثُمَّ اسْتَوى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ ** (Then He settled on the Throne)

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): اسْتَوى ** (He settled) means His management (تدبيره) settled, and His command (أمره) became exalted.

And from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He gained mastery (استولى) over what is subtle (دقّ) and what is manifest (جلّ).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He settled (اسْتَوى) upon everything, so there is nothing closer to Him than anything else.

And in another narration: **اسْتَوى ** (He settled) from everything, so there is nothing closer to Him than anything else.

And in another: **اسْتَوى ** (He settled) in everything, so there is nothing closer to Him than anything else. No distant thing is far from Him, and no near thing is close to Him. He settled in everything.

I say: The Throne (العرش) may sometimes be intended to mean the body (الجسم) that encompasses all bodies (الأجسام). And sometimes it may be intended to mean that body along with everything contained within it of bodies, meaning the entire physical world (العالم الجسماني). And sometimes it may be intended to mean that totality along with all the spirits (الأرواح) that mediate between it and Allah, the Glorified, by which bodies subsist (تتقوّم), meaning all the worlds (العوالم) with their dominion (ملكها), their spiritual realm (ملكوتها), and their realm of power (جبروتها).

In summary, [it refers to] everything other than Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And sometimes it may be intended to mean the knowledge (علم) of Allah, the Glorified, related to everything besides Him. And sometimes it may be intended to mean the knowledge of Allah, the Glorified, upon which He informed His prophets (أنبياءه), His messengers (رسله), and His proofs (حججه). An indication of each of these meanings has occurred in their sayings. And sometimes it is interpreted as dominion (الملك) and al-Istiwā' as encompassing (الاحتواء), as will come in Surat Taha, and this reverts to what has been mentioned. Then I say: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) interpreted al-Istiwā' in the narrations of al-Kafi as the equality of proportion (استواء النسبة) and the Throne (العرش) as the totality of things. And in the first narration, al-Istiwā' implies what is transitive with the preposition على (على), such as mastery (الاستيلاء) and supervision (الإشراف) and the like, in conformity with the Quran. Thus, the meaning becomes: His proportion (نسبته) to everything is equal, while He is dominant (مستولياً) over all. Therefore, the verse contains an indication of the negation of place (المكان) for Him, the Glorified, contrary to what the general public understands from it. It also contains an indication of His Sustaining Presence (معيّته القيّومية) and His spiritual connection (اتصاله المعنوي) to everything equally, in a manner that does not contradict His Oneness (أحدّيته) and the sanctity of His Majesty (قدس جلاله); and [an indication] of the bestowal of general mercy (الرحمة العامّة) upon all at a single proportion, and the encompassing of His knowledge (علمه) over all in a single manner, and His nearness (قربه) to everything on an equal path. The word من (from) was used in the second narration to realize the meaning of al-Istiwā' in nearness and distance, and the word في (in) was used in the third [narration] to realize the meaning of what He settles in. As for the difference in nearness and distance between the favored ones (المقربين), such as the prophets (الأنبياء) and the vicegerents (الأولياء), and those who are driven away (المعبّدين), such as the devils (الشياطين) and the disbelievers (الكفّار), this is not from Him, the Glorified, but rather due to the difference in the essence (ذواتها) of their spirits (أرواحهم).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the narration concerning the Catholicos (الجاثليق): He said: Indeed, the angels carry the Throne (العرش), and the Throne is not, as is supposed, like the form of a couch (السرير), but rather it is a limited (محدود), created (مخلوق), and managed (مدبر) thing. And your Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, is its Owner, not that He is upon it like the presence of a thing upon a thing.

**يُغْشِي اللَّيْلَ النَّهارَ ** (He covers the day with the night)

He covers it with it. And it was recited with tashdid (بالتشديد) [as يُغَشِّي].

**يَطْلُبُهُ حَثِيثاً ** (which pursues it swiftly)

It follows it quickly, like one seeking it, with nothing separating the two.

**وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ وَالنُّجُومَ مُسَخَّراتٍ بِأَمْرِهِ ** (and the sun, the moon, and the stars, subjected to His command)

And it was recited with the raf' (برفع) [nominative case] for all [the nouns].

**أَلا لَهُ الْخَلْقُ ** (Look! All creation belongs to Him)

The world of bodies (عالم الأجسام)

وَالْأَمْرُ (and the command)

The world of spirits (عالم الأرواح)

**تَبارَكَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (Blessed is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds)

He is exalted by Oneness (الوحدانية) in Divinity (الالوهية) and magnified by Singularity (الفردانية) in Lordship (الربوبية).

footnote (١) أي محكماً وخالصاً من كلّ خلل وقدح ومعجزاً ثابتاً باقياً على وجه الدهر.

footnote (١) انخزل الشيء أي انقطع والاختزال الانقطاع.

footnote (٢) أي فصلها عنها وجعل في طرف منها كالحاشية للشّيء.

(٥٥) ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعاً وَخُفْيَةً ** (Invoke your Lord humbly and secretly.) [Meaning:] those possessing humility (تضرّع) and secrecy (خفية). For secrecy (الإخفاء) is closer to sincerity (الإخلاص). And it was recited with a kasrah on the khā' (الخاءِ) [i.e., خِفْيَةً]. إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ ** (Indeed, He does not like the transgressors.) Those who exceed what they were commanded regarding supplication (الدعاء) and other matters.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was on a campaign (غزاة) and overlooked a valley, and the people began to say tahlīl (يهللون) and takbīr (يكبرون) and raise their voices. So He said: "O people, be easy (اربعوا) (1) upon yourselves. Indeed, you are not calling upon one who is deaf nor one who is absent. You are calling upon One Who is All-Hearing (سميعاً), Near (قريباً); indeed, He is with you."

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Seek help from Allah in all your affairs, supplicating humbly (متضرّعاً) to Him during the hours (آناء) of the night and the day." The Almighty Allah said: ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعاً وَخُفْيَةً إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ (Invoke your Lord humbly and secretly. Indeed, He does not like the transgressors.) And transgression (الاعتداء) is a characteristic and a sign (علامتهم) of the reciters (قرّاء) of our time.

(٥٦) وَلا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** by disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي) بَعْدَ إِصْلاحِها ** by the sending of the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the legislation of the rulings (الأحكام). (56) And do not cause corruption in the land after it has been set right. Supplicate Him in fear and hope. Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the virtuous.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The earth was corrupt, so Allah set it right through His Prophet. Thus, He said: وَلا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلاحِها. And al-Qummi (said): He set it right through the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), but they caused corruption in it when they abandoned the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَادْعُوهُ خَوْفاً وَطَمَعاً (And supplicate Him in fear and hope) meaning: possessing fear of rejection (الردّ) due to the deficiency of your deeds and your lack of deservingness, and hope (طمعاً) in His response out of grace (تفضلاً) and benevolence (إحساناً) due to the abundance of His mercy. إِنَّ رَحْمَتَ اللهِ قَرِيبٌ مِنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the virtuous.) This is a preference for hope (الطمع) and an alert regarding that through which one seeks proximity (يتوسل) to the response.

In al-Faqih, concerning the will (وصية) of the Prophet to Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them both): "O Ali, whoever fears a sorcerer (ساحراً) or a devil (شيطاناً), let him recite: إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (Indeed your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days) the Ayah."

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever spends the night in a desolate land (أرض قفر) and recites this Ayah, إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللهُ (Indeed your Lord is Allah) up to His saying: تَبارَكَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (Blessed is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds), the angels will guard him and the devils (الشياطين) will distance themselves from him." He (the narrator) said: So the man went forth, and behold, he came upon a ruined village (قرية خراب), so he spent the night there but did not recite this Ayah. The devils (الشياطين) enveloped him, and behold, he was seized by his muzzle (بخطمه) (1). His companion said to him: "Wait for him (انْظِرْهُ)." The man woke up and recited the Ayah. The devil said to his companion: "May Allah disgrace you (أرغم الله أنفك)! Guard him now until morning." When morning came, he returned to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and informed him, saying: "I saw healing (الشفاء) and truth (الصدق) in your words." He departed after sunrise, and behold, he saw the trace of the devil's hair gathered on the ground. The narrative (الحديث).

footnote (١). *Raba'a* (*ربع*) like *mana'a* (*منع*) means to stop, wait, and restrain oneself. From this comes their saying: *Irba' 'alayka* (*أربع عليك*) or *'ala nafsika* (*على نفسك*) or *'ala dhal'ika* (*على ظلعك*) (Restrain yourself/Be patient).

(٥٧) وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُرْسِلُ الرِّياحَ بُشْراً ** (57) And it is He who sends the winds as harbingers It is the plural of نُشُور (resurrection/spreading) meaning نَاشِر (spreader/publisher). It was also recited with the takhfif (lightening) [of the shaddah on the shīn] and with the fatḥah (vowel 'a') on the nūn [i.e., نُشُرًا], and with the bāʾ lightened [i.e., بُشْرًا], which is the plural of بَشِير (bringer of good news). بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَحْمَتِهِ (before His mercy) means in front of His mercy, meaning the rain. For the east wind (الصبا) (2) stirs up the clouds, the north wind (الشمال) gathers them, the south wind (الجنوب) brings them, and the west wind (الدبور) disperses them. حَتَّى إِذا أَقَلَّتْ ** means they bore/carried. سَحاباً ** means clouds heavy (ثِقَالًا) with water. سُقْناهُ لِبَلَدٍ مَيِّتٍ ** (We drive it to a dead land) for its revival. And it was recited with the takhfif (lightening) of the yāʾ [i.e., مَيْتٍ]. فَأَنْزَلْنا بِهِ الْماءَ فَأَخْرَجْنا بِهِ مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ ** (and send down water upon it, and with it bring forth every kind of fruit) meaning from all its types (أنواعها). كَذلِكَ نُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتى ** (Thus do We raise the dead) meaning We revive them and bring them forth from the graves (الأجداث). **لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (so that you may take admonition) so you may know that whoever is capable of that is capable of this.

(٥٨) وَالْبَلَدُ الطَّيِّبُ ** (58) And the good land The land whose soil is noble (الكريمة التّربة). يَخْرُجُ نَباتُهُ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِ ** its vegetation comes out by the leave of its Lord. By His command and His facilitation (تيسّره). He expressed through this the abundance of the vegetation, its beauty, and the richness (غزارة) of its benefit, due to the context of contrast: وَالَّذِي خَبُثَ (And that which is corrupt/bad), like the stony ground (الحرة) and the saline ground (السبخة). لا يَخْرُجُ ** its vegetation does not come out إِلَّا نَكِداً ** except meagerly. Little, devoid of benefit. كَذلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآياتِ ** Thus do We elaborate the signs We repeat them and reiterate them. لِقَوْمٍ يَشْكُرُونَ ** for a people who are grateful. For the favor of Allah, so they reflect upon them and take heed by them. It was said that the sign (الْآيَةَ) is a parable for the one who reflects upon the signs and benefits from them, and for the one who does not pay attention to them (لم يرفع إليها رأساً) and is not affected by them.

And al-Qummi (said that the verse) is a parable for the Imams (الأئمة), whose knowledge comes forth by the leave of their Lord, and for their enemies, whose knowledge does not come forth except as turbid (كدراً), corrupt (فاسداً) (knowledge).

And in al-Manaqib: Amr ibn al-As said to al-Husayn: "Why is the beard of the ruler (الحاكم) more abundant than our beards?" So he (al-Husayn) recited this verse.

لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً إِلَى قَوْمِهِ (Certainly We sent Noah to his people.) (This is) the answer to a suppressed oath. It is said that he is Nuh son of Malak son of Mutawashlakh son of Idris, the first Prophet after him. And Al-Qummi narrated in the report (الخبر): that the name of Nuh was 'Abd al-Ghaffar, and he was only named Nuh because he used to lament (ينوح) over himself. And in Al-Ilal, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): similar to it. He said: And in one narration: His name is 'Abd al-A'la. And in another: 'Abd al-Malik. He said: And in one narration: He was only named Nuh because he wept for five hundred years. And in Al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Indeed, Adam (peace be upon him) gave glad tidings of Nuh (peace be upon him), and that he would call to Allah and his people would deny him, so Allah would destroy them by the Flood (الطوفان). And he advised his children that whoever among them reached him should believe in him and follow him, for he would be saved from drowning. And there were ten fathers between them, Prophets and successors (أوصياء), and they were concealed (مستخفين), and that is why their mention was hidden in the Quran. And in it [(Al-Kafi)] and Al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The law (شريعة) of Nuh was to worship Allah with Monotheism (التوحيد) and sincerity (الإخلاص) and the removal of rivals (الأنداد), and this is the innate disposition (الفطرة) الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْها ** (which He originated mankind with). And Allah took His covenant upon Nuh and the Prophets that they should worship Allah and not associate anything with Him. And He commanded prayer (الصلاة), commanding the good (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the evil (النهي عن المنكر), and the lawful (الحلال) and the unlawful (الحرام). And He did not impose upon them rulings of punishments (حدود) nor impose inheritances (مواريث). So this is his law (شريعة). فَقالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ ** (So he said, 'O my people! Worship Allah!) Worship Him alone. ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ ** (You have no god other than Him.) And it was read with the jarr (الجرّ) [genitive case]. إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.') If you do not believe, and the Day is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) or the Day of the Flood (يوم الطوفان).

(٦٠) قَالَ الْمَلَأُ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ إِنَّا لَنَرَاكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُبِينٍ (٦٠) (The elite of his people said, 'We indeed see you in manifest error.') The elite of his people said—meaning, the nobles (الأشراف)—'We indeed see you in error'—meaning, firmly established (متمكّناً) in deviation (ذهاب) from the truth (الحق) and correctness (الصواب), 'manifest' (مُبِينٍ), meaning clear (بيّن).

(٦١) قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي ضَلَالَةٌ وَلَكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ (٦١) (He said, 'O my people, there is no error in me, but I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds.') He said, 'O my people, there is no error in me'—meaning, not a thing (شيء) of error (الضلال), being emphatic in the negation (النّفي) just as they were emphatic in the affirmation (الإثبات)—'but I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds'—meaning, possessing the utmost degree (غاية) of guidance (الهدى).

(٦٢) أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنْصَحُ لَكُمْ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٦٢) (I communicate to you the messages of my Lord, and I advise you sincerely, and I know from Allah what you do not know.) 'I communicate to you the messages of my Lord'—meaning, that which was revealed (أُوحيَ) to me over prolonged periods (الأوقات المتطاولة) and concerning various meanings (المعاني المختلفة). And it was read (وقرء) أبلغكم (I communicate) with takhfif (lightening the bā') and رسالة (message) in the singular (الوحدة). 'and I advise you sincerely'—the addition of the lām (لَكُمْ) indicates the purity (إمحاض) of the advice (النصيحة). 'and I know from Allah'—meaning, concerning His attributes (صفاته) and the severity of His grip (شدّة بطشه), or from His direction (جهته) through revelation (الوحي)—'what you do not know'—meaning, things (أشياء) about which you have no knowledge (علم).

(٦٣) أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ ** (Do you wonder) The hamzah is for denial (الإِنكار), and the waw is for conjunction (العطف) onto an omitted phrase, meaning: Did you deny and wonder? that there came to you from أَنْ جاءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (that a reminder came to you from your Lord), which is an admonition (موعظة) from Him, عَلى رَجُلٍ ** (upon a man), meaning: upon the tongue (على لسان) of رَجُلٍ مِنْكُمْ ** (a man from among you). That is because they wondered at the sending of a human being (البشر). لِيُنْذِرَكُمْ ** (so that He may warn you), meaning: so that He may caution you against the consequence (عاقبة) of disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي), وَلِتَتَّقُوا ** (and so that you may be Godwary) due to the warning (الإِنذار), and وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (and so that you may be shown mercy) through Godwariness (التقوى).

(64) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَنْجَيْناهُ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ ** (64) But they impugned him. So We delivered him and those who were with him And they are those who believed in him فِي الْفُلْكِ وَأَغْرَقْنَا الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا ** in the Ark, and We drowned those who impugned Our signs by the flood (بالطوفان) إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْماً عَمِينَ ** Indeed they were a blind lot. blind of heart (عمي القلب), not discerning (غير متبصرين). And its origin is عَمِيِّينَ. And the story of Noah (peace be upon him) will come in Surah Hud, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).

(65) وَإِلى عادٍ ** (And to 'Ād) — meaning, We sent — إِلى عادٍ أَخاهُمْ هُوداً ** (to 'Ād their brother Hūd). By "brother" (الأخ), He means one of them, like their saying, "O brother of the Arabs (يا أخا العرب)" for one of them.

Al-Ayyashi narrates from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): It was said to him that your grandfather said, "Our brothers (إخواننا) rebelled against us, so we fought them due to their rebellion (بغيهم)." He (peace be upon him) said: "Woe to you! Do you not read the Quran? وَإِلى عادٍ أَخاهُمْ هُوداً وَإِلى مَدْيَنَ أَخاهُمْ شُعَيْباً وَإِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً ** (and to 'Ād their brother Hūd, and to Midian their brother Shu'ayb, and to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ). 1. and to 'Ād their brother Hūd, and to Midian their brother Shu'ayb, and to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ. They were like them, and they were their brothers in their tribe (عشريتهم), but they were not their brothers in their religion (دينهم)."

And in another narration, he said: "So Allah destroyed 'Ād and saved Hūd, and Allah destroyed Thamūd and saved Ṣāliḥ."

In al-Ihtijaj is what is close to these two narrations. It was said that He only made him one of them so that they would be more comfortable (أسكن) with him and understand (أفهم) from him better. And he (Hūd) was from the progeny of Sām ibn Nūḥ, just as 'Ād was likewise. And it was said that 'Ād was the grandfather of Hūd.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: And Nūḥ gave Sām the glad tidings of Hūd and said: "Indeed, Allah will raise a Prophet called Hūd, and he will call his people to Allah, but they will deny him, so He will destroy them by the wind (بالرّيح). Whoever among them reaches his time, let him believe in him and follow him." And there were Prophets between them (Nūḥ and Hūd).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When death approached Nūḥ, he called the Shi'ah and said to them: "Know that after me there will be an occultation (غيبة) during which the tyrants (الطواغيت) will appear. And indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will grant you relief through the Qā'im from my progeny, whose name is Hūd. He possesses a characteristic (سمت) (1), tranquility (سكينة), and dignity (وقار). He resembles me in my physical form (خَلْقي) and my character (خُلْقي)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Hūd was sent, the descendants (العقب) of Sām submitted to him. As for the others, they said, "Who is mightier than us in strength?" So they were destroyed by the barren wind (بالريح العقيم). And Hūd advised them and gave them the glad tidings of Ṣāliḥ.

And in it (i.e., al-Ikmal), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Prophets were sent specifically (خاصّة) and generally (عامة). As for Hūd, he was sent to 'Ād with a specific prophethood (نبوّة خاصّة). **قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ ** (He said, 'O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. Will you not be wary [of Him]?') 1. He said, 'O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. Will you not be wary [of Him]?' — meaning, the punishment of Allah.

(٦٦) قالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ إِنَّا لَنَراكَ فِي سَفاهَةٍ ** (66) The elite of his people who were faithless said, 'Indeed we see you in manifest folly, and indeed we think you are one of the liars.' firmly established in lightness of mind (خفة عقل), deeply rooted in it, since you abandoned the religion of your people وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ. (٦٧) قالَ يا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي سَفاهَةٌ وَلكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (67) He said, 'O my people, there is no folly in me, but I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds. (٦٨) أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسالاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنَا لَكُمْ ناصِحٌ ** (68) I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you. regarding what I call you to: the Oneness of Allah (توحيد الله) and obedience to Him; trustworthy (أمين), reliable (ثقة), and secure (مأمون) in conveying the message, so I neither lie nor alter [it]. (٦٩) أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَنْ جاءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَلى رَجُلٍ مِنْكُمْ لِيُنْذِرَكُمْ ** (69) Do you find it strange that a reminder should come to you from your Lord through a man from among yourselves, so that he may warn you? Its interpretation has passed. And in the Prophets' answering the disbelievers regarding their foolish words with what they answered, and turning away from confronting them with the like of it, despite their knowledge that they [the disbelievers] were the most misguided and foolish of creation, there is good conduct (أدَبٌ حسن). And Allah's recounting of that is a teaching for His servants on how to address the foolish (السفهاء) and deal gently with them (يدارونهم). وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفاءَ مِنْ بَعْدِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ ** And remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah, meaning, you succeeded them on the earth after their destruction due to disobedience وَزادَكُمْ فِي الْخَلْقِ بَصْطَةً ** and increased you in stature and strength. stature (قامة) and strength (قوّة). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They were like tall palm trees, and a man among them would cut (1) the mountain with his hand, demolishing a piece of it. فَاذْكُرُوا آلاءَ اللهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ** so remember Allah's bounties so that you may be felicitous.' so that the remembrance of the blessings (النعم) may lead you to gratitude (الشكر) which results in felicity (الفلاح). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Do you know what آلاءَ اللهِ ** (Allah's bounties) means?" It was said: "No." He said: "They are the greatest blessings (نِعَم) of Allah upon His creation, and they are our Wilayah (ولايتنا)."

(1) السّمت (demeanor) is an expression for the state upon which a person is found, consisting of tranquility (السّكينة), dignity (الوقار), good conduct (حسن السّيرة), method (الطّريقة), uprightness of view (استقامة النظر), and appearance (الهيئة).

(٧٠) قالُوا أَجِئْتَنا لِنَعْبُدَ اللهَ وَحْدَهُ وَنَذَرَ ما كانَ يَعْبُدُ آباؤُنا ٧٠ (They said, 'Have you come to us so that we may worship Allah alone, and abandon what our fathers used to worship?') They deemed far-fetched the exclusivity of Allah the Exalted in worship and turning away from what their fathers associated with Him, out of immersion in imitation (التقليد) and love for what they were accustomed to. **فَأْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا ** (Bring us what you promise us) of the punishment, which is indicated by His saying: أَفَلا تَتَّقُونَ (Will you not be wary [of Allah]?) إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ فيه (if you are among the truthful regarding it).

(٧١) قالَ قَدْ وَقَعَ ** it has become necessary (وجب) عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ رِجْسٌ ** a punishment (عذاب) stemming from al-irtijās (الارتجاس), which is the agitation (الاضطراب). وَغَضَبٌ the desire for vengeance (ارادة انتقام). أَتُجادِلُونَنِي فِي أَسْماءٍ سَمَّيْتُمُوها أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمْ regarding things that are nothing but names, having no corresponding referents (مسميات) beneath them, because you named them deities (آلهةً) while the meaning of divinity (الإِلهيّة) is absent in them. And similar to this is what you call upon of anything. ما نَزَّلَ اللهُ بِها مِنْ سُلْطانٍ any proof (من حجة). And if they were worthy of worship, their worthiness would have been established by the sending down of a sign (آية) from Allah and the establishment of a proof (حجة) from Him. فَانْتَظِرُوا ** for the descent of the punishment (نزول العذاب). إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُنْتَظِرِينَ.**

قالَ قَدْ وَقَعَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ رِجْسٌ وَغَضَبٌ أَتُجادِلُونَنِي فِي أَسْماءٍ سَمَّيْتُمُوها أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمْ ما نَزَّلَ اللهُ بِها مِنْ سُلْطانٍ فَانْتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الْمُنْتَظِرِينَ. (He said, 'There has certainly befallen upon you from your Lord a defilement and wrath. Are you disputing with me concerning names which you and your fathers have named, for which Allah has not sent down any authority? So wait; I am with you among the waiters.')

(٧٢) فَأَنْجَيْناهُ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ ** (72) So We delivered him and those who were with him in the religion (الدّين) بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَّا ** by a mercy from Us, upon them وَقَطَعْنا دابِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَما كانُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ ** and We rooted out the last remnant of those who denied Our signs and were not believers. meaning, We utterly destroyed them (استأصلناهم). And that was by Allah, the Glorified, creating a black cloud which they claimed would bring them rain, but from it came a barren wind (ريح عقيم) which destroyed them.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The barren wind (الرّيح العقيم) emerges from beneath the seven earths (الأرضين السبع). No wind has ever emerged from it except against the people of 'Ad, when Allah became wrathful toward them. So He commanded the keepers (الخزان) to release from it an amount equal to the size of a ring. But it disobeyed (عتت) the keepers (الخزّان), and it emerged in the size of a bull's nostril, out of its fury (تغيّظاً) against the people of 'Ad. So the keepers (الخزنة) cried out to Allah, the Most High, concerning that, saying: "O our Lord, it has disobeyed (عتت) our command, and we fear that it may destroy those of Your creation who have not disobeyed You, and the inhabitants (عمّار) of Your lands." So Allah sent Gabriel (جبرئيل) to it, and he repelled it with his wing and said to it: "Emerge according to what you were commanded!" And it destroyed the people of 'Ad and those who were present with them.

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): That Allah, the Blessed and Most High, has a locked house of wind (بيت ريح مقفّل). If it were opened, it would scatter (لأذرئت) everything between the heaven and the earth. He did not send upon the people of 'Ad except an amount equal to the size of the ring. He said: And Hud, Salih, Shu'ayb, Isma'il, and our Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) spoke Arabic (بالعربية). The complete story of Hud will come in Surah Hud, if Allah wills.

(٧٣) وَإِلى ثَمُودَ And We sent إِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً ** (73) And to Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. They were another tribe of the Arabs, named after their eldest father, ثمود بن عابر بن آدم بن سام بن نوح. And Ṣāliḥ was from the progeny (وَلَد) of ثمود. And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for Ṣāliḥ, He was sent to ثمود, and it was a single village, not completing forty houses, small, on the seashore. قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ قَدْ جاءَتْكُمْ بَيِّنَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** He said, 'O my people, worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord: a miracle (معجزة) whose indication of the soundness of my prophethood is manifest. هذِهِ ناقَةُ اللهِ لَكُمْ آيَةً He attributed it to Allah because it was created without an intermediary (واسطة), and for that reason, it was a sign (آية). فَذَرُوها تَأْكُلْ فِي أَرْضِ اللهِ ** this she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. So let her graze in Allah's earth, the herbage (العشب). وَلا تَمَسُّوها بِسُوءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ.** and do not touch her with any harm, or a painful punishment will seize you.'

(٧٤) وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفاءَ مِنْ بَعْدِ عادٍ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِنْ سُهُولِها قُصُوراً وَتَنْحِتُونَ الْجِبالَ بُيُوتاً ** (74) And remember when He made you successors after [the people of] 'Ad, and settled you in the land, [where] you build castles in its plains and hew out houses in the mountains. In al-Majma', it is narrated: That due to the length of their lifespans, they needed to hew out houses in the mountains because the roofs and structures would decay before the end of their lives. فَاذْكُرُوا آلاءَ اللهِ وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ** So remember Allah's bounties, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption. Meaning: And do not go to extremes in corruption.

(٧٥) The Almighty's saying: قالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا ** (They denied following him) مِنْ قَوْمِهِ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا ** (to those whom they deemed weak and whom they humiliated) لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ (a substitute (بدل) for الَّذِينَ) أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صالِحاً مُرْسَلٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (They said it mockingly) قالُوا إِنَّا بِما أُرْسِلَ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ.** (75) The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those of them who were abased, to those who believed, 'Do you know that Salih is an apostle from his Lord?' They said, 'Indeed, we believe in what he has been sent with.')

footnote (١) ذررت الجسد والملح والدواء أذره ذرّاً فرقته ومنه الذّريرة. (1) I scattered (ذررت) the body, the salt, and the medicine—I scatter it (أذره) scattering (ذرّاً)—meaning I dispersed it (فرقته). From this comes الذّريرة (a type of perfume/powder).

(٧٦) قالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا بِالَّذِي آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ كافِرُونَ. (76) Those who were arrogant said, 'We indeed disbelieve in what you believe in.' (٧٧) فَعَقَرُوا النَّاقَةَ ** (77) So they hamstrung the she-camel The hamstrunging (العقر) was attributed to all of them, even though only some of them actually hamstrung her, because it was with their consent (برضاهم). وَعَتَوْا عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ ** (and defied the command of their Lord) They turned away and were arrogant about complying with it, being defiant (عاتين), and that is what He commanded through the tongue of Salih. فَذَرُوها تَأْكُلْ فِي أَرْضِ اللهِ وَقالُوا يا صالِحُ ائْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (So let her eat in Allah's earth.' And they said, 'O Salih! Bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles.')

(٧٨) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ ** (Thereupon the earthquake seized them) The earthquake (الزلزلة). And in Surat Hud (Chapter Hud): وَأَخَذَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ ** (and the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers). And in Surat al-Hijr (Chapter al-Hijr): فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ ** (Thereupon the Cry seized them). And perhaps it (the الرَّجْفَة) was one of its (الصَّيْحَة's) beginnings (مباديها).

Al-Qummi (said): So Allah sent upon them a cry (صيحة) and an earthquake (زلزلة), and they perished. فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ ** (and they lay lifeless in their homes.) Extinguished (خامدين), dead (ميّتين), not moving (لا يتحركون). It is said: The people جثم (lay prostrate/motionless), meaning: sitting (قعود) without movement (لا حراك بهم). And the origin of الجثوم is adherence (اللّزوم) to the place (في المكان).

(٧٩) **فَتَوَلَّى عَنْهُمْ وَقالَ يا قَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسالَةَ رَبِّي وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلكِنْ لا تُحِبُّونَ النَّاصِحِينَ ** (79) Then he turned away from them and said, 'O my people! I certainly delivered to you the message of my Lord and advised you, but you do not love the advisers.'

He said that, regretting what he had missed of their faith, and grieving for them after he saw them dead, laid low.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked Gabriel (peace be upon him): "How was the destruction of the people of Salih?" He replied: "O Muhammad, Salih was sent to his people when he was sixteen years old, and he remained among them until he reached one hundred and twenty years, yet they would not respond to him regarding any good. He said: They had seventy idols which they worshipped instead of Allah. When he saw that from them, he said: 'O my people, I was sent to you when I was sixteen years old, and I have now reached one hundred and twenty years. I present two matters to you: If you wish, ask me, so that I may ask My God, and He will answer you immediately regarding what you asked me. And if you wish, I will ask your gods, and if they answer me concerning what I ask them, I will depart from you, for I am weary of you, and you are weary of me.' They said: 'You have been fair, O Salih. Appoint a day for us when they (the idols) will come out.' He said: So they went out with their idols to their open ground, then they brought near their food and drink, and they ate and drank. When they finished, they called him and said: 'O Salih, ask.' So he said to their greatest idol: 'What is the name of this one?' They said: 'So-and-so (فلان).'

Then Salih said to it: 'O So-and-so (يا فلان), answer!' But it did not answer him. Salih said: 'What is wrong with it that it does not answer?' They said: 'Call upon another one.' He said: So he called upon all of them by their names, but none of them answered him. Then they turned to their idols and said to them: 'What is wrong with you that you do not answer Salih?' But they did not answer. They said: 'Move away from us and leave us and our gods for an hour.' Then they removed their mats and their furnishings, and they removed their clothes, and they rolled in the dust, and threw dust upon their heads, and said to their idols: 'If you do not answer Salih today, we will surely disgrace you.' He said: Then they called him and said: 'O Salih, call upon them.' So he called upon them, but they did not answer him.

So he said to them: "O my people, the first part of the day has passed, and I do not see your gods answering me. Ask me, so that I may call upon My God, and He will answer you immediately." So seventy men from among their elders and those looked up to among them volunteered for him. They said: "O Salih, we ask you, and if your Lord answers you, we will follow you and respond to you, and all the people of our village will pledge allegiance to you."

Salih said to them: "Ask me whatever you wish." They said: "Lead us forward to this mountain," and the mountain was close to them. So Salih went with them. When they reached the mountain, they said: "O Salih, call upon your Lord for us, that He may bring forth for us immediately from this mountain a red, light-colored she-camel (ناقة حمراء شقراء), pregnant (وَبَرْاء), ten months pregnant (وَعَشْراء), with a mile between her two sides." Salih said to them: "You have asked me for something that is great for me, but easy for my Lord, the Exalted."

He said: So Salih asked Allah, the Exalted, for that. Then the mountain split open with a fissure from which their minds nearly flew away when they heard it. Then that mountain shook a violent shaking, like a woman seized by labor pains. Then nothing surprised them except its head emerging upon them from that fissure. Before its neck was fully formed, it chewed the cud (اجترت) (1), then the rest of its body came out, and then it stood upright on the ground. When they saw that, they said: "O Salih, how quickly your Lord answered you! Call upon your Lord for us to bring forth its young one (فصيلها)." So he asked Allah for that, and she cast it forth, and it crawled around her.

So he said to them: "O my people, is anything left?" They said: "No. Let us go to our people and inform them of what we have seen, and they will believe in you."

He said: So they returned, but before the seventy reached them, sixty-four men among them apostatized and said: "Sorcery and lies."

He said: Then they reached the whole group. The six said it was the truth (حقّ), and the whole group said it was sorcery and lies.

He said: So they departed upon that. Then one of the six doubted, and he was among those who slaughtered her. The narrator said: I related this narration to a man among our companions named Sa'id ibn Yazid, and he informed me that he saw the mountain from which she emerged in al-Sham (بالشام). I saw that her side had rubbed against the mountain, and the mark of her side was in it, and there was another mountain between it and this one, a distance of a mile.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِالنُّذُرِ ** (The Thamūd denied the warnings). This is concerning their denial of Salih. Allah, the Exalted, never destroyed a people until He had first sent Messengers to them to establish proof against them. So Allah sent Salih to them, and he called them to Allah, but they did not respond and acted insolently toward Him. They said: "We will not believe in you until you bring forth for us from this rock a she-camel ten months pregnant (ناقة عَشْرَاء)." And they revered and worshipped that rock, sacrificing at it at the beginning of every year and gathering around it. So they said to him: "If you are a Prophet and Messenger as you claim, call upon your God for us, so that He may bring forth for us from this solid rock (الصخرة الصمّاء) a she-camel ten months pregnant (ناقة عشراء)." So Allah brought her forth as they requested.

Then Allah revealed to him: "O Salih, tell them: 'Indeed, Allah has appointed for this she-camel a share of the water for one day, and for you a share for one day.'" So when it was the she-camel's day for drinking, she would drink the water that day, and they would milk her, and no small or large creature remained but drank from her milk that day. Then, when night came and they awoke, they would go to their water and drink from it that day, and the she-camel would not drink that day.

So they remained in that state for as long as Allah willed. Then they acted insolently toward Allah, and some of them walked to others and said: "Slaughter this she-camel and be rid of her! We are not pleased that she should have a share of water for one day and we have a share for one day." Then they said: "Who will undertake her killing, and we will give him whatever reward (جعلاً) he desires?" So there came to them a red, light-haired, blue-eyed man, born of adultery (ولد الزّنا), whose father was unknown, called Qadhdhār (قذّار) (1), a wretched man among the wretched, ill-omened for them. They assigned him a reward (جعلاً). When the she-camel headed toward the water source she used to frequent, he left her until she drank that water and turned back returning. He sat for her on her path and struck her with the sword one blow, but it did nothing. He struck her another blow and killed her, and she fell to the ground on her side. Her young one (فصيلها) fled until it climbed the mountain, crying out (فرغا) three times toward the sky. The people of Salih came forward, and no one remained among them except that he shared in striking her, and they divided her meat among themselves. No small or large creature among them remained but ate from it.

When Salih saw that, he approached them and said: "O my people, what prompted you to do what you have done? Have you disobeyed your Lord?" So Allah revealed to Salih: "Indeed, your people have transgressed and acted unjustly, and they have killed a she-camel Whom I sent to them as a proof against them. There was no harm upon them from her, and there was the greatest benefit for them in her. So tell them: 'I am sending My punishment upon you within three days. If they repent and return, I will accept their repentance and turn away from them. But if they do not repent and do not return, I will send My punishment upon them on the third day.'"

So Salih came to them and said to them: "O my people, I am the Messenger of your Lord to you, and He says to you: 'If you repent, return, and seek forgiveness, I will forgive you and accept your repentance from you.'" When he said that to them, they were more rebellious and wicked than they had ever been, and they said: "O Salih, bring us what you promise us, if you are among the truthful." He said: "O my people, tomorrow morning you will wake up with your faces yellow, and the second day your faces will be red, and the third day your faces will be black."

When the first day came, they woke up with their faces yellow. Some of them walked to others and said: "What Salih told you has come to you." The rebellious ones among them said: "We will not listen to Salih's word, nor will we accept his saying, even if it is great." When the second day came, their faces became red. Some of them walked to others and said: "O my people, what Salih told you has come to you." The rebellious ones among them said: "Even if we were all destroyed, we would not listen to Salih's word, nor would we abandon our gods that our fathers used to worship." And they did not repent or return.

When the third day came, they woke up with their faces black. Some of them walked to others and said: "O my people, what Salih told you has come to you." The rebellious ones among them said: "What Salih told us has come to us." When it was midnight, Gabriel came to them and screamed at them a scream. That scream tore their hearing, split their hearts, and cracked their livers. They had already embalmed themselves (تحنّطوا) and shrouded themselves (تكفّنوا) during those three days, knowing that the punishment was descending upon them. So they all died in the blink of an eye, their young and their old. Nothing remained for them—neither a mourner (ناعية) nor a shepherdess (راعية) [or: a bleating sheep (ثاغية) nor a grumbling camel (راغية) according to another variant (خ ل)]—nor anything except that Allah destroyed it. So they woke up in their homes and resting places, all dead. Then Allah sent fire upon them from the sky along with the scream, and it burned them all. This was their story.

And al-Qummi narrated what is close to some of what is in this narration, in Surat Hud.

(1) Al-ijtirār (اجترّ) (with jim and rā’ without a dot) means the camel eating a second time what it brought forth from what it had eaten first. May Allah have mercy on him (the author/commentator).

(1) He said in Majma‘ al-Bahrayn: And in the Hadith: "Wretched is the servant, al-qadhūrah (القاذورة)." And "Indeed, Allah hates the servant, al-qadhūrah (القاذورة)." Al-qadhūrah (القاذورة) among men is the one who does not care about what he says or what he does. And al-qadhūrah (القاذورة) is the one with bad character. It seems that what is meant here is the filthy one (الوسخ) who does not keep himself clean from impurities (الأقذار). Al-qadhūrah (القاذورة) may also be applied to obscenity (الفاحشة), and a man who is miqdār (مقذار) is one whom people despise. [End of quote]. And it is apparent that the name of this wretched, cursed man is derived from this root.

(80) وَلُوطاً ** (And We sent) لُوطاً ** (Lot), or (And mention) لُوطاً ** (Lot). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The mother of Ibrahim and the mother of Lut were two sisters, and they were the daughters of الّلاحج. And الّلاحج was a warning prophet (نبيّاً منذراً) but was not a messenger (رسولاً). In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): And Lut was the son of Ibrahim's maternal aunt, and Sarah, the wife of Ibrahim, was the sister of Lut. And Lut and Ibrahim were two warning prophets (نبيّين منذرين). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Ibrahim departed from the lands of Nimrud, accompanied by Lut, who never left him, and Sarah, until he settled in the upper regions of الشامات (Syria/the Levant), and he left Lut in the lower regions of الشامات. إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ أَتَأْتُونَ الْفاحِشَةَ ** (When he said to his people, 'Do you commit the indecency) This is a reprimand (توبيخ) and censure (تقريع) for that persistent evil (السيئة المتمادية) in its ugliness. ما سَبَقَكُمْ بِها مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ ** (which no one in the world has committed before you?') No one among the worlds (أحد قطّ) committed it before you. In al-Kafi and al-Ilal, narrated from one of the two Imams (أحدهما) (peace be upon them both), concerning the people of Lut: Indeed, Iblis came to them in a beautiful form, possessing effeminacy (تأنيث), and wearing beautiful clothes. He came to some young men among them and commanded them to commit the act with him. Had he asked them to commit the act upon them (the young men), they would have refused him, but he asked them to commit the act with him. When they committed the act with him, they found pleasure (التذّوا). Then he departed from them and left them, so they turned some of them upon others. And in al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, the first one who committed the deed of the people of Lut was Iblis, for he allowed himself (to be used).

(81) إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجالَ ** (81) (Indeed, you approach men) from (أتى المرأة) when he had intercourse with her (إذا غشيها) شَهْوَةً مِنْ دُونِ النِّساءِ ** (with lust, instead of women.) abandoning the approaching of women whom Allah made lawful to approach, and your reading (قرء انكم) is based on the subsequent statement (الأخبار المستأنف) بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُسْرِفُونَ ** (Rather, you are a transgressing lot.) exceeding the limit in corruption until you exceeded the customary (المعتاد) to the non-customary (غير المعتاد).

(٨٢) وَما كانَ جَوابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا أَخْرِجُوهُمْ مِنْ قَرْيَتِكُمْ ** (And the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Expel them from your town) meaning, they did not come with what would be an answer to his speech, but they came with what is not related to his speech and his advice, namely, expelling him and those with him from their town. إِنَّهُمْ أُناسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ ** (They are indeed people who keep pure.') from indecencies (الفواحش) and foul deeds (الخبائِث).

(٨٣) فَأَنْجَيْناهُ ** (83) Then We delivered him We delivered (خلصنا)

Lot (لوطاً) وَأَهْلَهُ ** and his family, those specifically belonging to him (المختصّين به) from destruction (من الهلاك), إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ ** except his wife, and she was weak (واهلة), for she used to conceal disbelief (تسرّ الكفر) and support (وتوالي) the people of the town (أهل القرية).

كانَتْ مِنَ الْغابِرِينَ ** كانَتْ مِنَ الْغابِرِينَ ** (who was among those who remained behind) from those who remained (غبروا) in their homes (في ديارهم), meaning: they stayed (بقوا فيها) in them, and thus perished (فهلكوا).

وَأَمْطَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ مَطَراً ** (And We rained down upon them a rain) A strange kind of rain, which was a rain of stones of سِجِّيل (baked clay), as will come in another place. فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (So observe how was the fate of the guilty ones.)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That Lot remained among his people for thirty years, residing among them, though he was not one of them. He called them to Allah, forbade them from indecencies (الفواحش), and urged them toward obedience, but they did not respond to Him and did not obey Him. They did not purify themselves from ritual impurity (الجنابة), being miserly (بُخَلاء), stingy (أشِحّاء) with food. So, miserliness (البُخْل) brought upon them the disease for which there is no cure, in their private parts. This was because they were on the route of travelers (السيارة) going to al-Sham (Syria) and Egypt, and guests would stop with them. Miserliness (البُخْل) led them to expose their guest when he stopped with them, and they only did that to deter those who stopped with them, without having any lust for it. Thus, miserliness (البُخْل) brought them to this disease until they began seeking it from men and paying a fee (الجُعْل) for it. And Lot was generous and noble, hosting the guest when he stopped with them. So they forbade him from that, saying: "Do not host guests who stop with you, for if you do, we will expose your guest." So, when a guest stopped with Lot, he would conceal his matter, fearing that his people would expose him, because Lot did not have a clan (عَشِيرَة) among them.

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): the like of it. And the completion of the story will come in Surat Hud and al-Hijr, if Allah wills.

(٨٥) وَإِلى مَدْيَنَ And We sent إِلى مَدْيَنَ أَخاهُمْ شُعَيْباً It was said that they are the descendants of Midian son of Abraham (إبراهيم), and Shu'ayb was one of them. He was called the Orator of the Prophets (خطيب الأنبياء) due to the excellence of his addressing his people. They were named after their grandfather, and their village was named after him. And al-Qummi said: Allah sent Shu'ayb to Midian, which is a village on the road to Syria (الشام), but they did not believe in him. And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for Shu'ayb, He was sent to Midian, and it did not comprise forty houses. قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ alone ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ قَدْ جاءَتْكُمْ بَيِّنَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (It is) a miracle (معجزة) testifying to the soundness of my prophethood, which is not mentioned in the Quran, nor have we found it in any of the narrations (الأخبار). فَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَالْمِيزانَ What is meant by al-kayl (الْكَيْلَ) is the measuring vessel (المكيال), as in Surah Hud. وَلا تَبْخَسُوا النَّاسَ أَشْياءَهُمْ Do not reduce their rights (حقوقهم). The word al-ashya' (الأشياء) was brought for generalization (للتعميم). وَلا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ through disbelief (بالكفر) and injustice (والحيف) بَعْدَ إِصْلاحِها after the Prophets (الأنبياء) and their followers rectified it by establishing the Divine Laws (الشرايع) and the practices (السنن). ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ That is better for you, in terms of humanity (الإِنسانية), good repute (حُسن الأحدوثة), and the profit (الربح) you seek, because if people know you for fairness (النّصفة) and trustworthiness (الأمانة), they will desire to trade with you. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ believing me in what I say.

(85) **وَإِلى مَدْيَنَ أَخاهُمْ شُعَيْباً قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ قَدْ جاءَتْكُمْ بَيِّنَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَالْمِيزانَ وَلا تَبْخَسُوا النَّاسَ أَشْياءَهُمْ وَلا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلاحِها ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (85) And to Midian [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, 'O my people! Worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. A manifest proof has certainly come to you from your Lord. So fill up the measure and the balance, and do not diminish people's goods, and do not cause corruption in the earth after its rectification. That is better for you, should you be faithful.'

(٨٦) وَلا تَقْعُدُوا بِكُلِّ صِراطٍ ** (86) Do not lie in wait on every path, on every path (مَنْهَج) among the paths (مَنَاهِج) of the religion (الدين), imitating Satan in his saying: لَأَقْعُدَنَّ لَهُمْ صِراطَكَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ (I will lie in wait for them on Your straight path). تُوعِدُونَ (you threaten) — meaning, you threaten — وَتَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِهِ ** and obstruct the way of Allah for those who have faith in Him, It is said that they used to sit on the roads and say to whoever passed by them: "Indeed, Shu'ayb is a liar, so let him not tempt you away from your religion," just as Quraysh used to do in Mecca (مكّة). وَتَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً ** and seek to make it crooked. you seek for the path of Allah عِوَجاً ** meaning, you describe it as a crooked path (سَبِيل مَعْوَجَة), not straight (غَيْر مُسْتَقِيمَة), by casting doubts (الشُّبَه) to turn them away from traversing it and entering into it. وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ كُنْتُمْ قَلِيلاً ** Remember when you were few, in your number (عَدَدَكم) or your equipment (عُدَدكم), فَكَثَّرَكُمْ and He multiplied you. through offspring (النَّسْل) and wealth (الْمَال). It is said that Midyan son of Ibrahim al-Khalil (the Friend of Allah) married the daughter of Lut, and she bore him children, and Allah cast blessing (الْبَرَكَة) and growth (النَّمَاء) into her offspring, so they multiplied. وَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** Behold how was the fate of the agents of corruption. those among the nations who caused corruption before you, such as the people of Nuh, Hud, Salih, and Lut, and they were of recent memory to them.

(٨٧) وَإِنْ كانَ طائِفَةٌ مِنْكُمْ آمَنُوا بِالَّذِي أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِ ** (87) And if a group of you have believed in what I have been sent with And they accepted my saying وَطائِفَةٌ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا فَاصْبِرُوا ** and a group have not believed, then be patient So wait and anticipate حَتَّى يَحْكُمَ اللهُ بَيْنَنا ** until Allah judges between us meaning, between the two groups, by granting victory to the one who is truthful (المحق) over the one who is false (المبطل). And this is a promise to the believers and a threat to the disbelievers. وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحاكِمِينَ and He is the best of judges. since there is no one to overturn His judgment (حكمه) and no injustice (حيف) in it.

(٨٨) قالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ يا شُعَيْبُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَكَ مِنْ قَرْيَتِنا أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِي مِلَّتِنا ** (88) The elite of his people who were arrogant said, 'O Shu‘ayb, we will surely expel you and those who have believed with you from our town, unless you return to our creed.' meaning, one of the two matters shall surely occur. And the return (العود) is either in the sense of becoming (الصيرورة), or the address (الخطاب) was directed by giving precedence (تغليب) to the group over the single individual, or it was mentioned based on their assumption (زعمهم). This is because Shu'ayb was never upon their creed (ملتهم) at all, since disbelief (الكفر) is never permissible for the Prophets (الأنبياء). قالَ ** (Shu'ayb) أَوَلَوْ كُنَّا كارِهِينَ He said, 'Even if we are unwilling?' meaning, how can we return to it while we are unwilling (كارهون) towards it?

(٨٩) قَدِ افْتَرَيْنا عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (89) We would have certainly fabricated a lie against Allah regarding that to which we invited you. إِنْ عُدْنا فِي مِلَّتِكُمْ بَعْدَ إِذْ نَجَّانَا اللهُ مِنْها ** if we return to your creed after Allah has delivered us from it by establishing for us the proof (الدليل) of its falsehood and clarifying the truth (الحق) for us. وَما يَكُونُ لَنا ** And it is not for us And it is not proper (يصحّ) for us to return to it, except if Allah, our Lord, wills our abandonment (خذلاننا) and the withholding of His kindness (الإِلطاف), by knowing that it will not benefit us. وَسِعَ رَبُّنا كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْماً ** Our Lord comprehends all things in [His] knowledge. His knowledge encompasses everything that was and everything that will be. Thus, He knows how the states of His servants transform and how their hearts fluctuate. And it was said: He intended by this to cut off their greed (طمعهم) for their return by making it conditional upon what will not occur. عَلَى اللهِ تَوَكَّلْنا ** In Allah we have put our trust that He will keep us firm upon faith (الإِيمان) and grant us success (يوفقنا) in increasing certainty (الإيقان). رَبَّنَا افْتَحْ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنا بِالْحَقِ ** Our Lord! Judge between us and our people with the truth, Judge between us, for al-Fattāḥ (الفتاح) is the Judge (القاضي), and al-Fattāḥah (الفتاحة) is the judgment (الحكومة). Or, make our matter manifest so that what is between us and them is uncovered, and the one who is truthful (المحق) is distinguished from the one who is false (المبطل), derived from fataḥa al-mushkil (فتح المشكل) (He opened the difficult matter) when He clarified it. وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْفاتِحِينَ and You are the best of judges. according to both meanings.

(٩٠) وَقالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ ** their nobles (أشرافهم) لَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتُمْ شُعَيْباً ** you have abandoned your religion (تركتم دينكم) إِنَّكُمْ إِذاً لَخاسِرُونَ ** because of your exchange of misguidance (الضلالة) for guidance (الهدى). They said this to those beneath them, discouraging them from faith (الإيمان).

(91) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ ** (91) Then the earthquake seized them. The earthquake (الزلزلة). And in Sūrat Hūd: وَأَخَذَتِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ ** (67) And the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah sent upon them the single Cry (الصيحة الواحدة), and they died. Its parallel has already preceded. فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ ** (91) and they lay lifeless in their homes. Lying prostrate (جاثِمِينَ).

(٩٢) الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا شُعَيْباً كَأَنْ لَمْ يَغْنَوْا فِيهَا ** (Those who denied Shu‘ayb, as if they had never lived there.) meaning, they were utterly destroyed, as if they had never resided therein, and al-maghna (المغني) is the dwelling. الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا شُعَيْباً كانُوا هُمُ الْخاسِرِينَ ** (Those who denied Shu‘ayb were the losers.) in religion and in the world. And the meaning is that they are the ones specified for destruction and annihilation, and for the great loss, excluding the followers of Shu'ayb, because they are the gainers. And in this beginning and repetition, there is a denigration of the opinion of the elite (الملأ), a rejection of their saying, and an emphasis in that.

(93) فَتَوَلَّى عَنْهُمْ وَقالَ يا قَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسالاتِ رَبِّي وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ ** (93) So he turned away from them and said, 'O my people! Certainly I delivered to you the messages of my Lord and advised you sincerely. — but you did not believe me — فَكَيْفَ آسَى عَلَى قَوْمٍ كَافِرِينَ ** So how should I grieve for a faithless people?' — meaning, so how should I grieve for a people who are not worthy of being grieved over because of their disbelief (كفر) and their deserving the punishment (العذاب) descending upon them.

(٩٤) وَما أَرْسَلْنا فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ إِلَّا أَخَذْنا أَهْلَها بِالْبَأْساءِ ** (94) We did not send any prophet to any town but We seized its inhabitants with distress Misery (البؤس) and poverty (الفقر) وَالضَّرَّاءِ Harm (الضرر) and sickness (المرض) لَعَلَّهُمْ يَضَّرَّعُونَ so that they might humble themselves, repent, and submit. (٩٥) ثُمَّ بَدَّلْنا مَكانَ السَّيِّئَةِ الْحَسَنَةَ ** (95) Then We replaced the evil with good until they grew numerous, and they said, 'Hardship and ease have befallen our fathers [as well].' So We seized them suddenly while they were unaware. Meaning, We removed what they were experiencing of affliction (البلاء) and tribulation (المحنة), and We put in its place prosperity (الرخاء) and well-being (العافية). حَتَّى عَفَوْا ** Meaning, they multiplied (كثروا) and grew (نموا) in their souls and their wealth, derived from the saying: the plant عفا (grew profusely), meaning it multiplied (كثر). And from this is the term إعفاء اللحى (letting the beards grow). وَقالُوا قَدْ مَسَّ آباءَنَا الضَّرَّاءُ وَالسَّرَّاءُ ** The blessing made them insolent, so they abandoned thanking Allah and forgot the remembrance of Allah. And they said: "This is the custom of time (عادة الدهر); it alternates among people between ease (السرّاء) and hardship (الضرّاء). Our fathers were afflicted by something similar, yet they did not change from what they were upon, so remain (1) upon what you are upon, just as your fathers were." فَأَخَذْناهُمْ بَغْتَةً ** Suddenly (فجأة), as a lesson (عبرة) for those who came after them. وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** That the punishment (العذاب) was descending upon them, except after its occurrence.

(٩٦) وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرى ** (And had the people of the towns) And if they آمَنُوا ** (believed) instead of their disbelief وَاتَّقَوْا ** (and been Godwary) of polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي), We would have surely opened upon them blessings from the sky and the earth. We would have surely expanded the bounties (الخيرات) upon them and made them easy for them from every direction, by sending down rain (بانزال المطر), bringing forth vegetation (وإخراج النبات), and other than that (وغير ذلك). وَلكِنْ كَذَّبُوا ** (But they denied) the messengers (الرسل) فَأَخَذْناهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (so We seized them because of what they used to earn) because of their evil earning (بسوء كسبهم).

وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرَىٰ آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَرَكَاتٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَٰكِن كَذَّبُوا فَأَخَذْنَاهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ (And had the people of the towns believed and been Godwary, We would have surely opened upon them blessings from the sky and the earth. But they denied, so We seized them because of what they used to earn.)

(٩٧) أَفَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ الْقُرى (The deniers of our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ بَأْسُنا (as) a punishment (عذاباً) بَياتاً (by) night (ليلاً), at the time of resting (وقت بيات) وَهُمْ نائِمُونَ. (Did the people of the towns feel secure that Our might would not overtake them by night while they were asleep?)

(٩٨) أَوَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ الْقُرى أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمْ بَأْسُنا ضُحًى (meaning) the forenoon (ضحوة النهار), and it is originally the name for the light of the sun (ضوء الشمس) when it rises and ascends (إذا أشرقت وارتفعت). And it was recited with the sukūn on the wāw (الواو). وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ (meaning) they are occupied with what does not benefit them (يشتغلون بما لا ينفعهم). (Or did the people of the towns feel secure that Our might would not overtake them in the forenoon while they were playing?)

(1) This is derived from their saying: "This is the custom of time (عادة الدّهر)," meaning they said: "This is the custom of time (عادة الدّهر), ah, so be it."

(٩٩) أَفَأَمِنُوا مَكْرَ اللهِ (99) Did they feel secure against Allah's plan?

Allah's plan (مكر الله) is a metaphor for His gradual leading astray of the servant and seizing him from where he does not expect. And Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The plan (المكر) from Allah is the punishment (العذاب). فَلا يَأْمَنُ مَكْرَ اللهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** (99) none feels secure against Allah's plan except the people who are losers. By abandoning consideration and reflection, this contains an admonition regarding what the servant must be upon: fear of Allah's punishment and avoidance of disobedience (المعصية).

(١٠٠) أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لِلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ الْأَرْضَ مِنْ بَعْدِ أَهْلِها ** (100) Has it not guided those who inherit the earth after its former inhabitants They succeed those who passed away before them in their dwellings. And the verb يَهْدِ (to guide) was made transitive using the particle اللام (ل) only because it carries the meaning of يُبَيِّنُ (to make clear/to explain). أَنْ لَوْ نَشاءُ ** that He لَوْ نَشاءُ أَصَبْناهُمْ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ** that, if We wish, We can smite them for their sins? with the recompense of their sins, just as We smote those before them. وَنَطْبَعُ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ (We seal their hearts) is a new sentence (مُسْتَأْنَف), meaning: and We seal their hearts. فَهُمْ لا يَسْمَعُونَ ** so they do not hear. a hearing of understanding (تَفَهُّم) and consideration (اِعْتِبَار).
