(١٠١) تِلْكَ الْقُرى نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنْبائِها ** (Those are the towns whose accounts We recount to you) some of their accounts. وَلَقَدْ جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (and their apostles certainly brought them manifest proofs) with the miracles. فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا ** (But they would not believe) when they came with them, بِما كَذَّبُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (in what they had denied before) before their coming. Al-Qummi said: They will not believe in this world in what they denied in the الذرّ (pre-existence), and this is a refutation of those who denied the Covenant (الميثاق) in the first الذرّ.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah created the creation, and He created those whom He loved from what He loved, and what He loved was that He created them from the clay (طينة) of Paradise. And He created those whom He hated from what He hated, and what He hated was that He created them from the clay (طينة) of the Fire. Then He sent them forth in the shadows (الظلال). It was asked: "And what are the shadows (الظلال)?" He said: "Do you not see your shadow in the sun? It is a thing, yet it is not a thing." Then He sent forth the Prophets (النبيّين) from among them, and they called them to acknowledge Allah, which is His saying: وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ ** (And if you ask them, 'Who created them?' they will surely say, 'Allah.') Then they called them to acknowledge the Prophets (النبيّين), and some acknowledged while others denied. Then they called them to our ولايتنا (guardianship/authority), and by Allah, those whom He loved acknowledged it, and those whom He hated denied it. And that is the Almighty's saying: فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ (1) مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (But they would not believe in what they had denied before.) Then he (peace be upon him) said: "The denial (التكذيب) was then."

And in another narration: Some of them acknowledged with their tongues but did not believe with their hearts. So Allah said: وَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا (But they would not believe) بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ. (in what they had denied before.)

And al-Ayyashi narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): Indeed, Allah created the creation while they were shadows (أظلّة), and He sent His Messenger Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to them. Some of them believed in him, and some of them denied him. Then He sent him forth in the later creation (الخلق الآخر), and those who had believed in him in the shadows (الأظلّة) believed in him, and those who had denied him on that day rejected him. So He said: فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ. (But they would not believe in what they had denied before.)

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: Allah sent the Messengers (الرّسل) to the creation while they were in the loins (أصلاب) of men and the wombs (أرحام) of women. So, whoever affirmed the truth then, affirmed the truth after that, and whoever denied the truth then, denied the truth after that. كَذلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللهُ عَلى قُلُوبِ الْكافِرِينَ. (Thus does Allah seal the hearts of the faithless.)

(1) The word به (in it/with it) in the Quran is only in Surah Yunus and is not here [in Surah Al-A'raf]. From him, may Allah have mercy on him.

(١٠٢) وَما وَجَدْنا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ مِنْ عَهْدٍ ** (102) We did not find any covenant with most of them, meaning: fulfilling a covenant (وفاء عهد). For indeed, most of them broke the covenant of Allah made with them regarding faith (الإِيمان) and piety (التقوى). وَإِنْ وَجَدْنا أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَفاسِقِينَ ** and We found most of them to be transgressors. meaning: and We knew (علمنا) most of them to be deviating (خارجين) from obedience (الطاعة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): That it was revealed concerning the doubter (الشّاك).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said to Abu Basir: "O Abu Basir, you have fulfilled that which Allah took a covenant (ميثاقكم) from you concerning Our wilayah (ولايتنا), and you have not exchanged us for others. Had you not done so, Allah would have reproached you as He reproached them, where His mention is majestic, He says: وَما وَجَدْنا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ مِنْ عَهْدٍ وَإِنْ وَجَدْنا أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَفاسِقِينَ. (We did not find any covenant with most of them, and We found most of them to be transgressors.)"

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Abu Dharr: "By Allah, no one among those whose covenant (ميثاقه) was taken was truthful and fulfilled the covenant of Allah except the Household (أهل بيت) of their Prophet and a small group (عصابته قليلة) of their followers (شيعتهم). That is the saying of Allah: وَما وَجَدْنا لِأَكْثَرِهِمْ مِنْ عَهْدٍ وَإِنْ وَجَدْنا أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَفاسِقِينَ ** (We did not find any covenant with most of them, and We found most of them to be transgressors.) and His saying: وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ.** (103) but most people do not have faith.

(١٠٣) ثُمَّ بَعَثْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ مُوسى بِآياتِنا ** (103) Then We sent Mūsā after them with Our signs meaning, with the miracles (بالمعجزات). إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ فَظَلَمُوا بِها ** to Pharaoh and his elite, but they wronged them: by disbelieving in them instead of having the faith which was their due right (حَقّها) because of their clarity (لوضوحها). For this meaning, فَظَلَمُوا ** (they wronged) was placed in the position of (موضع) "they disbelieved" (كفروا). And فِرْعَوْنَ ** (Pharaoh) is a title (لقب) for whoever ruled Egypt, like Kisra (كِسرى) for whoever ruled Persia (فارس) and Qaysar (قيصر) for whoever ruled Rome (الرّوم). His name was Qabus (قابوس) or Al-Walid ibn Mus'ab ibn Al-Rayyan (الوليد بن مصعب بن الريّان). **فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** (So observe how was the fate of the agents of corruption.)

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, sent the Tribes (الأسباط)—twelve of them—after Joseph, and then Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his elite, to Egypt alone.

And al-Ayyashi narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): That Pharaoh built seven cities (مداين) to fortify himself in them against Moses. He placed between them thickets (آجاماً) and forests (غياضاً) (1) and placed lions (الأُسدَ) within them to fortify himself against Moses.

He said: When Allah sent Moses to Pharaoh, he entered the city. When the lions saw him, they wagged their tails (تبصبصت) and turned away in retreat (مدبرة). Then he said: He did not approach a city except that its gate opened for him, until he reached Pharaoh's palace where he was residing.

He said: So he sat at its gate, wearing a woolen cloak (مدرعة من صوف) and carrying his staff. When the doorkeeper (اذن) came out, Moses said to him: "Ask permission for me to see Pharaoh." He did not turn toward him.

He said: So he remained there for as long as Allah willed, asking him to seek permission for him. He said: When Moses pressed him repeatedly, the doorkeeper said to him: "Did the Lord of the worlds not find anyone other than you to send?"

He said: So Moses became angry and struck the gate with his staff. There remained no door between him and Pharaoh except that it opened, until Pharaoh looked at him while he was in his assembly (مجلسه). So he said: "Bring him in."

So he entered upon him while he was in a dome (قبّة) that was high, extremely high, eighty cubits (ذراعاً). He said: So Moses said: "I am the Messenger of the Lord of the worlds to you."

He said: So Pharaoh said: "Then bring a sign **إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (if you are one of the truthful)."

He said: فَأَلْقى عَصاهُ ** (So he cast his staff). It had two prongs (شعبتان). He said: Behold, it was a serpent (حيّة). One of the prongs landed on the ground and the other prong was at the top of the dome.

He said: Pharaoh looked into its mouth (جوفها) and it was blazing with fire (نيراناً). He said: And it lunged toward him, so he soiled himself (أحدث) and cried out: "O Moses, take it!"

(١٠٤) وَقالَ مُوسى يا فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِنْ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ إليك **حَقِيقٌ عَلى أَنْ لا أَقُولَ عَلَى اللهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَ ** (104) And Moses said, 'O Pharaoh! I am indeed a messenger from the Lord of all the worlds, sent to you. I am bound not to attribute to Allah anything but the truth.

وكان أصله حقيق عليّ أن لا أقول فقلب لأمن الالتباس أو لأنّ ما ألزمك فقد لزمته أو للاغراق في الوصف بالصدق يعني أنّه حقّ واجب عليّ القول الحقّ أن أكون أنا قائله لا يرضى إلّا بمثلي أو ضمّن حَقِيقٌ معنى حريص أو وضع على مكان الباء كقولهم رميت السّهم على القوس وقرء عليّ على الأصل وعن ابيّ أنّه قرء بالباء وقرء في الشواذ بحذف على

And its origin was (حقيق عليّ أن لا أقول) (I am bound upon myself not to say), but it was inverted (فقلب) to prevent ambiguity (لأمن الالتباس), or because what He has obligated upon you, He has obligated upon Himself, or for the sake of hyperbole (للاغراق) in the description of truthfulness. Meaning that it is a necessary right (حقّ واجب) upon the truthful saying (القول الحقّ) that I be its speaker, and it is not satisfied except with one like me. Or the word (حَقِيقٌ) (bound/worthy) was made to contain the meaning of (حريص) (eager/keen), or (على) (upon) was placed in the position of (الباء) (the letter bā’), like their saying: (رميت السّهم على القوس) (I shot the arrow upon the bow [meaning: with the bow]). And it was recited (عليّ) according to the original form (على الأصل). And it is narrated from Ubayy that he recited it with (الباء). And it was recited in the anomalous readings (في الشواذ) by omitting (على).

**قَدْ جِئْتُكُمْ بِبَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعِيَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (105) I have certainly brought you a manifest sign from your Lord. So let the Children of Israel go with me.'

فخلهم حتّى يرجعوا معي إلى الأرض المقدّسة التي هي وطن آبائهم وكان قد استعبدهم واستخدمهم في الأعمال الشاقة.

So release them until they return with me to the Holy Land (الأرض المقدّسة), which is the homeland (وطن) of their forefathers. He (Pharaoh) had enslaved them and employed them in difficult labors (الأعمال الشاقة).

(١٠٦) قالَ إِنْ كُنْتَ جِئْتَ بِآيَةٍ ** (He said, 'If you have brought a sign) from the One Who sent you, فَأْتِ بِها إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ (then bring it, if you are among the truthful.') in the claim (الدعوى).

footnote (١) الغيضة الأجمة وهي مغيض ماء يجتمع فينبت فيه الشجر والجمع غياض وأغياض وغيّض الأسد اي ألف ألِفَ الغضة. (1) الغيضة (al-Ghayḍah) is الأجمة (al-Ajamah), which is a place where water sinks/collects (مغيض ماء) and where trees grow. The plural is غياض (ghiyāḍ) and أغياض (aghyaḍ). And غَيَّضَ the lion, meaning: he became accustomed to (ألف) the thicket (الغضة).

(١٠٧) فَأَلْقى عَصاهُ فَإِذا هِيَ ثُعْبانٌ مُبِينٌ ** (107) Then he cast down his staff, and behold, it was a manifest serpent. Manifest in its state, there is no doubt that it is a serpent (ثُعْبَان), and it is the great snake (الْحَيَّة الْعَظِيمَة). (١٠٨) وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُ ** from his bosom (جَيْب) فَإِذا هِيَ بَيْضاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ ** (108) And he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to the onlookers. with a luminous whiteness (بَيَاضًا نُورَانِيًّا) whose radiance overcame the radiance of the sun, and Musa was dark-skinned (آدَم), intensely dark (شَدِيد لِلْأُدْمَة), according to what is narrated. (١٠٩) قالَ الْمَلَأُ مِنْ قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّ هذا لَساحِرٌ عَلِيمٌ ** (109) The elite of Pharaoh’s people said, ‘This is indeed a skillful sorcerer. In Surat Ash-Shu'ara, [it says]: قالَ لِلْمَلَإِ حَوْلَهُ ** (He said to the elite around him), and perhaps he [Pharaoh] said it and they said it, or they said it about him [Musa]. (١١٠) يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنْ أَرْضِكُمْ فَما ذا تَأْمُرُونَ ** (110) He seeks to expel you from your land. So what do you advise? What do you advise (تُشِيرُونَ) us to do. (١١١) قالُوا أَرْجِهْ وَأَخاهُ ** (111) They said, ‘Put him off and his brother. Delay them both (أَخِّرْهُمَا) and send them away from you (أَصْدِرْهُمَا) until you see your opinion concerning them both and manage their affair. Al-Ayyashi narrated, disconnectedly (مَقْطُوعًا): There was no illegitimate child (وَلَد سِفَاح) among his courtiers that day, for if there had been, he would have commanded their killing. He said: And likewise, nothing rushes to us except every wicked (خَبِيث) offspring. And أَرْجِهْ was recited by omitting the second hamza (الْهَمْزَة الثَّانِيَة), and by kasra on the hā (الْهَاء) with ishbā' (الإِشْبَاع) (lengthening the vowel) and without it, and with sukūn (سُكُون) on the hā without a hamza. وَأَرْسِلْ فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ** (And send summoners throughout the cities)

(١١٢) يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ ساحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ ** (112) they will bring you every skillful sorcerer. And it was read (سجّار). (١١٣) وَجاءَ السَّحَرَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ قالُوا إِنَّ لَنا لَأَجْراً إِنْ كُنَّا نَحْنُ الْغالِبِينَ. (113) The sorcerers came to Pharaoh and said, 'Is there a reward for us if we are the victors?' (١١٤) قالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ لَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ ** (114) He said, 'Yes, and you will surely be among the favored ones.' And it was read (انّ لنا) based on informing and necessitating the reward (الأجر). (١١٥) قالُوا يا مُوسى إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ ** (115) They said, 'O Moses! Will you cast, or shall we be the casters?' They gave him the choice out of respect for etiquette (الأدب), but their desire was to cast before him, so they drew attention to this by changing the structure (النظم) to that which is more eloquent (أبلغ). (١١٦) قالَ أَلْقُوا فَلَمَّا أَلْقَوْا سَحَرُوا أَعْيُنَ النَّاسِ وَاسْتَرْهَبُوهُمْ وَجاؤُ بِسِحْرٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (116) He said, 'Cast!' And when they cast, they bewitched the people's eyes and terrified them, and they produced a great sorcery. [He said this] out of generosity (كرماً), tolerance (تسامحاً), lack of concern for them, and confidence in the divine support (التأييد الإِلهي) He was about to receive. by making them imagine what was contrary to reality, through tricks (الحيل) and sleight of hand (الشعوذة) (1) and they terrified them with intense terror, as if they sought their fear. in its craft. It is narrated that they **أَلْقُوا ** (cast) thick ropes and long pieces of wood, which appeared like snakes that filled the valley, piled one upon the other.

(117) وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى أَنْ أَلْقِ عَصاكَ ** (And We revealed to Moses: 'Cast your staff') So he cast it, and it became a great serpent. فَإِذا هِيَ تَلْقَفُ ما يَأْفِكُونَ ** (and behold, it swallowed what they had faked.) What they fabricated of the lie (الإفك), which is the turning away (الصرف) and the turning of the thing away from its true nature (وجهه). And تلقف was read with takhfif (lightening) wherever it occurred. It is narrated that when it swallowed their ropes and staffs and devoured them entirely, it turned towards the attendees, so they fled and crowded together until a great multitude perished. Then Moses took it, and it became a staff as it was before. So the sorcerers said: "Had this been sorcery, our ropes and staffs would have remained."

(١١٨) فَوَقَعَ الْحَقُ So it occurred and was established due to the manifestation of its matter. وَبَطَلَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (This refers to) the magic (السحر) and the opposition (المعارضة). (١١٩) فَغُلِبُوا هُنالِكَ وَانْقَلَبُوا صاغِرِينَ So they became humiliated (أذلّاء) and vanquished (منهزمين). (١٢٠) وَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ ساجِدِينَ **And they fell down prostrating, as if someone had cast them down due to the intensity of their falling. And perhaps the truth overwhelmed them (2) and compelled them to prostrate, to the extent that they retained no self-control, so that Pharaoh would be broken by those whom he intended to use to break Moses, and the matter would turn against him.

(١٢١) قالُوا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (121) They said, 'We believe in the Lord of all the worlds,' (١٢٢) رَبِّ مُوسى وَهارُونَ ** (122) the Lord of Moses and Aaron. They substituted the second (phrase) for the first lest it be imagined that they meant Pharaoh by it. (١٢٣) قالَ فِرْعَوْنُ آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ آذَنَ لَكُمْ ** (123) Pharaoh said, 'Did you believe in him before I gave you leave? And it was recited (وقرئ) by omitting the hamzah (الهمزة) [in آمَنْتُمْ] as a statement (على الإخبار). إِنَّ هذا لَمَكْرٌ مَكَرْتُمُوهُ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ (This is indeed a plot you have hatched in the city). This action (الصنيع) is a trick (حيلة) that you and Moses devised (احتلتموها) in Egypt (في مصر) before you left it for this desert (الصحراء), and you conspired (تواطأتم) upon that, لِتُخْرِجُوا مِنْها أَهْلَها ** (so that you may expel its people from it). Meaning: the Copts (القبط), so that it may become clear (تخلّص) for you and for the Children of Israel (لبني إسرائيل). And this speech from Pharaoh was a deception (تمويهاً) upon the people lest they follow the sorcerers in faith. فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ** (Soon you will know). This is a generalized threat (وعيد مجمل) which what follows elaborates upon.

(١٢٤) لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ مِنْ خِلافٍ ** (124) I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and then I will surely crucify you all. meaning, a limb from every side (مِنْ كُلِّ شِقٍّ طَرَفاً) ثُمَّ لَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** [This is] to disgrace you (تَفْضِيحاً لَكُمْ) and to make an example of you (تَنْكِيلاً) for those like you. (١٢٥) قالُوا إِنَّا إِلى رَبِّنا مُنْقَلِبُونَ ** (125) They said, 'Indeed, we will return to our Lord.' meaning, we do not care about death and killing because of our return (انْقِلَابِنَا) to the meeting with our Lord and His mercy, and we shall all return (نَنْقَلِبُ) to Allah, and He will judge between us. (١٢٦) وَما تَنْقِمُ مِنَّا إِلَّا أَنْ آمَنَّا بِآياتِ رَبِّنا لَمَّا جاءَتْنا ** (126) 'You are taking vengeance on us only because we have believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord! Pour patience upon us, and make us die as Muslims.' meaning, you do not deny or find fault with us (وَمَا تُنْكِرُ مِنَّا وَتُعَيِّبُ) except for the belief in the signs of Allah, and that is the foundation of every virtue (مَنْقَبَة) and goodness. رَبَّنا أَفْرِغْ ** meaning, bestow (أَفِضْ). عَلَيْنا صَبْراً ** [meaning] vast, abundant patience that overwhelms us, just as water is poured out (كَمَا يُفْرَغُ الْمَاءُ). وَتَوَفَّنا مُسْلِمِينَ ** [meaning] firm upon Islam (ثَابِتِينَ عَلَى الْإِسْلَامِ).

(١٢٧) وَقالَ الْمَلَأُ مِنْ قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ أَتَذَرُ مُوسى وَقَوْمَهُ لِيُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ (127) Said the elite of Pharaoh’s people, ‘Will you let Moses and his people work corruption in the land, by changing the people against you and calling them to oppose you, وَيَذَرَكَ وَآلِهَتَكَ and abandon you and your gods?’ your objects of worship (معبوداتك). Al-Qummi said: Pharaoh used to worship idols (الأصنام), then after that, he claimed Lordship (الرّبوبيّة). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He recited وَيَذَرَكَ وَإِلَاهَتَكَ (and abandon you and your إلاهتك), meaning your worship (عبادتك). And it was said that Pharaoh made idols (أصناماً) for his people and commanded them to worship them as a means of drawing near (تقرّباً) to him. And for that reason, فَقالَ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى قالَ (So he said, ‘I am your Lord, the most high.’) Pharaoh said: سَنُقَتِّلُ أَبْناءَهُمْ وَنَسْتَحْيِي نِساءَهُمْ ‘We will kill their sons and spare their women,’ just as we used to do before, so that it may be known that we are upon what we were upon of subjugation (القهر) and dominance (الغلبة), and that the dominance of Moses has no effect on our kingdom (ملكتا). And it was recited سَنَقْتُلُ (we will kill) with takhfif (lightening). وَإِنَّا فَوْقَهُمْ قاهِرُونَ and indeed we are dominant over them. dominant (غالبون), and that they are subjugated (مقهورون) under our hands.

(128) قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ اسْتَعِينُوا بِاللَّهِ وَاصْبِرُوا (128) Moses said to his people, 'Seek help from Allah and be patient.' (This was) to calm them down from their distress due to Pharaoh's threat and to console their hearts. إِنَّ الْأَرْضَ لِلَّهِ يُورِثُهَا مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ Indeed the earth belongs to Allah, He makes whomever He wishes of His servants inherit it, and the ultimate outcome is for the Godwary. (This was) a promise from Him to them of victory, a reminder of what He had promised them regarding the destruction of the Copts (القبط) and their inheritance of their homes, and a realization of that promise.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said regarding: إِنَّ الْأَرْضَ لِلَّهِ يُورِثُهَا مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ (Indeed the earth belongs to Allah, He makes whomever He wishes of His servants inherit it). He said: "That which belongs to Allah belongs to His Messenger, and that which belongs to the Messenger of Allah belongs to the Imam after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "We found in the book of Ali: إِنَّ الْأَرْضَ لِلَّهِ يُورِثُهَا مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (Indeed the earth belongs to Allah, He makes whomever He wishes of His servants inherit it, and the ultimate outcome is for the Godwary). And I and the People of my House (أهل بيتي) are those whom Allah has made inherit the earth, and we are the Godwary (المتّقون). And the entire earth belongs to us. So, whoever among the Muslims revives a piece of land and cultivates it, let him pay its land tax (خراجها) to the Imam from the People of my House (أهل بيتي), and he shall have what he consumes from it. But if he abandons it and ruins it after having cultivated it, and a man from the Muslims takes it after him, cultivates it, and revives it, then he is more entitled to it than the one who abandoned it. So, let him pay its land tax (خراجها) to the Imam from the People of my House (أهل بيتي), and he shall have what he consumes from it, until the Qā'im (القائم) from the People of my House (أهل بيتي) appears with the sword, and He will seize it, and prevent (others from taking) it, and expel them from it, just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم seized it and prevented (others from taking) it. Except for what is in the hands of our followers (شيعتنا), for He will make an arrangement (يقاطعهم) with them and leave the land in their hands."

The Almighty's saying: (١٢٩) قالُوا ** (They said) meaning, the Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل). أُوذِينا مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَأْتِيَنا ** (We were tormented before you came to us) [meaning] with the message (بالرسالة). It was said: meaning, by the killing of the prophets (بقتل الأنبياء). وَمِنْ بَعْدِ ما جِئْتَنا ** (and after you came to us) meaning, by its recurrence (بإعادته).

(They said, 'We were tormented before you came to us and after you came to us.')

And al-Qummi said: Those who believed in Moses said: "We were tormented before your coming, O Moses, by the killing of our children, وَمِنْ بَعْدِ ما جِئْتَنا ** (and after you came to us)" when Pharaoh imprisoned them due to their belief in Moses. قَالَ عَسى رَبُّكُمْ أَنْ يُهْلِكَ عَدُوَّكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ (He said, 'Maybe your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land'). He stated explicitly what He had alluded to (كنّى عنه) initially, when He saw that they were not consoled by that [initial statement]. فَيَنْظُرَ ** (then He will observe) meaning, He will see. كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (how you will act) regarding gratitude (شكر) and ingratitude (كفران), and obedience (طاعة) and disobedience (عصيان), so that He may recompense you according to what is found from you.

(He said, 'Maybe your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land, then He will observe how you will act.')

(١٣٠) وَلَقَدْ أَخَذْنا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ بِالسِّنِينَ ** (130) Certainly We afflicted Pharaoh’s clan with years [of famine] with droughts (بالجدوب) due to the scarcity of rains and waters. And al-Qummi means the years of drought (السنين المجدبة). I say : The word al-sinnah (السنّة) became dominant for the year of famine (عام القحط) due to the frequency with which it is mentioned and dated by it. Then, a derivation was made from it, so it was said: asnata al-qawm (أَسْنَتَ القوم) when they suffered drought (أقحطوا) (1). وَنَقْصٍ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ ** and a deficit in crops due to the abundance of blights (العاهات). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ so that they may take admonition. so that they may become aware (يتنبهوا) that this is due to the ill-omen (بشؤم) of their disbelief and their acts of disobedience (معاصيهم), and thus take heed (فيتعظوا). And so that their hearts may soften (ليرق قلوبهم) through hardships (بالشدائد), leading them to seek refuge (فيفزعوا) with Allah and desire what is with Him.

footnote (١) Al-qaht (*القحط*) with the vowelization (*بالتحريك*) is drought (*الجدب*). Qaḥaṭa al-maṭar (*قحط المطر*), yaqḥaṭu (*يقحط*) from the pattern of nafaʿa (*نفع*), means when the rain is withheld (*احتبس*). It is narrated from al-Farra’ that qaḥiṭa al-maṭar (*قحط المطر*) is from the pattern of taʿiba (*تعب*). Qaḥaṭa al-qawm (*قحط القوم*) means drought afflicted them. And quḥiṭū (*قحطوا*) is in the passive voice (*على ما لم يسمّ فاعله*).

(131) فَإِذا جاءَتْهُمُ الْحَسَنَةُ ** (When good came to them)—from abundance (الخصب) and ease (السعة)—قالُوا لَنا هذِهِ ** (they would say, 'This is our due') for our sake and we are deserving of it. وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ ** (but if an ill befell them)—drought (جدب) and affliction (بلاء)—يَطَّيَّرُوا بِمُوسى وَمَنْ مَعَهُ ** (they ascribed the ill omen to Moses and those who were with him), meaning, they took them as an ill omen (يتشأموا بهم) and said, "It only afflicted us due to their ill omen (بشؤمهم)."

Al-Qummi said: الْحَسَنَةُ ** (the good) here means health (الصحة), safety (السلامة), security (الأمن), and ease (السعة). And السيئة (the ill) here means hunger (الجوع), fear (الخوف), and sickness (المرض).

أَلا إِنَّما طائِرُهُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (Look! Their ill omen is only with Allah), meaning, the cause (سبب) of their good and their evil is with Him, and that is His judgment (حكمه) and His will (مشيئته), just as He said: قُلْ كُلٌّ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ.** (Say, 'All is from Allah.' But most of them do not understand.)

(١٣٢) وَقالُوا مَهْما تَأْتِنا بِهِ مِنْ آيَةٍ لِتَسْحَرَنا بِها فَما نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (132) And they said, 'Whatever sign you may bring us to bewitch us with it, we will not be believers in you.' (أي شيء تأتنا) means: Whatever thing you bring us (لِتَسْحَرَنا بِها) to deceive us, (فَما نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ) then we are not believers in you. They intended that they were insistent (مُصِرُّونَ) upon denying him, even if he brought all the signs (الآيات).

(١٣٣) فَأَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمُ الطُّوفانَ ** (133) So We sent upon them the deluge, That which encompassed them (طَافَ بِهِمْ) and overwhelmed them (غَشِيَهُمْ). Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what الطُّوفانَ ** (the deluge) was, and he replied: "It is the deluge of water and the plague (الطَّاعُون)." وَالْجَرادَ وَالْقُمَّلَ ** and the locusts, and the lice, It was said that it is large ticks (كِبَارُ الْقِرْدَان), and it was said that it is small locusts (صِغَارُ الْجَرَاد), and it was said (1) otherwise (غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ). وَالضَّفادِعَ وَالدَّمَ آياتٍ مُفَصَّلاتٍ and the frogs, and the blood—distinct signs. (مُفَصَّلاتٍ) means: manifest signs (مُبَيَّنَات) that no rational person (عَاقِل) would doubt are the signs (آيَات) of Allah and His vengeance (نِقْمَتَهُ) upon them. Or, (مُفَصَّلاتٍ) means: distinct signs (مُفَصَّلَات) for testing their conditions, since there was a year (سَنَة) between every two of these signs, and the duration (اِمْتِدَاد) of each one was a week (أُسْبُوعًا). فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا ** Yet they acted arrogantly, (فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا) means: from belief (عَنِ الْإِيمَان). وَكانُوا قَوْماً مُجْرِمِينَ. and they were a guilty lot.

(134) وَلَمَّا وَقَعَ عَلَيْهِمُ الرِّجْزُ (134) And when the plague fell upon them (meaning) the punishment (العذاب). Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The plague (الرِّجْزُ) is the snow (الثلج). Then He said: Khurasan is a land of rijz (رجز). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That red snow (ثلج أحمر) afflicted them, which they had not seen before that, so they died in it and were distressed, and they were afflicted by what they had not known before it. قالُوا يا مُوسَى ادْعُ لَنا رَبَّكَ بِما عَهِدَ عِنْدَكَ (They said, 'O Moses, invoke your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you) (meaning) by His covenant (بعهده) with you. لَئِنْ كَشَفْتَ عَنَّا الرِّجْزَ لَنُؤْمِنَنَّ لَكَ وَلَنُرْسِلَنَّ مَعَكَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ. (if You remove the plague from us, we will surely believe in you, and we will surely send the Children of Israel with you.')

footnote (١).** وقيل** الدّبا الّذي لا أجنحة له (1). And it was said (وقيل) that al-dabbā (الدّبا) is that which has no wings. Some exegetes (المفسّرين) said: The scholars differed concerning the lice (الْقُمَّلَ) sent upon the Children of Israel. It was said that it is the weevil (السوس) and that which emerges from wheat (الحنطة). And it was said (وقيل) otherwise. And it was narrated: That Moses (peace be upon him) walked to a dusty dune (كثيب أعفر), a shifting dune (كثيب مهيل), and struck it with his staff, and it all scattered as qummal (قملاً) throughout Egypt. So it pursued their cultivated lands (حروثهم), their trees (أشجارهم), and their vegetation (نباتهم), and ate it and licked the earth. And it would enter between the garment (ثوب) of one of them and his skin (جلدهم) and bite him. And one of them would eat food, and it would become full of qummal (قملاً). And they were not afflicted by a trial that was harder upon them than the lice (الْقُمَّلَ), for it seized their hair (شعورهم), their skins (أبشارهم), the edges of their eyes (أشفار عيونهم), and their eyebrows (حواجبهم), and clung to their skins as if it were smallpox (الجدري), and prevented them from sleep (النّوم) and rest (القرار).

(١٣٥) فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنا عَنْهُمُ الرِّجْزَ إِلى أَجَلٍ هُمْ بالِغُوهُ ** (135) But when We removed the plague from them until a term which they were to reach, [Meaning:] until a limit of time which they were to reach, behold, they broke [their promise] (يَنْكُثُونَ). They were surprised by the breaking [of the promise] (النكث) and hastened to it, and did not delay it. (١٣٦) فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ ** (136) We took vengeance on them, [Meaning:] So We desired vengeance from them, فَأَغْرَقْناهُمْ فِي الْيَمِ ** (and drowned them in the sea) [Meaning:] in the sea whose bottom cannot be reached, بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَكانُوا عَنْها غافِلِينَ ** (because they denied Our signs and were oblivious to them.) Al-Qummi (narrated this) disconnectedly (مقطوعاً), and its narration was attributed in al-Majma' to al-Baqir (peace be upon him) and al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He (the Imam) said: When the sorcerers prostrated and the people believed in him (Musa), Haman said to Pharaoh: "The people have believed in Musa, so look for whoever has entered his religion and imprison him." So he imprisoned everyone from the Children of Israel who believed in him. Musa came to him and said: "Release the Children of Israel." But he did not do so. So, in that year, Allah sent down upon them الطُّوفانَ ** (the flood). It destroyed their houses and dwellings until they went out into the wilderness and pitched tents. Pharaoh said to Musa: "Call upon your Lord for us, so that He may remove the flood (الطوفان) from us, and I will release the Children of Israel and your companions." So Musa called upon his Lord, and He removed the flood from them. Pharaoh intended to release the Children of Israel, but Haman said to him: "If you release the Children of Israel, Musa will overcome you and remove your kingdom." So he accepted Haman's advice and did not release the Children of Israel. So, in the second year, Allah sent down upon them الْجَرادَ ** (the locusts). They stripped everything they had of vegetation and trees, even stripping their hair and beards. Pharaoh was greatly distressed by this and said: "O Musa, call upon your Lord to remove الْجَرادَ ** (the locusts) from us, and I will release the Children of Israel and your companions." So Musa called upon his Lord, and He removed الْجَرادَ ** (the locusts) from them. But Haman did not allow him to release the Children of Israel. So, in the third year, Allah sent down upon them الْقُمَّلَ ** (the lice/vermin). Their crops vanished, and famine afflicted them. Pharaoh said to Musa: "If you remove الْقُمَّلَ ** (the lice) from us, I will cease [to hold] the Children of Israel." So Musa called upon his Lord until the lice (الْقُمَّلَ) departed. He (the Imam) said: The first time Allah created the lice (الْقُمَّلَ) was in that era. But he did not release the Children of Israel. After that, Allah sent upon them الضَّفادِعَ ** (the frogs). They were found in their food and drink, and it is said that they emerged from their anuses (أدبارهم), ears, and noses. They were greatly distressed by this. So they came to Musa and said: "Call upon Allah to remove الضَّفادِعَ ** (the frogs) from us. Indeed, we will believe in you and send the Children of Israel with you." So Musa called upon his Lord, and Allah removed that from them. But when they refused to release the Children of Israel, Allah turned the water of the Nile into blood. The Copt saw it as blood, while the Israelite saw it as water. If the Israelite drank it, it was water, but if the Copt drank it, he drank blood. The Copt would say to the Israelite: "Take the water in your mouth and pour it into mine." But when he poured it into the Copt's mouth, it turned into blood. They were greatly distressed by this and said to Musa: "If the blood is removed from us, we will surely send the Children of Israel with you (لَنُرْسِلَنَّ مَعَكَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ)." But when Allah removed the blood from them, they betrayed [their promise] and did not release the Children of Israel. So Allah sent upon them الرِّجْزُ ** (the plague/torment), which was snow (الثلج), and they had never seen it before that. They were overwhelmed by it, distressed, and afflicted by what they had not known before. So قالُوا يا مُوسَى ادْعُ لَنا رَبَّكَ بِما عَهِدَ عِنْدَكَ لَئِنْ كَشَفْتَ عَنَّا الرِّجْزَ لَنُؤْمِنَنَّ لَكَ وَلَنُرْسِلَنَّ مَعَكَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (They said, 'O Moses, invoke your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you: if you remove the plague from us, we will surely believe in you, and we will surely send the Children of Israel with you.') So he called upon his Lord, and He removed the snow (الثلج) from them. So he released the Children of Israel. When they were released, they gathered around Musa, and Musa departed from Egypt. Those who had fled from Pharaoh gathered around him. This reached Pharaoh, and Haman said to him: "I forbade you from releasing the Children of Israel, and now they have gathered around him." Pharaoh was distressed and sent **فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** (summoners into the cities) and went out in pursuit of Musa.

(١٣٧) وَأَوْرَثْنَا الْقَوْمَ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا يُسْتَضْعَفُونَ (137) And We made the people who were abased inherit meaning the Children of Israel, whom Pharaoh and his people used to abase through enslavement and the slaughter of their sons. مَشَارِقَ الْأَرْضِ وَمَغَارِبَهَا the easts and the wests of the land, meaning the land of Egypt and Syria (الشام). The Children of Israel inherited it after the Pharaohs and the Amalekites (العمالقة), and they became established in its regions which We had blessed with fertility (الخصب) and livelihood (العيش). وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ الْحُسْنَى عَلَى بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ and the good word of your Lord was fulfilled for the Children of Israel and it was carried out upon them and connected to the fulfillment of His promise to them of victory and establishment (التمكين). This is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا And We desired to confer favour upon those who were abased up to His saying: ما كانوا يحذرون (what they used to beware of). And it was recited (وقرء) as kalimātu Rabbika (كلمات ربّك) (the words of your Lord) due to the multiplicity of promises (المواعيد). بِمَا صَبَرُوا because of their patience. because of their patience in the face of hardships (الشدائد). وَدَمَّرْنَا And We destroyed and We ruined what Pharaoh and his people used to construct (يَصْنَعُ) of palaces (القصور) and buildings (العمارات), and what they used to trellis (يَعْرِشُونَ) of gardens (الجنان), or what they used to raise up of structures (البنيان). And it was recited (وقرء) with a ḍammah on the rā’ (بضمّ الراء).

(١٣٨) وَجاوَزْنا بِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ الْبَحْرَ ** after the destruction of Pharaoh فَأَتَوْا عَلى قَوْمٍ ** so they passed by them يَعْكُفُونَ عَلى أَصْنامٍ لَهُمْ ** they were persisting in their worship قالُوا يا مُوسَى اجْعَلْ لَنا إِلهاً ** an idol (صنماً) which we may worship كَما لَهُمْ آلِهَةٌ ** which they worship قالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ.** (138) We carried the Children of Israel across the sea. Then they came upon a people who were devoted to their idols. They said, 'O Moses, make for us a god, just as they have gods.' He said, 'You are indeed an ignorant lot.'

(١٣٩) إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ ** (Indeed, these) is a reference to the people (القوم).

مُتَبَّرٌ ** (will be destroyed) means annihilated (مدمّر), shattered (مكسّر).

ما هُمْ فِيهِ ** (what they are involved in). Indeed, Allah will demolish their religion (دينهم) which they adhere to, through my hands, and He will shatter these idols (أصنامهم) of theirs and turn them into fragments (رضاضاً).

وَباطِلٌ ** (and futile) means vanished (مضمحل).

ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (what they have been doing) of worshipping them (the idols). They will not benefit from it, even if they intended thereby to seek nearness (التقرب) to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عز وجل).

(١٤٠) قالَ أَغَيْرَ اللهِ أَبْغِيكُمْ إِلهاً (He said, 'Shall I seek for you a god other than Allah,) I seek for you a deity (معبوداً). وَهُوَ فَضَّلَكُمْ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ (while He has favoured you over all the nations?') —and the situation is that He has singled you out with blessings (نعم) which He has not given to others besides you. (١٤١) وَإِذْ أَنْجَيْناكُمْ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ (And when We delivered you from Pharaoh's clan,) And remember His favor (صنيعه) toward you at that time. And it was read (وقرء) as أنجيكم. يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذابِ (who inflicted on you a terrible torment,) They sought for you and imposed upon you the severity of the torment. يُقَتِّلُونَ أَبْناءَكُمْ Slaughtering your sons. And it was read (وقرء) with takhfif (بالتخفيف). وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِساءَكُمْ وَفِي ذلِكُمْ بَلاءٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (and sparing your women. And in that there was a great trial from your Lord.) In the deliverance (الإِنجاء) there is a great blessing (نعمة عظيمة), or in the torment (العذاب) there is a great affliction (محنة عظيمة).

(١٤٢) وَواعَدْنا مُوسى ثَلاثِينَ لَيْلَةً ** (142) And We appointed with Moses thirty nights (These were the nights of) ذي القعدة. And it was read (وقرئ) as wa-waʿadnā (We promised). وَأَتْمَمْناها بِعَشْرٍ and completed them with ten from ذي الحجة. فَتَمَّ مِيقاتُ رَبِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً so the term appointed by his Lord was completed as forty nights. Its interpretation has already preceded extensively in Sūrat al-Baqarah. وَقالَ مُوسى لِأَخِيهِ هارُونَ اخْلُفْنِي فِي قَوْمِي ** And Moses said to his brother Aaron, 'Be my successor among my people, Be my successor (خليفتي) among them. وَأَصْلِحْ and set things right, what must be set right concerning their affairs. وَلا تَتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ and do not follow the way of the agents of corruption.' And do not obey whoever calls you to corruption (الإِفساد), nor tread his path.

(١٤٣) وَلَمَّا جاءَ مُوسى لِمِيقاتِنا ** And when Moses came to Our tryst to Our appointed time (لِمِيقاتِنا) which We had set and specified for him, وَكَلَّمَهُ رَبُّهُ ** and his Lord spoke to him without an intermediary, just as He speaks to the angels, قالَ رَبِّ أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ ** he said, 'My Lord, show me [Yourself], that I may look at You.' [meaning] show me Yourself, and enable me to see You by manifesting Yourself to me, so that I may look at You and see You. قالَ لَنْ تَرانِي ** He said, 'You shall not see Me.' You will not be able to bear My sight. وَلكِنِ انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ اسْتَقَرَّ مَكانَهُ ** 'But look at the mountain: if it abides in its place,' when I manifest Myself upon it, فَسَوْفَ تَرانِي فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّى رَبُّهُ لِلْجَبَلِ ** 'then you will see Me.' So when his Lord manifested His glory to the mountain, His greatness appeared to it, and His power and command confronted it, جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا ** He levelled it, [meaning] crushed (مدكوكاً), pulverized (مفتّتاً). Al-Dakk (الدك) and al-Daqq (الدق) are close in meaning. It was also recited as dakkā’ (دكّاء), meaning a level ground. وَخَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً ** and Moses fell down unconscious. fainting from the terror of what he saw. فَلَمَّا أَفاقَ قالَ ** When he recovered, he said, magnifying what he had witnessed, سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ ** 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You,' from the audacity (الجرأة) and boldness (الإِقدام) of asking such a question, **وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** 'and I am the first of the faithful.' [meaning] that You cannot be seen.

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: **أَنَا أَوَّلُ ** (I am the first) [He said]: of those who believe and affirm that You cannot be seen.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked how it is permissible that Moses son of Imran, the one spoken to by Allah (كليم الله موسى ابن عمران), did not know that vision (الرؤية) is not permissible for Allah, such that he asked Him this question. He (peace be upon him) said: Indeed, the one spoken to by Allah (كليم الله) knew that Allah is exalted above being seen by the eyes (بالأبصار). However, when Allah spoke to him and brought him near as a confidant (نجيّاً) (1), he returned to his people and informed them that Allah had spoken to him, brought him near, and confided in him (ناجاه). They said: "We will not believe you until we hear His speech as you heard it." The people numbered seven hundred thousand. He chose seventy thousand from them, then chose seven thousand from them, then chose seven hundred from them, then chose seventy men from them for the tryst (ميقات) of his Lord. He went out with them to Mount Sinai (طور سيناء) and stationed them at the foot (سفح) (2) of the mountain. Moses ascended the Mount (الطور) and asked Allah to speak to him and let them hear His speech. So Allah spoke to him, and they heard His speech from above, below, right, left, behind, and in front, because Allah created it in the tree (الشجرة) then made it emanate from it until they heard it from all directions. They said: "We will not believe that this which we heard is the speech of Allah حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً. until we see Allah manifestly."

When they uttered this great saying, and acted arrogantly and rebelliously (عتوا), Allah sent upon them a thunderbolt (صاعقة), meaning a fire that fell from the sky. The thunderbolt seized them due to their wrongdoing (بظلمهم), and they died. Moses said: "O Lord, what shall I say to the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) when I return to them and they say that you took them and killed them because you were not truthful in your claim of Allah confiding in you (مناجاة الله إيّاك)?" So He revived them and sent them back with him. They said: "If you had asked Allah to show you [Himself] so that you could look at Him, He would have answered you. Then you could inform us what He is like, and we would know Him with true knowledge."

Moses said: "O my people, Allah cannot be seen by the eyes (بالأبصار), and He has no modality (كيفية). He is only known by His signs (آياته) and recognized by His marks (أعلامه)."

They said: "We will not believe you until you ask Him."

Moses said: "O Lord, You have heard the saying of the Children of Israel, and You know best what is beneficial for them."

So Allah revealed to him: "O Moses, ask Me what they have asked you, for I will not hold you accountable for their ignorance."

It was then that Moses said: رَبِّ أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ قالَ لَنْ تَرانِي وَلكِنِ انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ اسْتَقَرَّ مَكانَهُ ** 'My Lord, show me [Yourself], that I may look at You.' He said, 'You shall not see Me. But look at the mountain: if it abides in its place,' while it is collapsing (يهوي), فَسَوْفَ تَرانِي فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّى رَبُّهُ لِلْجَبَلِ ** 'then you will see Me.' So when his Lord manifested His glory to the mountain, with His signs (بآياته) from among His signs (آياته), جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا وَخَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً فَلَمَّا أَفاقَ قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ ** He levelled it, and Moses fell down unconscious. When he recovered, he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You,' meaning: I have returned to my knowledge of You, away from the ignorance of my people, وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** 'and I am the first of the faithful.' among them [my people] that You cannot be seen.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) in a discourse: "When we found the choice of the one whom Allah had chosen for prophethood—meaning Moses (peace be upon him)—falling upon the more corrupt (الأفسد) rather than the more righteous (الأصلح), while he thought it was the more righteous rather than the more corrupt, we knew that there is no choice except for the One Who knows what is in the breasts and what the consciences conceal." The narration continues, and its completion will come in Surat al-Qasas, if Allah wills.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: Moses asked, and it flowed upon his tongue from the praise of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: رَبِّ أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ ** 'My Lord, show me [Yourself], that I may look at You.' His asking this was a great matter (أمراً عظيماً) and he asked for a momentous thing (أمراً جسيماً). So he was admonished. Allah the Almighty said: لَنْ تَرانِي ** 'You shall not see Me.' in this world until you die, then you will see Me in the Hereafter. وَلكِنِ ** 'But' if you wish to see Me in this world, then انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ اسْتَقَرَّ مَكانَهُ فَسَوْفَ تَرانِي ** 'look at the mountain: if it abides in its place, then you will see Me.' So Allah the Glorified revealed some of His signs (آياته), and our Lord manifested His glory to the mountain, and the mountain was cut into pieces and became dust (رميماً). وَخَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً ** and Moses fell down unconscious. Then Allah revived him and raised him up, so قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You, and I am the first of the faithful.' meaning: the first of them [his people] to believe in You that He will not see You.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Moses son of Imran asked his Lord for vision (النّظر إليه), Allah promised him that He would sit in a place. Then He commanded the angels to pass by him in a procession (موكباً) with lightning (البرق), thunder (الرعد), wind (الريح), and thunderbolts (الصواعق). Every time a procession passed by him, his shoulder blades (فرائضه) (1) trembled. He would raise his head and ask: "Is my Lord among you?" He would be answered: "He is coming, and you have asked for a great thing, O son of Imran."

And from him [al-Sadiq] and from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon them both): When Moses (peace be upon him) asked his Lord the Almighty: قالَ رَبِّ أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ قالَ لَنْ تَرانِي وَلكِنِ انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ اسْتَقَرَّ مَكانَهُ فَسَوْفَ تَرانِي ** He said, 'My Lord, show me [Yourself], that I may look at You.' He said, 'You shall not see Me. But look at the mountain: if it abides in its place, then you will see Me.' He said: When Moses ascended the mountain, the gates of heaven were opened, and the angels approached in throngs (أفواجاً). In their hands were staffs (العُمُد) with light at their tips. They passed by him throng after throng, saying: "O son of Imran, stand firm, for you have asked for a great thing." He said: Moses (peace be upon him) remained standing until our Lord, Glorious is His Majesty (جل جلاله), manifested His glory, and He levelled the mountain (جَعَلَ الجَبَلَ دَكّاً). وَخَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً ** and Moses fell down unconscious. When Allah restored his soul to him and he recovered, قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You, and I am the first of the faithful.'

In a narration, fire surrounded Moses (peace be upon him) so that he would not flee from the terror of what he saw. And it said that when Moses fell down unconscious (خَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً), he died. When Allah restored his soul to him, he recovered, and قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You, and I am the first of the faithful.'

Al-Qummi [narrated] regarding His saying: وَلكِنِ انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ ** 'But look at the mountain,' He said: So Allah removed the veil (الحجاب), and [Moses] looked at the mountain, and the mountain sank (ساخ) (1) into the sea, and it is still collapsing (يهوي) until this hour. The angels descended, and the gates of heaven were opened. Allah revealed to the angels: "Overtake Moses, lest he flee." So the angels descended and surrounded Moses, saying: "Stand firm, O son of Imran, for you have asked Allah for a great thing." When Moses looked at the mountain sinking and the angels descending, he fell upon his face out of fear of Allah and the terror of what he saw. Allah restored his soul to him, and he raised his head and recovered, and قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You, and I am the first of the faithful,' meaning: the first to affirm that You cannot be seen.

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Karubiyyun (الكروبيين) are a group of our followers (شيعتنا) from the first creation (الخلق الأوّل). Allah placed them behind the Throne (خَلْف العرش). If the light of one of them were divided among the people of the earth, it would suffice them. Then he said: When Moses (peace be upon him) asked his Lord what he asked, He commanded one of the Karubiyyun (الكروبيين) to manifest his glory to the mountain, and He levelled it (جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا).

He said in al-Jawami': And it was said that there is another interpretation for the verse, which is that the meaning of His saying: أَرِنِي أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ (show me [Yourself], that I may look at You) is: Make Yourself known to me (عرفني نفسك) with a clear, manifest definition (تعريفاً واضحاً جَليّاً) by displaying some of the signs of the Hereafter (آيات الآخرة) which compel creation to know You. أَنْظُرْ إِلَيْكَ (that I may look at You) means: I know You with necessary knowledge (معرفة ضرورية) as if I am looking at You, just as it came in the tradition: "You will see your Lord as you see the moon on the night of the full moon," meaning: You will know Him with manifest knowledge (معرفة جليّة), which is in clarity (الجلاء) like your sight of the moon when it is full and has become a full moon (بدراً). قالَ لَنْ تَرانِي ** He said, 'You shall not see Me.' [means]: You will not be able to bear My knowledge (معرفتي) in this manner, and your strength will not endure that sign (الآية). وَلكِنِ انْظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ ** 'But look at the mountain,' for I will bring upon it a sign from those signs. If it remains firm against its manifestation and اسْتَقَرَّ مَكانَهُ فَسَوْفَ ** 'abides in its place, then you will' remain firm by it and be able to bear it. فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّى رَبُّهُ ** So when his Lord manifested His glory [means]: when a sign from the signs of his Lord appeared لِلْجَبَلِ ** to the mountain, جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا وَخَرَّ مُوسى صَعِقاً ** He levelled it, and Moses fell down unconscious. due to the magnitude of what he saw. فَلَمَّا أَفاقَ قالَ سُبْحانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ ** When he recovered, he said, 'Immaculate are You! I repent to You,' from what I proposed, وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** 'and I am the first of the faithful.' in Your greatness (عظمتك) and Your majesty (جلالك).

I say: The verification of the saying regarding the vision (رؤية) of Allah, the Glorified, is what our Master, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), stated: "The eyes did not see Him through the observation of sights (بمشاهدة الأبصار) (1), but the hearts saw Him through the realities of faith (حقائق الإِيمان). He is not known by analogy (بالقياس), nor perceived by the senses (بالحواس), nor resembled to people (بالناس). He is described by the signs (بالآيات), known by the marks (بالعلامات)." And he (peace be upon him) said: "I would not worship a Lord Whom I have not seen."

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: Will the believers see Him on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة)? He said: "Yes, and they saw Him before the Day of Resurrection." It was asked: "When?" He said: "When He said to them: أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قالُوا بَلى (Am I not your Lord? They said, 'Yes')." Then he remained silent for a moment, then said: "Indeed, the believers see Him in this world before the Day of Resurrection. Do you not see Him at this very moment?" It was asked: "Should I narrate this from you?" He said: "No, for if you narrate it, an ignorant denier who does not understand the meaning of what you say will deny it, and then he might assume that this is anthropomorphism (تشبيه), which is disbelief (كفر). Vision by the heart (الرؤية بالقلب) is not like vision by the eye (الرؤية بالعين). Exalted is Allah above what the anthropomorphists (المشبهون) and the atheists (الملحدُونَ) describe Him with."

(1) The Almighty's saying قَرَّبْناهُ نَجِيًّا ** (We brought him near as a confidant) means: one who confides (مناجياً). It is a verbal noun (مصدر) like al-sahil (الصّهيل) and al-na'iq (النّعيق), and applies to both singular and plural.

(2) Safh al-jabal (سفح الجبل) is its lower part where water flows down (يسفح).

(1) In the tradition: "His shoulder blades (فرائصه) trembled, and the shoulder blades (فرائص) of the angels clashed." Fara'is (فرائص) is the plural of farisah (فريصة), which is the flesh between the flank of the animal and its shoulder, which constantly trembles in the animal. Its plural is also faris (فريص). The faris (فريص) of the neck are its jugular veins (اوداجها), the singular being farisah (فريصة).

(1) Sākhat qawā’imuhu fī al-arḍ (ساخت قوائمه في الأرض) (His legs sank into the earth) tasūkh sawkhan (تسوخ سوخاً) and tasīkh sīkhan (تسيخ سيخاً) from the category of qāla (قال) and bā'a (باع): it entered into it and disappeared. Sākhat farasī (ساخت فرسي) (My horse sank) means it plunged into the earth. Sākhat bihim al-arḍ (ساخت بهم الأرض) (The earth swallowed them) means it was swallowed up (خسفت), used in both forms. It is made transitive with the hamzah (الهمزة), so one says asākhahu Allāh (اساخه الله) (Allah caused it to sink). (M.)

(١٤٤) قالَ يا مُوسى إِنِّي اصْطَفَيْتُكَ ** (144) He said, 'O Moses, I have chosen you I have chosen you عَلَى النَّاسِ ** over mankind—meaning, those who are in your time—and even though Aaron (هرون) was a prophet, he was commanded to follow him (Moses) and was neither the one spoken to directly (كليماً) nor the possessor of a Law (صاحب شرع). بِرِسالاتِي with My messages—meaning the books (أسفار) of the Torah (التوراة). And it was recited (وقرء) as بِرِسالاتِي (with My message). وَبِكَلامِي and with My speech—and by My speaking to you. فَخُذْ ما آتَيْتُكَ ** so take what I have given you—what I have given you of the message (الرسالة). وَكُنْ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ ** and be among the grateful—for the favor therein.

It is narrated that the request for the vision (سؤال الرؤية) occurred on the Day of Arafah (يوم عرفة), and the giving of the Torah (التوراة) occurred on the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النحر).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Allah the Almighty revealed to Moses: "O Moses, do you know why I have chosen you (اصْطَفَيْتُكَ) with My speech, excluding My creation?" He said: "My Lord, and why is that?" He (the Imam) said: Then Allah the Almighty revealed to him: "O Moses, I turned My servants over, front and back (ظهراً لبطن), and I did not find among them anyone more humble in soul (أذلّ لي نفساً) to Me than you, O Moses. When you pray, you place your cheek upon the dust," or He said, "upon the ground."

(145) And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): That which is close to it: وَكَتَبْنا لَهُ فِي الْأَلْواحِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ (And We wrote for him on the Tablets an admonition concerning all things) And that which they needed concerning the matter of religion: مَوْعِظَةً وَتَفْصِيلاً لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ (and a detailed explanation of all things) And they were made of chrysolite (زبرجدة) from Paradise, as narrated by al-Ayyashi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): That they were made of green emerald (زمرّد أخضر). فَخُذْها بِقُوَّةٍ (So hold on to them with power) With seriousness and resolve (بجدّ وعزيمة). Al-Qummi said: meaning the strength of the heart (قوة القلب). وَأْمُرْ قَوْمَكَ يَأْخُذُوا بِأَحْسَنِها (and bid your people to hold on to the best of them) With the best of what is in them, such as patience (الصبر) and forgiveness (العفو) in addition to vengeance (الانتقام) and retribution (الاقتصاص). This is similar to the Almighty's saying: وَاتَّبِعُوا أَحْسَنَ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (and follow the best of what has been sent down to you from your Lord) And His saying: فَيَتَّبِعُونَ أَحْسَنَهُ (and follow the best of it). سَأُرِيكُمْ دارَ الْفاسِقِينَ (I shall show you the abode of the transgressors) refers to the dwellings of the past generations who opposed the command of Allah and departed from the obedience of Allah, so that they may take heed. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding al-Jafr: When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down the Tablets to Moses, He sent them down to him, and in them was the clarification (تبيان) of everything that was or will be until the Hour is established. When the days of Moses ended, Allah revealed to him to entrust the Tablets—which were chrysolite (زبرجدة) from Paradise—to a mountain called Zīnah. So Moses came to the mountain, and the mountain split open for him. He placed the Tablets, wrapped up, inside it. When He placed them in it, the mountain closed upon them. They remained in the mountain until Allah sent His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then a group of riders from Yemen approached, seeking the Messenger. When they reached the mountain, the mountain opened up, and the Tablets came out, wrapped up just as Moses had placed them. The people took them. When they fell into their hands, it was cast into their hearts not to look at them, and they held them in awe until they brought them to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then Allah sent down Gabriel (جبرئيل) to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and informed him about the matter of the people and what they had obtained. When they arrived before the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and greeted him, He initiated the conversation and asked them about what they had found. They said: "And what knowledge do You have of what we found?" He said: "My Lord informed Me of it, and it is the Tablets." They said: "We bear witness that You are indeed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلَّم." So they brought them out and placed them before Him. He looked at them and read them, and they were in Hebrew (بالعبراني). Then He called the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "Take these, for in them is the knowledge of the first and the last, and they are the Tablets of Moses. My Lord has commanded Me to hand them over to you." He (the Commander of the Faithful) said: "I do not know how to read them." He (the Prophet) said: "Gabriel (جبرئيل) commanded Me to order you to place them under your head this night, and you will wake up having learned how to read them." He (the narrator) said: So he placed them under his head, and he woke up, and Allah had taught him everything in them. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that they be copied, so he copied them onto a hide, which is al-Jafr. In it is the knowledge of the first and the last, and it is with us, and the Tablets are with us, and the staff of Moses (عصا موسى) is with us, and we are the inheritors of all the Prophets. He said: Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: That rock which preserved the Tablets of Moses was beneath a tree in a valley known as such-and-such (بكذا). And in al-Basa'ir: That al-Baqir (peace be upon him) identified that rock to a man from Yemen who entered upon him. And in it is this narration in another form, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and at its end: So the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took it, and behold, it was a book in Hebrew (بالعبرانية). He opened it and handed it to me. I placed it near my head, and I woke up in the morning, and it was a magnificent book in Arabic (بالعربية), containing the knowledge of what Allah created from the time the heavens and the earth were established until the Hour is established. So I knew that.

footnote (1) With *kasrah* (*بالكسر*) on the verbal noun (*المصدر*) in contrast to faith (*الإيمان*). And in *Tawhid al-Saduq*, *al-Iyan* (*العيان*) is in place of sight (*الأبصار*). And the realities of faith (*حقايق الايمان*) are its pillars, consisting of affirming belief in Allah and His Oneness (*بوحدانيته*), and the considerations of His Names and Attributes, the Mighty and Majestic. And the vision of Allah, the Glorified, by the hearts has ranks according to the degrees of faith, in strength and weakness.

(١٤٦) سَأَصْرِفُ عَنْ آياتِيَ الَّذِينَ يَتَكَبَّرُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ ** (146) I shall turn away from My signs those who are arrogant in the land unjustly. (This turning away is) by sealing their hearts (بالطبع على قلوبهم), so they do not reflect upon them nor take heed from them. وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كُلَّ آيَةٍ ** (meaning) revealed (منزلة) or a miracle (معجزة) لا يُؤْمِنُوا بِها ** due to the corruption (اختلاف) of their intellects because of their immersion (انهماكهم) in blind imitation (التقليد) and caprice (الهوى).

In the Hadith: "If My community (أمّتي) glorifies the world (الدنيا), the awe (هيبة) of Islam will be removed from them. And if they abandon enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر), the blessing (بركة) of the Revelation (الوحي) will be forbidden to them."

وَإِنْ يَرَوْا سَبِيلَ الرُّشْدِ لا يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلاً ** (146) And though they see the way of rectitude, they will not take it as a way. And الرشد (rectitude) was read with two fathahs (i.e., الرَّشَد).

وَإِنْ يَرَوْا سَبِيلَ الغَيِّ يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلاً. (146) But if they see the way of error, they will take it as a way.

Al-Qummi said: If they see faith (الإِيمان), truthfulness (الصدق), fulfillment (الوفاء), and righteous deeds (العمل الصالح), they will not take it as a way. But if they see polytheism (الشرك), fornication (الزنا), and acts of disobedience (المعاصي), they will adopt them and act upon them.

ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَكانُوا عَنْها غافِلِينَ ** (146) That is because they denied Our signs and were oblivious to them. That turning away (الصرف) is due to their denial and their failure to reflect (عدم تدبرهم) upon the signs (الآيات).

(147) وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَلِقاءِ الْآخِرَةِ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ ** (147) But those who deny Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter—their works have failed. They will not benefit from them. هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ** (Shall they be requited except for what they used to do?) except the recompense of their deeds.

(148) وَاتَّخَذَ قَوْمُ مُوسى مِنْ بَعْدِهِ ** (148) The people of Moses took to worshipping a calf made of their ornaments, after he had left, a mere body which lowed. after his departure for the appointed time (لِلْمِيقَاتِ) مِنْ حُلِيِّهِمْ ** And it was recited with a kasrah on the ḥā' (بِكَسْرِ الْحَاءِ) عِجْلاً جَسَداً ** a body devoid of spirit (الرُّوح) لَهُ خُوارٌ ** a sound like the sound of cattle. The story of the calf has already passed in detail in Surat al-Baqarah.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, among what Moses secretly conversed with his Lord, he said: "O Lord, this Samiri made the calf, and the lowing (الْخُوَار) is from his making." He (the narrator) said: So Allah revealed to him: "O Moses, that is My trial (فِتْنَتِي), so do not inquire about it (فَلَا تَفْحَصْ عَنْهَا)."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "O Lord, and who made the idol low (أَخَارَ الصَّنَم)?" So Allah said: "O Moses, I made it low (أَنَا أَخَرْتُهُ)." Then Moses said:

إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ تُضِلُّ بِها مَنْ تَشاءُ وَتَهْدِي مَنْ تَشاءُ أَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّهُ لا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ وَلا يَهْدِيهِمْ سَبِيلاً ** It was only Your trial, by which You lead astray whomever You wish, and guide whomever You wish. Did they not see that it did not speak to them, nor guide them to any way? This is a reprimand (تَقْرِيع) for their excessive misguidance (فَرْطِ ضَلَالَتِهِمْ) and their failure to reflect (إِخْلَالِهِمْ بِالنَّظَرِ). Meaning that it (the calf) is not like ordinary human beings, so how could it be the Creator of strength (الْقُوَى) and destiny (الْقَدَر)? اتَّخَذُوهُ ** as a god (إِلَهاً) وَكانُوا ظالِمِينَ ** placing things in other than their proper places (وَاضِعِينَ الْأَشْيَاءَ فِي غَيْرِ مَوَاضِعِهَا), so the taking of the calf was not an innovation (بِدْعاً) from them.

(١٤٩) وَلَمَّا سُقِطَ فِي أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (149) And when they regretted This is a metaphor (كناية) for the intensity of their regret, for the regretful (النادم) and sorrowful (المتحسر) person bites his hand out of grief (غمّاً), so his hand becomes that into which something has fallen (مسقوطاً فيها). وَرَأَوْا ** And they saw (and they knew) أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ ضَلُّوا ** that they had certainly gone astray (by taking the calf), قالُوا لَئِنْ لَمْ يَرْحَمْنا رَبُّنا وَيَغْفِرْ لَنا ** they said, 'If our Lord does not have mercy on us and forgive us (by overlooking the sin), لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ we will surely be among the losers.' And it was recited (وقرء) with the second-person address (بالخطاب) and the vocative particle (والنداء).

(١٥٠) وَلَمَّا رَجَعَ مُوسى إِلَى قَوْمِهِ غَضْبانَ أَسِفاً ** (150) When Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved, intensely angry, or grieved. قالَ بِئْسَما خَلَفْتُمُونِي مِنْ بَعْدِي ** he said, 'Evil is what you have done in my absence! meaning: you stood in my place and were my successors after me, when you worshipped the calf instead of worshipping Allah. أَعَجِلْتُمْ أَمْرَ رَبِّكُمْ ** Did you seek to hasten the command of your Lord?' It is said (عَجِلَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ) when one leaves an affair incomplete, and (أَعْجَلَهُ عَنْهُ) when someone else makes him hasten from it. It also implies the meaning of preceding (سَبَقَ), so it is said (عَجِلَ الْأَمْرَ). The meaning is: Did you abandon أَمْرَ رَبِّكُمْ ** (part of the previous Ayah) incomplete, which was waiting for Moses while preserving His covenant? وَأَلْقَى الْأَلْواحَ ** And he threw down the Tablets, He threw them down due to the intensity of his anger for Allah and excessive distress, out of zeal for the religion. It is narrated that when he threw them down, they broke, and some parts of them were lost. In al-Basa'ir, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): that some of them broke, some remained, and some were raised up. And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that he showed a man from Yemen a rock in Yemen, then said: "That is the rock which swallowed what was lost from the Torah when Moses threw down the Tablets. When Allah sent His Messenger, it delivered it to him, and it is with us." And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "May Allah have mercy on my brother Moses (peace be upon him). The one informed is not like the one who witnesses. Allah had informed him of the trial (فِتْنَة) of his people, and he certainly knew that what his Lord informed Him was true, and despite that, he held fast to what was in his hands. Then he returned to his people and saw them, so he became angry and threw down the Tablets." And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) something similar in meaning. وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ (١) and seized his brother by the head, dragging him toward himself. (1) In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That was because he did not leave them when they did that, nor did he join Moses, and had he left them, the punishment would have descended upon them. قالَ ابْنَ أُمَ ** He said, 'Son of my mother! And it was recited (أُمِّ) with a kasrah (بالكسر). He only attributed him to the mother because it is closer to evoking compassion (الاستعطاف). And in al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He did not say, "O son of my father," because if the sons of a father have different mothers, enmity between them is not unlikely, except for those whom Allah protects from it. Rather, it is unlikely between the sons of a single mother. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the Sermon of al-Wasilah (خطبة الوسيلة): that he was his brother from both his father and his mother. And al-Qummi narrated the same from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both). It was said that Aaron was three years older than Moses and was forbearing (حَمُولاً) (2) and gentle, and for that reason, he was more beloved to the Children of Israel. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that the revelation (الوحي) would descend upon Moses, and Moses (peace be upon him) would reveal it to Aaron. Moses was the one who communed secretly with his Lord (يُنَاجِي رَبَّهُ), wrote the knowledge, and judged among the Children of Israel. He said: Moses did not have children, but the children belonged to Aaron. إِنَّ الْقَوْمَ اسْتَضْعَفُونِي ** Indeed, the people overpowered me, they overpowered me and deemed me weak, and I spared no effort in restraining them through warning (الإنذار) and admonition (الوعظ). وَكادُوا يَقْتُلُونَنِي ** and were about to kill me. and they came close to killing me due to the severity of my condemnation of them. فَلا تُشْمِتْ بِيَ الْأَعْداءَ So do not make the enemies gloat over me, so do not do to me that for which they would gloat over me. وَلا تَجْعَلْنِي مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** and do not count me among the wrongdoing people.' counted among their number by holding me accountable and attributing shortcoming (التقصير) to me.

(١٥١) قالَ رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِأَخِي وَأَدْخِلْنا فِي رَحْمَتِكَ وَأَنْتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ. (151) He said, 'My Lord! Forgive me and my brother, and admit us into Your mercy, for You are the most merciful of the merciful.' (١٥٢) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا الْعِجْلَ سَيَنالُهُمْ غَضَبٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ قيل هو ما أمرَوا من قتل أنفسهم وَذِلَّةٌ فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا قيل هي خروجهم من ديارهم وقيل هي الجزية وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُفْتَرِينَ وافتراؤهم قولهم هذا إِلهُكُمْ وَإِلهُ مُوسى. (152) Indeed, those who took up the calf shall incur the wrath of their Lord and abasement in the life of the world, and thus do We requite the fabricators. It was said that this is what they were commanded regarding the killing of their souls/selves. And abasement in the life of the world. It was said that this is their expulsion from their homes, and it was said that it is the poll-tax (الجزية). And thus do We requite the fabricators. And their fabrication is their saying: This is your god and the god of Moses.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He recited this verse and then said: "You will not see the follower of an innovation (صاحب بدعة) except that he is abased (ذليلاً), nor one who fabricates lies against Allah, His Messenger, and His Household (أهل بيته) except that he is abased (ذليلاً)."

footnote (١)** قيل** في معناه أنّ موسى انّما فعل ذلك مستعظماً لفعلهم مفكّراً فيما كان منهم كما يفعل الإِنسان بنفسه عند حالة الغضب وشدة الفكر مثل ذلك فيقبض على لحيته ويعضّ على شفتيه فأجرى موسى أخاه مجرى نفسه فصنع به ما يصنع الإنسان بنفسه عند حالة الغضب والفكر. (1) It was said regarding its meaning that Moses only did that out of great astonishment at their action, reflecting upon what had occurred from them, just as a person does to himself in a state of anger and intense thought—for example, he grabs his beard and bites his lips. Thus, Moses treated his brother as he would treat himself, doing to him what a person does to himself in a state of anger and thought.

footnote (٢) حَمَل عنه حَلُمَ فهو حمول ذو حلم. (2) حَمَل عنه (ḥamala ʿanhu) means حَلُمَ (ḥaluma), so he is حَمُول (ḥamūl), possessing forbearance (ذو حلم).

(١٥٣) وَالَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السَّيِّئاتِ ثُمَّ تابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِها وَآمَنُوا إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنْ بَعْدِها لَغَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. (But those who commit misdeeds, then repent after that and believe—indeed your Lord is thereafter all-forgiving, all-merciful.)

وَالَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السَّيِّئاتِ —meaning disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي)—ثُمَّ تابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِها —meaning after the misdeeds (السيئات)—وَآمَنُوا —and act according to the requirement (مقتضى) of faith (الإِيمان)—إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنْ بَعْدِها —meaning after the repentance (التوبة)—لَغَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ.

(١٥٤) وَلَمَّا سَكَتَ عَنْ مُوسَى الْغَضَبُ ** (And when the wrath subsided in Moses) He expressed the calming and extinguishing of the anger by [using the word] 'silence' (السكوت), as an indication that the anger was what compelled him to what he did, and the one commanding him to it and inciting him to it. And this is from the eloquence (البلاغة) in speech. أَخَذَ الْأَلْواحَ ** (he took up the Tablets) which he had cast down. وَفِي نُسْخَتِها هُدىً ** (in whose inscription was guidance) a sign (دلالة) and clarification (بيان) of what is needed concerning the matter of the religion (الدين). وَرَحْمَةٌ ** (and mercy) a blessing (نعمة) and a benefit (منفعة). لِلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِرَبِّهِمْ يَرْهَبُونَ ** (for those who fear their Lord.) the disobedience (معاصي) of Allah.

(١٥٥) وَاخْتارَ مُوسى قَوْمَهُ سَبْعِينَ رَجُلاً لِمِيقاتِنا ** (155) Moses chose seventy men from his people for Our tryst. The phrase قَوْمَهُ (his people) means "from his people" (مِنْ قَوْمِهِ), following the rule of elision (الحذف) and connection (الإِيصال). سَبْعِينَ رَجُلاً لِمِيقاتِنا ** (seventy men for Our tryst). Their story preceded when the request for the vision (سؤال الرؤية) was mentioned. فَلَمَّا أَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ قالَ رَبِّ لَوْ شِئْتَ أَهْلَكْتَهُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَإِيَّايَ ** (Then, when the earthquake seized them, he said, 'My Lord, had You wished, You would have destroyed them and me before.) He wished for their destruction and his own destruction before he saw what he saw. أَتُهْلِكُنا بِما فَعَلَ السُّفَهاءُ مِنَّا ** (Will You destroy us because of what the fools amongst us have done?) ...due to the audacity of requesting the vision (طلب الرّؤية). In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): When the seventy men went with him to the mountain, they said to him: "You have seen Allah, the Glorified, so show Him to us as You saw Him." He said: "I did not see Him." So they said: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ ** (We will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly.' Thereupon the thunderbolt seized you) And they were burned completely, down to the last one. Moses remained alone. He said: "O Lord, I chose seventy men from the Children of Israel, and I brought them, but I return alone. How will my people believe me regarding what I inform them?" So, لَوْ شِئْتَ أَهْلَكْتَهُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَإِيَّايَ أَتُهْلِكُنا بِما فَعَلَ السُّفَهاءُ مِنَّا ** (had You wished, You would have destroyed them and me before. Will You destroy us because of what the fools amongst us have done?) So, Allah brought them back to life after their death. And in al-Uyun, there is a narration close to this, as previously mentioned. إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ ** (This is nothing but Your trial.) ...meaning: Your test (ابتلاؤك) when You made them hear Your speech, until they became greedy for the vision (الرّؤية). تُضِلُّ بِها مَنْ تَشاءُ وَتَهْدِي مَنْ تَشاءُ أَنْتَ وَلِيُّنا ** (You lead astray whomever You wish by it, and guide whomever You wish. You are our Guardian,) ...meaning: the One Who manages our affairs (القائم بأمرنا). فَاغْفِرْ لَنا وَارْحَمْنا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْغافِرِينَ ** (so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of forgivers.) You forgive the evil deed (السيئة) and replace it with a good deed (الحسنة).

(١٥٦) وَاكْتُبْ لَنا فِي هذِهِ الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةً ** (156) and write for us good in this world (A good livelihood and success in obedience) وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ and in the Hereafter (Paradise) إِنَّا هُدْنا إِلَيْكَ ** Indeed, we have turned to You. (We repented to You, derived from (هادَ) (يَهُودُ) when he returns.) قالَ عَذابِي أُصِيبُ بِهِ مَنْ أَشاءُ ** He said, 'I inflict My punishment on whomever I wish, (the one He wishes to punish) وَرَحْمَتِي وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ but My mercy comprehends all things. (in this world, for there is no Muslim, nor disbeliever, nor obedient person, nor disobedient person, except that he is turning in My blessing; or (it means) in this world and the Hereafter, except that a people will not enter it due to their misguidance.) فَسَأَكْتُبُها Soon I shall write it (So I shall establish it and reveal it in the Hereafter) لِلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ for those who are Godwary, (polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي)) وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكاةَ وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ بِآياتِنا يُؤْمِنُونَ** and pay the zakat, and those who have faith in Our signs, (so they do not disbelieve in any part of them.)

(١٥٧) الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الرَّسُولَ النَّبِيَ. (those who follow the Apostle, the unlettered prophet,)

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): الرَّسُولَ ** (the Apostle) is he to whom the angel appears and speaks to him, and النَّبِيَ ** (the Prophet) is he who sees in his sleep. And sometimes prophethood (النبوّة) and apostleship (الرسالة) are combined in one person.

الْأُمِّيَ ** (the unlettered) is the one attributed to the Mother of the Towns (أم القرى), which is Mecca (مكّة). Thus it is in al-Majma'.

And from al-Baqir (عليه السلام) and al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked why he was named النَّبِيَّ الْأُمِّيَ ** (the unlettered Prophet). He said: "He was attributed to Mecca (مكّة), and that is based on the saying of Allah لِتُنْذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرَى وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا (that you may warn the Mother of the Towns and those around it). And the Mother of the Towns (أم القرى) is Mecca (مكّة), so he was called Ummi (أُمِّي) for that reason."

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): He was asked about that and said: "What do the people say?" It was said: "They claim that He was only named الْأُمِّيَ ** (the unlettered) because He did not know how to write." He said: "They lie, may the curse of Allah be upon them!
