How could that be, when Allah says: هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ رَسُولًا مِنْهُمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ (It is He who sent to the unlettered a messenger from among themselves, to recite to them His signs, and to purify them, and to teach them the Book and wisdom)? How could He teach them what He Himself did not know? By Allah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to read and write in seventy-two, or He said seventy-three, languages. He was only named al-Ummi (الْأُمِّيَ) because He was from the people of Mecca (مكّة), and Mecca (مكّة) is one of the Mothers of the Towns (أمّهات القرى). That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: لِتُنْذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرَى وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا** (that you may warn the Mother of the Towns and those around it)."

الَّذِي يَجِدُونَهُ مَكْتُوباً عِنْدَهُمْ فِي التَّوْراةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ ** (whom they find written down with them in the Torah and the Evangel) with His name and description. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the Jews and the Christians [find] the description and name of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: A Jew said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I have read your description (نعتك) in the Torah: Muhammad ibn Abdullah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, His birthplace is in Mecca (مكّة) and His place of migration is in Taybah (طيبة). He is neither coarse, nor harsh, nor loud (سخاب) (1), nor does He chant obscenity (الفحش) or speak foul language (الخنا). And I bear witness that there is no god but Allah and that you are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. This is my wealth, so judge concerning it according to what Allah has revealed."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the Torah was revealed to Moses, he gave the glad tidings of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said: "The Prophets continued to give glad tidings of Him until Allah sent the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, and he gave the glad tidings of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." And that is His saying: يَجِدُونَهُ ** (whom they find) meaning the Jews and the Christians, مَكْتُوباً ** (written down) meaning the description of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, عِنْدَهُمْ ** (with them) meaning in the Torah and the Evangel. And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informing about Jesus (peace be upon him): وَمُبَشِّراً بِرَسُولٍ يَأْتِي مِنْ بَعْدِي اسْمُهُ أَحْمَدُ.** (and an announcer of a messenger who will come after me, whose name is Ahmad.)

And in it (al-Kafi), narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): That Moses supplicated secretly to his Lord, the Exalted, and He said to him in His secret supplication: "I enjoin you, O Moses, with the counsel of the compassionate and caring One, concerning the son of the Virgin, Jesus son of Mary, and after him, concerning the possessor of the red camel, the good, the pure, the purified. His likeness is in your Book: He is a guardian over all the Books, and He is one who bows (راكع), prostrates (ساجد), desires [good] (راغب), fears [Allah] (راهب). His brethren are the poor, and His helpers are other people. **يَأْمُرُهُمْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهاهُمْ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَيُحِلُّ لَهُمُ الطَّيِّباتِ وَيُحَرِّمُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْخَبائِثَ ** (He enjoins them to follow the right and forbids them from the wrong, and makes lawful to them the good things and forbids them from the foul things,)

It is understood from some narrations that the interpretation (تأويل) of the good things (الطيبات) is taking knowledge from its rightful people (أهله), and the foul things (الخبائث) is the saying of those who oppose.

وَيَضَعُ عَنْهُمْ إِصْرَهُمْ وَالْأَغْلالَ الَّتِي كانَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (and relieves them of their burdens and the shackles that were upon them.) And He lightens for them what they were obligated with of difficult duties (التكاليف الشاقة). The origin of al-isr (الأصر) is heaviness (الثقل). The tradition concerning the removal of the burden (الأصر) from this Ummah has already passed at the end of Surat al-Baqarah. And it was read (وقرء): asarahum (أصارهم).

فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِهِ وَعَزَّرُوهُ ** (So those who believe in him and support him) and honored Him by strengthening Him and defending Him. The origin of al-ta'zir (التعزير) is prevention (المنع). وَنَصَرُوهُ وَاتَّبَعُوا النُّورَ الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ مَعَهُ ** (and help him, and follow the light that has been sent down with him,) It was said: al-Nur (النُّورَ) is the Quran.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): **النُّورَ ** (the light) is Ali (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): النُّورَ ** (the light) in this context is Ali and the Imams (peace be upon them). أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ.** (it is they who are the felicitous.)

(1) In the tradition: "Beware of being sakhkhāb (سخّاب)." It is with an open sīn (س) and a single bā' (ب), a superlative form (صيغة مبالغة) derived from al-sakhab (السّخَبَ) with vocalization, which means loudness of voice. And al-khanā (الخنا) is synonymous with obscenity (الفحش).

(١٥٨) **قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللهِ إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعاً ** (158) Say, 'O mankind! I am Allah’s Apostle to you all.'

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him), he said: A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, are you the one who claims that you are the Messenger of Allah and that revelation is sent down to you just as it was sent down to Moses son of Imran?" The Prophet remained silent for a moment, then he said: "Yes, I am the Master of the Children of Adam, and this is not a boast (ولا فخر). I am the Seal of the Prophets (خاتم النبيينّ), the Imam of the God-wary (إمام المتّقين), and the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds (رسول ربّ العالمين)." They said: "To whom? To the Arabs, or to the non-Arabs (العجم), or to us?" So Allah sent down this Ayah:

**الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحيِي وَيُمِيتُ فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ النَّبِيِّ الْأُمِّيِّ الَّذِي يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَكَلِماتِهِ ** 'to Whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. There is no god except Him. He gives life and brings death. So believe in Allah and His Apostle, the unlettered Prophet, who believes in Allah and His words.'

By this, He means what Allah sent down to him and to the Messengers who preceded him.

وَاتَّبِعُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ. 'and follow him so that you may be guided.'

I say: This means [guidance] to the Divinely-granted knowledge (العلم اللّدنّي) which leads to the love of Allah and His guardianship (ولايته), for it is not attained except through faith (الإِيمان) and following the Prophet and those whom the Prophet commanded to be followed (1).

(١٥٩) وَمِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِ ** (159) And among the people of Moses is a nation who guide by the truth (meaning) by the word of truth, وَبِهِ ** (meaning) and by the truth they act justly among themselves in judgment.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: among the people of Moses (قَوْمِ مُوسى) are the people of Islam (أهل الإسلام).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This nation (الامّة) is a people from beyond China (الصين). Between them and China is a hot valley (واد حارّ) of sand. They have not changed nor altered (their faith). None of them possesses wealth exclusive of his companion. They are rained upon at night and are exposed to the sun (يضحون) (2) during the day, and they cultivate. None of us reaches them, nor do any of them reach us, and they are upon the truth (الحق). He said: And it was said that Gabriel (جبرئيل) went with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Night of al-Mi'raj (المعراج) to them, and he recited to them ten Surahs (سور) of the Quran that were revealed in Mecca (مكّة). So they believed in him and affirmed (his message). He commanded them to remain in their place and to abandon the Sabbath (السبت). He commanded them to perform the prayer (الصلاة) and the Zakat (الزكاة), and no obligatory duty (فريضة) other than these two had yet been revealed. So they did (as commanded). He said: And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated that they will emerge with the Qa'im (قائم) of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them).

And it was narrated that Dhul-Qarnayn (ذا القرنين) saw them and said: "If I were commanded to stay, it would please me to reside among you."

وَقَطَّعْناهُمُ ** (And We divided them) And We made them distinct groups, some separated from others اثْنَتَيْ عَشْرَةَ (into twelve) أَسْباطاً أُمَماً ** (tribes, nations). And the Asbāṭ (الأسباط) are the children of the children, and the Asbāṭ among the descendants of Jacob are equivalent to the tribes (القبائل) among the descendants of Ishmael. وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى إِذِ اسْتَسْقاهُ قَوْمُهُ (And We revealed to Moses when his people asked Him for water) in the wilderness (التيه) أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصاكَ الْحَجَرَ فَانْبَجَسَتْ ** (saying, 'Strike the rock with your staff.' Thereupon it gushed forth). Meaning, so he struck, and it gushed forth. And in its omission [the striking action] there is an indication that he did not hesitate in compliance. مِنْهُ اثْنَتا عَشْرَةَ عَيْناً قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُناسٍ مَشْرَبَهُمْ ** (from it twelve springs. Every group knew its drinking-place). Every tribe (سبط) مَشْرَبَهُمْ وَظَلَّلْنا عَلَيْهِمُ الْغَمامَ ** (And We shaded them with clouds) so that it might protect them from the heat of the sun. وَأَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَنَّ وَالسَّلْوى كُلُوا (and We sent down to them manna and quails. 'Eat') Meaning, and We said to them كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّباتِ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ وَما ظَلَمُونا وَلكِنْ كانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ ** (of the good things We have provided you.' They did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong themselves). Its interpretation has passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

footnote (١) العيّاشي عن الصّادق عليه السلام قال : إذا قام قائم آل محمّد عليهم السلام استخرج من ظهر الكعبة سبعة وعشرين رجلاً خمسة عشر **يَعْدِلُونَ ** وسبعة من أصحاب الكهف ويوشع وصّى موسى ومؤمن آل فرعون وسلمان الفارسيّ وأبا دجّانة الأنصاري ومالك الأشتر. وعن أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام : انّ بني إسرائيل بعد موسى افترقت على أحدى وسبعين فرقة كلها في النار إلّا واحدة فانّ الله يقول **وَمِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ ** فهذه التي تنجو «منه رحمه الله» أقول ولا يبعد أن يكونوا هم المقصودون بالآخرين في الرّواية المتقدّمة. (1) Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: When the Qā’im (القائم) of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them) rises, He will bring forth from the back of the Ka'bah twenty-seven men: fifteen يَعْدِلُونَ ** (who act justly) and seven of the Companions of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف), and Yūsha' (يوشع), the successor (وصّى) of Moses, and the Believer of the Family of Pharaoh (مؤمن آل فرعون), and Salmān al-Fārisī (سلمان الفارسيّ), and Abū Dujānah al-Anṣārī (أبا دجّانة الأنصاري), and Mālik al-Ashtar (مالك الأشتر). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Children of Israel after Moses split into seventy-one sects, all of which are in the Fire except one, for Allah says: وَمِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ ** (And among the people of Moses is a nation who guide by the truth and act justly by it). So, this is the one that will be saved. "From him [the author] (may Allah have mercy on him)." I say: It is not unlikely that they are the ones intended by "the others" (الآخرين) in the preceding narration.

footnote (٢) ضحى ضحواً وضحيّاً اصابته الشّمس وارض مضحاة لا تكاد تغيب عنها الشّمس ويضحون مبني للمفعول امّا من باب نصر أو من باب الأفعال. (2) Ḍaḥā (ضحى), ḍuḥuwwan (ضحواً) and ḍuḥiyyan (وضحيّاً): the sun struck him. And arḍ muḍḥāh (ارض مضحاة) is land where the sun hardly ever sets. And yaḍḥawna (يضحون) is built in the passive voice (مبني للمفعول), either from the pattern of naṣara (نصر) or from the pattern of al-af'āl (الأفعال).

(161) وَإِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمُ اسْكُنُوا هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةَ ** with the implication of اذكر [remember/mention], and الْقَرْيَةَ ** [refers to] بيت المقدس وَكُلُوا مِنْها حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ وَقُولُوا حِطَّةٌ وَادْخُلُوا الْبابَ سُجَّداً نَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطِيئاتِكُمْ سَنَزِيدُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (And when it was said to them, 'Settle in this town, and eat thereof wherever you wish, and say, "Relieve us of our burdens," and enter the gate prostrating. We will forgive you your iniquities, and soon We shall enhance the virtuous.')

(162) فَبَدَّلَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ قَوْلاً غَيْرَ الَّذِي قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِجْزاً مِنَ السَّماءِ بِما كانُوا يَظْلِمُونَ ** (But the wrongdoers among them changed the saying with one other than that which they had been told. So We sent down on them a plague from the sky because of the wrongs they used to commit.) Its interpretation passed therein. And it was read تغفر (with the tāʾ and built for the passive voice (البناء للمفعول)), and خطيئتكم (your sin) with the singular form (الْتوحيد) and خطاياكم (your sins).

(١٦٣) وَسْئَلْهُمْ ** واسأل اليهود وهو سؤال تقريع بقديم كفرهم وتجاوزهم حدود الله عَنِ الْقَرْيَةِ ** عن خبرها وما وقع بأهلها الَّتِي كانَتْ حاضِرَةَ الْبَحْرِ ** قريبة منه إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِي السَّبْتِ ** يتجاوزون حدود الله تعالى بالصيد يوم السبت وقد نهوا عنه إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ ** يوم تعظيمهم أمر يوم السبت مصدر سبت اليهود إذا عظمت سبتها بالتجرد للعبادة شُرَّعاً ** ظاهرة على وجه الماء من شرع عليه إذا دنا منه وأشرف وَيَوْمَ لا يَسْبِتُونَ لا تَأْتِيهِمْ كَذلِكَ نَبْلُوهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ. (163) Ask them about the town that was situated by the sea, when they violated the Sabbath, when their fish came to them on the day of their Sabbath, visible on the surface, and did not come to them on the day they did not observe the Sabbath. Thus did We test them because of the transgressions they used to commit.

وَسْئَلْهُمْ ** (And ask them) means: Ask the Jews, and this is a question of reprimand (تقريع) regarding their ancient disbelief and their transgression of the limits (حدود) of Allah. عَنِ الْقَرْيَةِ ** (about the town) means: about its story (خبرها) and what befell its people. الَّتِي كانَتْ حاضِرَةَ الْبَحْرِ ** (that was situated by the sea) means: close to it. إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِي السَّبْتِ ** (when they violated the Sabbath) means: they transgressed the limits (حدود) of Allah, the Exalted, by hunting on the day of the Sabbath, though they had been forbidden from it. إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ ** (when their fish came to them on the day of their Sabbath) means: the day they honored the matter of the day of the Sabbath. The verbal noun (مصدر) is سَبَتَ (to observe the Sabbath) for the Jews when they honored their Sabbath by devoting themselves purely to worship (بالتجرد للعبادة). شُرَّعاً ** (visible on the surface) means: appearing (ظاهرة) on the surface of the water, derived from شَرَعَ عَلَيْهِ (he came upon it) when he drew near to it and overlooked it (أَشْرَفَ). وَيَوْمَ لا يَسْبِتُونَ لا تَأْتِيهِمْ كَذلِكَ نَبْلُوهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ.

(١٦٤) وَإِذْ قالَتْ أُمَّةٌ مِنْهُمْ (And when a community among them said) a group from the people of the town (القرية) لِمَ تَعِظُونَ قَوْماً اللهُ مُهْلِكُهُمْ (Why do you admonish a people whom Allah is going to destroy) He Who will cut them off (مخترمهم) أَوْ مُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً (or punish them with a severe punishment) due to their persistence in disobedience. قالُوا مَعْذِرَةً (They said: 'As an excuse') And معذرة was read with the raf' (الرفع) إِلى رَبِّكُمْ (to your Lord) meaning, our admonition is an excuse to Allah so that we are not attributed with negligence in forbidding the evil (النهي عن المنكر). وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (and so that they may be Godwary) since despair does not occur except with annihilation.

وَإِذْ قَالَتْ أُمَّةٌ مِّنْهُمْ لِمَ تَعِظُونَ قَوْمًا اللَّهُ مُهْلِكُهُمْ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا قَالُوا مَعْذِرَةً إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (164) And when a community among them said, 'Why do you admonish a people whom Allah is going to destroy or punish them with a severe punishment?' They said, 'As an excuse to your Lord, and so that they may be Godwary.'

(١٦٥) فَلَمَّا نَسُوا ما ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ أَنْجَيْنَا الَّذِينَ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ السُّوءِ وَأَخَذْنَا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا بِعَذابٍ بَئِيسٍ بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ (165) So when they forgot what they had been reminded of, We delivered those who forbade the evil, and seized those who did wrong with a terrible punishment because of the transgressions they used to commit.

فَلَمَّا نَسُوا ** They abandoned, the abandonment of one who forgets. ما ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ ** what the admonishers reminded them of. أَنْجَيْنَا الَّذِينَ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ السُّوءِ وَأَخَذْنَا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا بِعَذابٍ بَئِيسٍ ** severe, derived from بَؤُسَ يَبْؤُس بأساً when it becomes intense. And it was recited on the pattern of ضيغم, and with a kasrah on the باء and a sukūn on the همزة, and with a kasrah on it, and by changing the همزة into a باء. بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ because of their transgression (فسق).

(166) When they rebelled against what they had been forbidden (7:166) they became arrogant regarding the prohibition, or regarding the abandonment of what they were forbidden. This is similar to the Almighty's saying: And they rebelled against the command of their Lord. We said to them, 'Be apes, abject and rejected.' (7:77) meaning: expelled, removed from all good, like His saying: All that We say to a thing, when We will it, is that We say to it 'Be,' and it is. (16:40)

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from Imam (peace be upon him): Regarding Surat al-Baqarah at His saying: And certainly you know those of you who violated the Sabbath, whereupon We said to them, 'Be apes, abject and rejected.' (2:65) Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both) said: These were a people who resided on the shore of a sea. Allah and His Prophets forbade them from fishing on the Sabbath (Yawm al-Sabt). So, they resorted to a trick (حيلة) to make lawful for themselves what Allah had forbidden. They dug trenches (أخاديد) (1) and made paths leading to pools (حياض) where it was easy for the large fish (الحيتان) to enter through those paths, but it was not easy for them to exit when they intended to return. The large fish came on the Sabbath, swimming securely, and entered the trenches and ended up in the pools and ponds (الغُدران). When it was evening, they intended to return from them to the deep sea (اللّجُجَ) to be safe from their hunter, but they attempted to return and were unable. They remained the night in a place where they could be taken without actual hunting, due to their ease of access therein and their inability to resist because the place prevented them. They would take them on Sunday and say, "We did not fish on the Sabbath; rather, we fished on Sunday." The enemies of Allah lied; rather, they were taking them by means of their trenches (أخاديد) which they had made on the Sabbath, until their wealth and riches increased greatly from this, and they enjoyed women and others due to the expansion of their means through it.

There were over eighty thousand people in the city. Seventy thousand of them did this, and the rest denounced them, as Allah recounted: And ask them about the town that was situated by the sea (7:163) the Ayah. That was because a group among them admonished them, restrained them, warned them of Allah's punishment, and cautioned them against His vengeance and the severity of His might. Those who were admonished replied to those who admonished them: "Why do you admonish a people whom Allah is going to destroy due to their sins with utter annihilation (هلاك الاصطلام) (2) or punish with a severe punishment?" (7:164) Those who said this replied to them: "A plea of excuse to your Lord (مَعْذِرَةً إِلى رَبِّكُمْ)." This saying of ours to them is a plea of excuse to your Lord (7:164), since we have been obligated with enjoining the good and forbidding the evil. So, we forbid the evil so that our Lord may know our opposition to them and our dislike for their action. They also said: and so that they may be Godwary (7:164). And we admonish them also so that the admonitions might be effective in them, and they may become Godwary of this destructive sin (الموبقة) (1) and beware of its punishment.

The Almighty Allah said: When they rebelled (7:166) meaning they deviated, turned away, and became arrogant about accepting the restraint against what they had been forbidden, We said to them, 'Be apes, abject and rejected.' (7:166) meaning removed from good, hated. When the ten thousand and some saw that the seventy thousand did not accept their admonitions and were not afraid despite their warnings and cautions, they isolated themselves from them, moving to another village, or they moved to a part of their village, and said: "We dislike that the punishment of Allah should descend upon them while we are among them." So, they spent a night, and Allah transformed all of them into apes. The gate of the city remained closed; no one exited it and no one entered it. The people of the surrounding villages heard about this, so they headed towards them and climbed the walls of the town and looked down upon them. They found that all of them, their men and their women, were apes, surging one upon the other. These observers recognized their acquaintances, relatives, and associates. The observer would say to some of them, "You are so-and-so man, and you are so-and-so woman," and the ape's eye would tear up, and it would signal with its head or mouth, "Yes" or "No." They remained like that for three days. Then the Almighty Allah sent rain and wind, which swept them into the sea. No transformed creature (مسخ) remained after three days. Indeed, those you see of these creatures in their forms are merely their likenesses, not the actual individuals, nor are they of their progeny.

In al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: We found in the book of Ali (peace be upon him): That a people from the inhabitants of Aylah (أيْلَة) were from the people of Thamud, and the large fish (الحيتان) used to come to them specifically on the Sabbath so that Allah might test their obedience in that matter. They appeared to them on their Sabbath in their gathering places, in front of their doors, in their rivers and their irrigation channels (سواقيهم). They rushed to them and began to hunt them. They persisted in this for as long as Allah willed. The rabbis (الأحبار) did not forbid them from it, nor did the scholars (العلماء) prevent them from hunting them. Then Satan inspired a group of them, saying: "You were only forbidden from eating them on the Sabbath, but you were not forbidden from hunting them. So, hunt them on the Sabbath and eat them on the other days." A group of them said, "Now we will hunt them," and they rebelled (عتت). Another group separated from them to the right and said: "We forbid you from the punishment of Allah, lest you expose yourselves by disobeying His command." And a group separated from them to the left and remained silent. They were not admonished, so they said to the group that admonished them: "Why do you admonish a people whom Allah is going to destroy or punish with a severe punishment?" (7:164) The group that admonished them said: A plea of excuse to your Lord, and so that they may be Godwary. (7:164)

He said: Then the Almighty Allah said: So when they forgot what they had been reminded of (7:165) meaning, when they abandoned what they had been admonished about, they persisted in the sin. The group that admonished them said: "No, by Allah, we will not associate with you nor spend the night with you tonight in this city of yours where you have disobeyed Allah, fearing that the affliction may descend upon you and encompass us with you." He said: So they left the city, fearing that the affliction might strike them, and they settled near the city and spent the night under the sky. When the friends of Allah (أولياء الله), who were obedient to the command of the Almighty Allah, awoke (1), they went early to see the condition of the people of disobedience. They came to the gate of the city, and behold, it was solid and closed. They knocked on it, but they received no answer and heard no sound from anyone inside. So, they placed a ladder against the wall of the city, and then they sent one of their men up. He looked down upon the city and saw the people as apes, howling. The man said to his companions: "O people, by Allah, I see a wonder!" They asked: "What do you see?" He said: "I see the people have become apes, howling, and they have tails." So, they broke the gate and entered the city. He said: The apes recognized their relatives among the humans, but the humans did not recognize their relatives among the apes. The people said to the apes: "Did we not forbid you?"

He said: Then Ali (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, Who split the seed and created the soul (النسمة), I certainly know their lineages among this Ummah. They do not deny or change, but they abandoned what they were commanded, so they dispersed. And Allah has said: So away with the wrongdoing people! Then Allah said: We delivered those who forbade the evil, and seized those who were wrongdoers with a dire punishment because of the transgressions they used to commit. (7:165)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: They were three groups: A group that obeyed the command (ائتمروا) and enjoined (the good), and they were saved. A group that obeyed the command (ائتمروا) but did not enjoin (the good), and they were transformed into ants (ذرّاً). And a group that neither obeyed the command (ائتمروا) nor enjoined (the good), and they perished. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): a narration with the same meaning. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The two groups perished, and the third group was saved.

(1) The trench (الأخدود) is a long slit in the earth; its plural is (أخاديد). (وخدّ الأرض) (from the category of (مدّ)) means He split it.

(2) (الاصطلام) is eradication (الاستيصال). It is the ifti'al form of (الصّلم), which is the cutting that eradicates. (وصلمت الأذن) (from the category of (ضرب)) means I eradicated it by cutting.

(1) (وَبَق) (like (وعد) and (وجل) and (ورث)) and (وبوقاً) and (موبقاً) means perished, like (استوبق). And (كمجلس) is the place of perishing.

(١٦٧) **وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكَ ** (167) And when your Lord proclaimed

This is the تَفَعَّلَ form derived from الإِيذان (announcement) or meaning determination (العزم) and oath (الإِقسام). Its meaning is: And remember when He informed or determined, رَبُّكَ ** (your Lord), and swore: لَيَبْعَثَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (He will surely send against them)—meaning, He will surely grant dominance over the Jews—إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ مَنْ يَسُومُهُمْ ** (until the Day of Resurrection, those who will inflict upon them)—meaning, will burden them/impose upon them—سُوءَ الْعَذابِ ** (a terrible punishment)—meaning, its severity through killing, humiliation, and the imposition of the poll-tax (الجزية).

It was said that Allah sent against them, after Sulayman (Solomon), Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصر), who destroyed their homes, killed their fighters, and took their women and children captive, and imposed the poll-tax (الجزية) upon those who remained among them. They used to pay it to the Magians (المجوس) until Allah sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so He did what He did (i.e., conquered them) and imposed the poll-tax (الجزية) upon them, and it will remain imposed until the end of time.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: that those meant by this are the community (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

**إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَسَرِيعُ الْعِقابِ ** Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution

He punished them in this world. وَإِنَّهُ لَغَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (and He is indeed All-forgiving, All-merciful) for whoever repents and believes.

footnote (١) غدا غدوّا من باب قعد ذهب غدوة وجمع الغدوة غدى كمدية ومدى هذا أصله ثمّ كثر حتّى استعمل في الذهاب والانطلاق أي وقت كان. (1) غدا (ghadā) takes the verbal noun غُدُوًّا (ghudūwwan) from the pattern of قَعَدَ (qa‘ada), meaning he went in the morning (غُدْوَة). The plural of الغُدْوَة (al-ghudwah) is غُدًى (ghudan), like مُدْيَة (mudyah) and مُدًى (mudan). This is its origin, then it became widespread until it was used for going (الذهاب) and setting off (الانطلاق) at any time.

(١٦٨) وَقَطَّعْناهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أُمَماً ** (168) And We dispersed them in the land as communities. And We dispersed them therein such that scarcely a land is devoid of a group of them. مِنْهُمُ الصَّالِحُونَ ** Among them are the righteous, They are those who believed in Allah and His Messenger. وَمِنْهُمْ دُونَ ذلِكَ and among them are those who are otherwise. people below that, meaning: those who are degraded (مُنْحَطُّونَ) from righteousness, and they are their disbelievers (كُفَّرَتُهُمْ) and their transgressors (فَسَقَتُهُمْ). وَبَلَوْناهُمْ بِالْحَسَناتِ وَالسَّيِّئاتِ ** We tested them with good times and bad times, with blessings (النِّعَم), misfortunes (النِّقَم), transformation (المَسْخ), and trials (المِحَن). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ so that they may return. so that they might become aware (يَتَنَبَّهُونَ) and repent (فَيُنِيبُونَ).

(١٦٩) فَخَلَفَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ ** A bad successor (بدل سوء). The word (خَلْف) with the sukūn (بالتسكين) is common for evil, and with the ḥarakah (بالتحريك) (i.e., خَلَف) is common for good. It is said that those intended by this are the ones who lived during the era of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم وَرِثُوا الْكِتابَ ** The Torah from their predecessors يَأْخُذُونَ عَرَضَ هذَا الْأَدْنى ** The debris (حطام) of this lowest thing, meaning the world (الدنيا). It is said that this refers to what they used to take as bribes (الرشاء) in judgment and for distorting the words (تحريف الكلم) to make things easy for the common people (العامّة) وَيَقُولُونَ سَيُغْفَرُ لَنا ** Allah will not hold us accountable for that and will overlook it. وَإِنْ يَأْتِهِمْ عَرَضٌ مِثْلُهُ يَأْخُذُوهُ Meaning, they hope for forgiveness while they are persistent (in sin) (مصرون) and return to the like of their action, not repenting from it أَلَمْ يُؤْخَذْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِيثاقُ الْكِتابِ ** The covenant (الميثاق) in the Torah أَنْ لا يَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَ ** That they should not lie against Allah, nor attribute to Him anything except what He has revealed **وَدَرَسُوا ما فِيهِ ** And they read what is in it, so they are mindful of that.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah has singled out His servants with two verses (بآيتين) (1) from His Book: أَنْ لا يَقُولُوا ** until they know, and that they should not reject what they do not know. And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: أَلَمْ يُؤْخَذْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِيثاقُ الْكِتابِ أَنْ لا يَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَ ** And He said: بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِما لَمْ يُحِيطُوا بِعِلْمِهِ. (Rather, they denied what they did not comprehend by knowledge.)

And al-Ayyāshī narrated from him and from al-Kāẓim (peace be upon them both) what is close to this.

وَالدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ** The prohibitions (محارم) of Allah from what these people take أَفَلا يَعْقِلُونَ ** So that they may know that. And it was recited in the second person address (بالخطاب).

footnote (١) It is said that He means His servants who are from the People of the Book (*أهل الكتاب*), and the statement implies that those besides them are not attributed to Him by servitude (*بالعبودية*). "With two verses (*بآيتين*)" means their content, otherwise the verses concerning this are more than two, such as His saying, the Almighty: **وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآياتِهِ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَحْكُمْ بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكافِرُونَ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ* فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ*** and other such verses. "And that they should not reject what they do not know" means that they should not deny it, but rather entrust the knowledge of it to the one who stated it. For just as affirming something requires its conceptualization (*تصوره*) in affirmation (*إثباتاً*), so too is it dependent upon it in negation (*نفياً*). This is extremely clear, but most people do not know.

(١٧٠) **وَالَّذِينَ يُمَسِّكُونَ بِالْكِتابِ وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ إِنَّا لا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُصْلِحِينَ ** (7:170) And those who hold fast to the Book and maintain the prayer—indeed We do not waste the reward of the reformers.

Either it is a ʿaṭf (conjunction) upon الَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ** (those who are Godwary), and what lies between them is an interjection (اِعْتِرَاض). Or it is a new beginning (اِسْتِئْنَاف), and the explicit noun (الظاهر) is placed in the position of the implicit pronoun (المضمر), because it is in its meaning, and to draw attention to the fact that reformation (الإِصْلَاح) prevents loss (الإِضَاعَة). And it was recited (قُرِئَ) as يَمْسِكُونَ (with takhfīf—lightening) from the root الإِمْسَاك (holding fast).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and their followers (أَشْيَاعِهِمْ).

(١٧١) وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُ واقِعٌ بِهِمْ (171) And when We shook the mountain over them, as if it were a canopy, and they thought that it was about to fall on them,

And when (1) We shook the mountain (نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ): We uprooted it and raised it, and its origin (أصله) is pulling (الجذب). over them, as if it were a canopy (كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ): a roof (سقيفة), and it is everything that casts a shade (أظلّ). and they thought (وَظَنُّوا): and they became certain (تيقنوا). that it was about to fall on them (أَنَّهُ واقِعٌ بِهِمْ): falling upon them, because the mountain does not remain suspended in the air, and because they were being threatened with it. It was said that al-ẓann (الظنّ) was only used because its object (متعلقة) had not yet occurred.

خُذُوا ما آتَيْناكُمْ بِقُوَّةٍ (Take hold of what We have given you with power)

Take hold (2) of what We have given you with power (ما آتَيْناكُمْ بِقُوَّةٍ): with resolve (عزم) from your hearts and your bodies. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse: "Is it strength (قوّة) in the bodies (الأبدان) or strength (قوّة) in the hearts (القلوب)?" He said: "In both of them together."

وَاذْكُرُوا ما فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (and remember what is in it, so that you may be Godwary.)

and remember what is in it (وَاذْكُرُوا ما فِيهِ): of commands (الأوامر) and prohibitions (النّواهي). so that you may be Godwary (لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ).

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah sent down the Torah upon the Children of Israel, they did not accept it. So Allah raised Mount Tur Sinai over them. Moses said to them: "If you do not accept it, the mountain will fall upon you." So they accepted it and bowed their heads. Its interpretation has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah in a more detailed manner than this.

(١٧٢) **وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ بَنِي آدَمَ مِنْ ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ ** (172) When your Lord took from the children of Adam, from their backs, their descendants,

And it was read (وقرئ) as ذرّياتهم (their descendants, plural). He brought forth their progeny from their loins, according to how they reproduce, generation after generation. Meaning, He scattered their realities (حقائقهم) before His knowledge, and He made those realities speak through the spearheads (بأسنة) of the receptivities (قابليّات) of their essences (جواهرها) and the tongues (ألسن) of the preparedness (استعدادات) of their selves (ذواتها).

**وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قالُوا بَلى شَهِدْنا ** and made them bear witness over themselves: 'Am I not your Lord?' They said, 'Yes, we bear witness!'

That is, He established for them the proofs of His Lordship (ربوبيته) and instilled in their intellects that which calls them to affirm it, until they became like those who bear witness, following the method of similitude (التمثيل). Similar to this is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic:

**إِنَّما قَوْلُنا لِشَيْءٍ إِذا أَرَدْناهُ أَنْ نَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** Our only word to a thing, when We will it, is to say to it, 'Be,' and it is.

And His saying, the Glorious and Exalted:

**فَقالَ لَها وَلِلْأَرْضِ ائْتِيا طَوْعاً أَوْ كَرْهاً قالَتا أَتَيْنا طائِعِينَ ** Then He said to it and to the earth, 'Come, willingly or unwillingly.' They said, 'We come willingly.'

And it is known that there is no speech (قول) there; rather, it is a similitude (تمثيل) and a depiction (تصوير) of the meaning. And that was when their souls were in the intellectual loins (أصلاب) of their fathers and their original sources (معادنهم الأصلية). Meaning, He witnessed them while they were subtle entities (دقايق) within those realities. And He expressed those fathers using the term "backs" (الظهور) because every one of them is a manifestation (ظهر) or a locus of manifestation (مظهر) for a group of souls, or apparent to Him because He is an intellectual, luminous form (صورة عقلية نوريّة) manifest by its own essence (بذاتها).

وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (and made them bear witness over themselves): meaning, He granted them, in that intellectual perceptive existence (النشأة الإِدراكية العقليّة), the witnessing (شهود) of their intellectual selves (ذواتهم العقليّة) and their luminous identities (هوّياتهم النورية). Thus, by means of those intellectual powers (القوى العقليّة), they heard the address: أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ** ('Am I not your Lord?'), just as they hear the address in the abode of this world (دار الدنيا) through these physical powers (القوى البدنية).

And قالُوا ** (They said) with the tongues of those intellects: بَلى ** ('Yes'), You are our Lord, Who granted us a sacred, Divine existence (وجوداً قدسيّاً ربّانياً). We heard Your speech and responded to Your address.

It is also not far-fetched that this utterance (النطق) occurred with the celestial tongue (اللسان الملكوتي) in the World of Similitudes (عالم المثالي), which is below the World of Intellect (عالم العقل), for everything has a celestial realm (ملكوت) in that world, as He, the Glorified, indicated with His saying:

**فَسُبْحانَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** So immaculate is He in whose hand is the dominion of all things,

And the celestial realm (الملكوت) is the inner reality (باطن) of the dominion (الملك), and it is all life. And every atom (ذرة) has a celestial tongue (لسان ملكوتي) speaking with glorification (التسبيح), magnification (التمجيد), affirmation of unity (التوحيد), and praise (التحميد). And with this tongue, the pebbles spoke in the palm of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And with it, the earth will speak on the Day of Resurrection: يَوْمَئِذٍ تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبارَها (on that day she will relate her news). And with it, the limbs will speak: أَنْطَقَنَا اللهُ الَّذِي أَنْطَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ (Allah, who makes everything speak, has made us speak).

أَنْ تَقُولُوا (lest you should say) (meaning, out of dislike/fear that) أَنْ تَقُولُوا ** (lest you should say) (And it was read (وقرئ) with the yā’ (بالياء), meaning يَقُولُوا [they should say]) يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هذا غافِلِينَ** (on the Day of Resurrection, 'We were indeed unaware of this,'), meaning, we were not alerted to it.

footnote (١) Its meaning is: And remember, O Muhammad, when We uprooted the mountain from its base and raised it above the Children of Israel. The army of Moses was a *farsakh* (*فرسخ*) by a *farsakh*, and Allah raised the mountain above all of them.

footnote (٢) The Almighty's saying: **خُذُوا ما آتَيْناكُمْ بِقُوَّةٍ ** (Take what We have given you with power) meaning, take what We have obligated upon you from the rulings (*أحكام*) and duties (*فرائضه*) of Our Book, so accept it with diligence (*بجدّ*) and effort (*اجتهاد*) from yourselves at all times, without negligence (*تقصير*) or slackness (*توان*).

(١٧٣) أَوْ تَقُولُوا إِنَّما أَشْرَكَ آباؤُنا مِنْ قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا ذُرِّيَّةً مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ** (Or lest you should say, 'Our fathers were polytheists before, and we were a progeny after them.') so we followed their example, because blind following (التقليد), when the proof (الحجّة) is established and one is able to know it, is not a valid excuse. أَفَتُهْلِكُنا بِما فَعَلَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ ** (Will You destroy us because of what the falsifiers did?') meaning their fathers, who were the falsifiers (المبطلون) by establishing polytheism (الشرك).

(174) وَكَذلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (174) And thus do We elaborate the signs, so that they may return — meaning, from blind imitation (التقليد) and following falsehood (اتباع الباطل).

In al-Kafi, al-Tawhid, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "He brought forth the progeny of Adam from his loins until the Day of Resurrection, and they came forth like tiny particles (الذّرّ). Then He introduced Himself to them and showed them His creation (صنعه). Were it not for that, no one would have known his Lord."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (al-Baqir عليه السلام), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said—while His father was listening: "My father narrated to me that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, took a handful of the soil (تراب التّربة) from which Adam was created, and poured upon it sweet, flowing water (الماء العذب الفرات). Then He left it for forty mornings. Then He poured upon it salty and bitter water (المالح والأجاج) (1), and left it for forty mornings. When the clay (الطينة) fermented, He took it and rubbed it (عركها) (2) with a severe rubbing (عركاً شديداً). They came forth like tiny particles (الذّرّ) from His right and His left. He commanded all of them to enter the Fire. The Companions of the Right entered, and it became cool and safe for them, but the Companions of the Left refused to enter it."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how they responded while they were tiny particles (ذرّ). He said: "He placed within them that by which, when He questioned them, they responded to Him." And al-Ayyashi added: "Meaning, in the Covenant (الميثاق)."

I say: This is precisely what We stated: that He, the Mighty and Majestic, installed in their intellects (عقولهم) that which calls them to affirmation (الإِقرار).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): When Allah desired to create creation, He scattered them before Him and said to them: "Who is your Lord?" The first to speak were the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and the Imams (peace be upon them). They said: "You are our Lord." So He entrusted them with knowledge (العلم) and religion (الدين). Then He said to the angels: "These are the bearers of My religion and My knowledge, and My trustees among My creation, and they are the ones who will be questioned." Then He said to the Children of Adam: "Affirm the Lordship (الرّبوبيّة) of Allah and the Wilayah (الولاية) and obedience (الطاعة) of these individuals (النفر)." They said: "Yes, our Lord, we affirm." Then Allah said to the angels: "Bear witness." So the angels said: شَهِدْنا (We bear witness) — lest you say tomorrow: إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هذا غافِلِينَ أَوْ تَقُولُوا (‘Indeed we were unaware of this,’ or say) — the Ayah.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He was asked whether this was a direct witnessing (معاينة). He said: "Yes. Thus, the recognition (المعرفة) was established, but they forgot the standing (الموقف), though they will remember it. Were it not for that, no one would know who his Creator (خالقه) and Sustainer (رازقه) is. Among them were those who affirmed with their tongues while they were tiny particles (الذرّ) but did not believe in their hearts. So Allah said: فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ (So they would not believe in what they had denied before.)

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him, from His father (peace be upon them both): [A narration] similar in meaning up to His saying: "and Sustainer (ورازقه)." And in another narration by him (al-Ayyashi): "And some of them concealed the opposite of what they manifested." And concerning the meaning of these narrations, there are many reports, some of which are more detailed than what has been mentioned. We have already explained some of them in a manner that leaves nothing further to be desired in Our book, al-Wafi.

(175) وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ الَّذِي آتَيْناهُ آياتِنا ** (And recount to them the account of him to whom We gave Our signs) Al-Qummi (narrates): It was revealed concerning Bal'am ibn Ba'ura, who was from the Children of Israel and was given knowledge of some of the Books of Allah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The origin of it is Bal'am, then Allah set him forth as an example for every person among the people of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة) who prefers his own desire over the guidance of Allah.

And al-Ayyashi narrates from him (peace be upon him): The example of al-Mughirah ibn Sa'id is like the example of Bal'am, who was given the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم), concerning whom Allah said: آتَيْناهُ آياتِنا ** (We gave Our signs) (the rest of) the Ayah. فَانْسَلَخَ مِنْها ** (whereupon he slipped away from them) by disbelieving in them and casting them behind his back. فَأَتْبَعَهُ الشَّيْطانُ ** (Then Satan pursued him) so Satan caught up with him, overtook him, and became a companion (قريناً) to him. فَكانَ مِنَ الْغاوِينَ ** (and he became one of the perverse) meaning, one of the misguided (الضالين).

Al-Qummi narrates from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Bal'am ibn Ba'ura was given the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم), and he would supplicate with it and He would respond to him. Then he inclined toward Pharaoh. When Pharaoh passed by in pursuit of Moses and his companions, Pharaoh said to Bal'am: "Supplicate to Allah against Moses and his companions so that He may detain them for us." So he mounted his she-donkey to proceed in pursuit of Moses, but his she-donkey refused him. He began to strike her, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made her speak. She said: "Woe unto you! Why are you striking me? Do you want me to come with you so that you may supplicate against the Prophet of Allah and a believing people?" He continued to strike her until he killed her, and the Name (الاسم) slipped away from his tongue. This is His Almighty saying: فَانْسَلَخَ مِنْها ** (whereupon he slipped away from them) (the rest of) the Ayah.

(١٧٦) وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَرَفَعْنَاهُ بِهَا وَلَكِنَّهُ أَخْلَدَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ وَاتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ (Had We wished, We would have raised him by their means, but he clung to the earth and followed his [own] desires.) to the stations of the righteous among the scholars بِهَا by those signs (الآيات) and adherence to them وَلَكِنَّهُ أَخْلَدَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ he inclined towards the world (الدنيا) وَاتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ in preferring the world (الدنيا), seeking the pleasure of his people, and turning away from what the signs (الآيات) necessitated, so We abased him. فَمَثَلُهُ كَمَثَلِ الْكَلْبِ إِنْ تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْهِ يَلْهَثْ أَوْ تَتْرُكْهُ يَلْهَثْ (So his parable is that of a dog: if you attack it, it lolls its tongue, and if you leave it alone, it lolls its tongue.) so his description is like the description of a dog in its most abject states إِنْ تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْهِ by scolding and driving away—from the attack (الحملة), not from carrying (الحمل) يَلْهَثْ it puts out its tongue due to severe panting أَوْ تَتْرُكْهُ يَلْهَثْ it is perpetually panting, unlike the rest of the animals, for they pant only if they are agitated and moved, otherwise they do not pant. And the meaning is that if you admonish him, he is astray, and if you do not admonish him, he is astray; he is astray in every state. ذَلِكَ مَثَلُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا فَاقْصُصِ الْقَصَصَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (That is the parable of the people who deny Our signs. So recount the narratives, so that they may reflect.) the aforementioned لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ so they may take heed and beware of a consequence like his.

(177) ساءَ مَثَلاً الْقَوْمُ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا ** (177) Evil is the case of the people who deny Our signs. meaning, their case (مثلهم). وَأَنْفُسَهُمْ كانُوا يَظْلِمُونَ ** and they used to wrong their own souls. and not others. (178) مَنْ يَهْدِ اللهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِي وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** (178) Whomever Allah guides, it is he who is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray—it is they who are the losers. It is said that the singular form in the first part and the plural form in the second part is due to considering the wording (اللفظ) and the meaning (المعنى), serving as an alert (تنبيه) that the rightly guided ones (المهتدين) are like a single entity (كواحد) due to the unity of their path (طريقتهم), unlike the misguided ones (الضّالين).

(179) وَلَقَدْ ذَرَأْنا We created (خلقنا) لِجَهَنَّمَ كَثِيراً مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ بِها وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ لا يُبْصِرُونَ بِها وَلَهُمْ آذانٌ لا يَسْمَعُونَ بِها. (179) Certainly We have created for hell many of the jinn and humans: they have hearts with which they do not understand, and they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ بِها ** (They have hearts with which they do not understand). He says: Allah has sealed (طبع) them, so they do not reason (تعقل). وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ (And they have eyes) upon which there is a covering (غطاء) from guidance (الهدى), لا يُبْصِرُونَ بِها (with which they do not see). وَلَهُمْ آذانٌ لا يَسْمَعُونَ بِها (And they have ears with which they do not hear). He placed deafness (وقراً) in their ears, so they did not hear the guidance (الهدى). أُولئِكَ كَالْأَنْعامِ (Those are like cattle) in the lack of understanding (الفقه) and sight (الإِبصار) for reflection (لِلاعتبار) and listening (الاستماع) for contemplation (للتّدبّر), and in that their senses (مشاعرهم) and faculties (قواهم) are directed toward the means of livelihood (أسباب التعيش) and restricted to them. بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُ (Rather, they are more astray), for [cattle] perceive what they are able to perceive of benefits (المنافع) and harms (المضار) and strive (تجتهد) to attract the former and repel the latter with the utmost effort (غاية جهدها). But they [the humans] are not like that; rather, most of them know that they are being obstinate (معاند) yet they proceed toward the Fire. أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْغافِلُونَ ** (Those are the heedless ones), meaning: those who are complete (الكاملون) in heedlessness (الغفلة).

In al-Ilal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: Allah installed intellect (عقلاً) in the angels without desire (شهوة), and He installed desire (شهوة) in the beasts (البهائم) without intellect (عقل), and He installed both of them (كلتيهما) in the children of Adam (بني آدم). So, whoever's intellect overcomes his desire, he is better than the angels. And whoever's desire overcomes his intellect, he is worse than the beasts.

(١٨٠) وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى ** (180) To Allah belong the Best Names. which are the best names because they encompass meanings that are the best of meanings. Al-Qummi said: الرحمن الرحيم (the All-beneficent, the All-merciful). فَادْعُوهُ بِها ** So supplicate Him by them. So name Him with those names.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Name (الاسم), and He said: "It is a description (صفة) of the described (موصوف)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "If a hardship (شدّة) descends upon you, seek aid through us upon Allah, and this is the saying of Allah: وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى فَادْعُوهُ بِها. (To Allah belong the Best Names. So supplicate Him by them.)"

He said: Abu Abd Allah (peace be upon him) said: "We, by Allah, are الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى (the Best Names), through Whom He does not accept obedience from anyone except through our recognition (معرفتنا)." He said: "So supplicate Him by them." The complete verification (تحقيق) of the meaning of the Name (الاسم) has already passed in the beginning of Surat al-Baqarah. وَذَرُوا الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي أَسْمائِهِ ** And leave those who blaspheme His Names. And it was recited (وقرء) with the opening (فتح) of the ya (الياء) and the ha (الحاء) (يَلْحَدُونَ), and it is the same in meaning (بمعناه). That is: And leave those who deviate (يعدلون) with His Names from what they truly are, so they name their idols (أصنامهم) with them, or they describe Him with what is not befitting for Him, or they name Him with what is not permissible to name Him with.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The Creator (الخالق) is not described except by what He described Himself with. And how can He be described Whom the senses (الحواسّ) are unable to perceive, Whom the imaginations (الأوهام) are unable to attain, Whom the thoughts (الخطرات) are unable to define, and Whom the sights (الأبصار) are unable to encompass? He is exalted far above what the describers (الواصفون) describe Him with, and He is highly exalted far above what the qualifiers (الناعتون) qualify Him with—the hadith.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a long hadith: And to Him belong الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى (the Best Names), by which none other than Him is named. And these are the ones He described in the Book, so He said: فَادْعُوهُ بِها وَذَرُوا الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي أَسْمائِهِ (So supplicate Him by them. And leave those who blaspheme His Names) out of ignorance (جهلاً) without knowledge. So, he who blasphemes His Names without knowledge commits shirk (يشرك) while he does not know, and he disbelieves in Him while he thinks that he is doing good. And for this reason, He said: وَما يُؤْمِنُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِاللهِ إِلَّا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ ** (106) Most of them do not believe in Allah except while they are polytheists. So, they are الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي أَسْمائِهِ** (those who blaspheme His Names) without knowledge, placing them in other than their proper places. سَيُجْزَوْنَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ. (They will be requited for what they used to do.)

(١٨١) وَمِمَّنْ خَلَقْنا أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ. (181) And among those We have created is a nation who guide by the truth and dispense justice thereby.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse (الآية): "They are the Imams (peace be upon them)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from them both (peace be upon them both): They both said: "We are them."

And al-Qummi said that this **الْآيَةَ ** is for the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon them) and their followers.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, this nation will surely split into seventy-three sects, all of which will be in the Fire except for one sect: وَمِمَّنْ خَلَقْنا أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ . This is the one that will be saved from this nation."

And from him (peace be upon him): "It means the nation (أمّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "This is for you, and the people of Moses were given the like of it."

And from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "It is for my nation (أمّتي); they take by the truth and they give by the truth. And the like of it was given to a people before you: وَمِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ." (159) And among the people of Moses is a nation who guide by the truth and dispense justice thereby.

I say: What is intended by these three narratives is a part of the nation (الأمّة), as is indicated by His saying "the like of it," and by what was narrated in al-Majma': "Indeed, among my nation (أمّتي) there will be a group upon the truth until Jesus son of Mary descends."

(١٨٢) وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُمْ (182) And those who deny Our signs, We will draw them gradually

We will bring them near, little by little, toward destruction until they fall into it suddenly. And the origin (أصل) of al-istidrāj (الاستدراج) is ascending and descending, degree after degree.

مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَعْلَمُونَ from whence they do not know.

[They do not know] what is intended for them. That is, when blessings (النعم) continuously flow upon them, they assume that it is a kindness (لطف) from Allah toward them, so they increase in insolence (بطراً) and immersion (انهماكاً) in error (الغيّ), until the word of punishment (كلمة العذاب) becomes due upon them.

Al-Qummi said: [It is] the renewal of blessings (النعم) concurrent with acts of disobedience (المعاصي).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "It is the servant who commits a sin, and then a blessing (النعمة) is renewed for him. That blessing distracts him from seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) for that sin."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "If Allah intends good for a servant, and he commits a sin, He follows it with a retribution (نقمة) and reminds him of seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار). But if He intends evil for a servant, and he commits a sin, He follows it with a blessing (نعمة) so that He makes him forget seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) and he persists in it. And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic:

سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُمْ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَعْلَمُونَ We will draw them gradually from whence they do not know.

[This is done] through blessings (النعم) concurrent with acts of disobedience (المعاصي)."

(١٨٣) وَأُمْلِي لَهُمْ ** (and I respite them) And I grant them respite. إِنَّ كَيْدِي مَتِينٌ (١) (My stratagem is indeed firm.) It is not repelled by anything. He only named it a stratagem (كيداً) because its outward appearance is beneficence (إحسان) and its inward reality is abandonment (خذلان).

footnote (١) *Al-Matin* (*المتين*) is one of His Names. And He is the Severe, the Powerful, Whom no weakness afflicts and no weariness (*لغوب*) touches. And the meaning in describing Him with strength (*القوّة*) and firmness (*المتانة*) is that He is Capable, possessing profound power (*بليغ الاقتدار*) over everything. And the verb *matana* (*متن*) (with *ḍamma*) means it became strong and hard (*متانة*), so it is firm (*متين*).

(١٨٤) أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا ما بِصاحِبِهِمْ مِنْ جِنَّةٍ ** (184) Have they not reflected that their companion has no madness? meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. مِنْ جِنَّةٍ ** meaning madness (جنون). It was narrated that He ascended al-Safa and called them tribe by tribe (فخذاً) (1), warning them of Allah's might (بأسَ الله). So one of them said: "Your companion is surely mad; he spent the night shouting (يهوت) (2) until morning." So [this Ayah] was revealed: **إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (He is nothing but a manifest warner), clarifying His warning in such a way that it is not hidden from any observer.

(١٨٥) أَوَلَمْ يَنْظُرُوا فِي مَلَكُوتِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ وَأَنْ عَسى أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدِ اقْتَرَبَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ يُؤْمِنُونَ (Have they not looked at the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and what things Allah has created, and that perhaps their term may have already drawn near? Then in what discourse after it will they believe?)

أَوَلَمْ يَنْظُرُوا (a look of contemplation) فِي مَلَكُوتِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ in their inner reality (باطنها) and their spirits (أرواحها) وَما خَلَقَ اللهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ (and what Allah has created of anything) from that upon which the name 'thing' falls, from the categories of His creation which cannot be enumerated, so that they may be guided by them to the perfection of the power of their Maker, the oneness of their Originator, and the greatness of the status of their Owner and the One Who manages their affair, so that the truth of what He calls them to may become manifest to them. وَأَنْ عَسى (And that perhaps)—meaning, and that perhaps—أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدِ اقْتَرَبَ أَجَلُهُمْ (their term may have already drawn near), meaning, in the drawing near of their terms and the expectation of their arrival, so they hasten to seek the truth and turn towards that which saves them before the sudden seizure (3) (مغافصة) of death and the descent of the punishment. فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ (Then in what discourse after it)—after the Quran—يُؤْمِنُونَ (will they believe) if they do not believe in it? And the meaning is: Perhaps their term has drawn near, so what is wrong with them that they do not hasten to believe in the Quran? And what do they await after its clarity? For if they do not believe in it, then in what discourse more worthy than it do they wish to believe?

(١٨٦) مَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَلا هادِيَ لَهُ وَيَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (٤) (4) Whomever Allah leads astray, there is no guide for him, and He leaves them to wander in their transgression.

Al-Qummi said: He used to entrust him to his own soul (نفسه). And وَيَذَرُهُمْ was read with the yāʾ (بالياء), and with it (the yāʾ) and with the jazm (بالجزم). As if it was said: No one guides him other than Him, and He leaves them.

(١٨٧) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ (187) They question you concerning the Hour meaning the Resurrection (القيامة), and it is one of the dominant names (for it). أَيَّانَ مُرْساها when will be its anchorage? When will be its anchorage (إرساؤها), meaning its establishment (إثباتها) and stabilization (استقرارها). قُلْ إِنَّما عِلْمُها عِنْدَ رَبِّي Say, 'Its knowledge is only with my Lord. He has reserved (5) it for Himself (استأثر به); He has not informed of it any angel near [to Him] nor any Messenger Prophet. لا يُجَلِّيها لِوَقْتِها None shall disclose it at its time He does not manifest it at its time (لا يظهرها في وقتها) إِلَّا هُوَ except Him. meaning that the concealment of it remains continuous from others until the time of its occurrence, and the lām (اللام) is for timing (للتوقيت). ثَقُلَتْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ It is momentous in the heavens and the earth. It is mighty (عظمت) upon its inhabitants, from the angels and the two weighty ones (الثقلين), due to its terror (لهو لها) and severity (شدّتها). لا تَأْتِيكُمْ إِلَّا بَغْتَةً It will not come to you except suddenly.' Suddenly (فجأة), while you are heedless (على غفلة).

In al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The Hour will stir up the people while a man is repairing his cistern (حوضه), and a man is watering his livestock (ماشيته), and a man is setting up his merchandise (سلعته) in his market, and a man is lowering and raising his scale (ميزانه).

يَسْئَلُونَكَ كَأَنَّكَ حَفِيٌّ عَنْها They question you as if you were eager to know it. It is said (قيل): meaning, knowledgeable of it (عالم بها). Its origin is as if you were persistent (حفيت) in asking until you knew it, meaning you sought full knowledge (استقصيت) and were insistent (الحفت) (2). قُلْ إِنَّما عِلْمُها عِنْدَ اللهِ Say, 'Its knowledge is only with Allah, He has not granted it to any of His creation, because it is part of the knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب) which Allah has reserved for Himself. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ but most people do not know.' that He is the One specialized in the knowledge of it.

Al-Qummi says: Indeed, Quraysh sent Al-ʿĀṣ ibn Wāʾil al-Sahmī, Al-Naḍr ibn al-Ḥārith ibn Kaldah, and ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ to Najrān so that they might learn questions from the Jewish scholars to ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Among these questions was that they should ask Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when the Hour would commence. If he claimed knowledge of that, then he would be a liar, for Allah has not informed any angel near [to Him] nor any Messenger Prophet of the commencement of the Hour. When they asked Him, [this verse] was revealed.

(١٨٨) قُلْ لا أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي نَفْعاً وَلا ضَرًّا ** (188) Say, 'I have no control over any benefit or harm for myself (It means) bringing benefit nor repelling harm, and this is a manifestation of servitude (العبودية) and disavowal of claiming knowledge of the unseen things (الغيوب). إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ except what Allah may wish. from that, so He inspires me with it and grants me success (يوفّقني) for it. وَلَوْ كُنْتُ أَعْلَمُ الْغَيْبَ لَاسْتَكْثَرْتُ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ وَما مَسَّنِيَ السُّوءُ Had I known the unseen, I would have acquired much good, and no ill would have touched me. In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means poverty (الفقر). And al-Qummi said: I would have chosen health (الصحّة) and safety (السلامة) for myself. إِنْ أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** I am only a warner and a bearer of good news to a people who have faith.' For they are the ones who benefit.

footnote (١) أي كأنّك استحفيت بالسؤال عنها حتّى علمتها والحفيّ المستقصي بالسؤال عن الشّيء وأعفى فلان في المسألة إذا ألحّ فيها وبالغ. (1) Meaning, it is as if you were persistent (استحفيت) in asking about it until you knew it. Al-Hafiyy (الحفيّ) is the one who is exhaustive in questioning about a thing, and a'fa fulan fi al-mas'alah (أعفى فلان في المسألة) means that he insisted upon it and exaggerated (in his request).

footnote (٢) قوله تعالى لا يَسْئَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحافاً أي الحاحاً وهو أن يلازم المسئول حتّي يعطيه من قولهم لحفني من فضل لحافه أي أعطاني من فضل ما عنده والمعنى على ما قيل لا يسألون وإن سألوا عن ضرورة لم يلحفوا. (2) The Almighty's saying: لا يَسْئَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحافاً ** They do not ask people importunately. Meaning, insistence (إلحاحاً), which is that one clings to the person being asked until he gives him, derived from their saying: lahafani min fadl lihafih (لحفني من فضل لحافه), meaning: he gave me from the surplus of what he possessed. The meaning, according to what has been said, is that they do not ask, and if they do ask out of necessity, they do not insist (لم يلحفوا).

(١٨٩) هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ (189) He it is who created you from a single soul. which is the soul (نفس) of Adam وَجَعَلَ مِنْها from the excess (فضل) of his clay (طينتها) زَوْجَها Eve (حواء) لِيَسْكُنَ إِلَيْها that he might find rest in her, so that he might feel intimacy (يأنس) with her and find tranquility (يطمئن) in her فَلَمَّا تَغَشَّاها he had intercourse (جامعها) with her حَمَلَتْ حَمْلاً خَفِيفاً which was light upon her فَمَرَّتْ بِهِ meaning, she continued (استمرت) with the pregnancy (بالحمل) فَلَمَّا أَثْقَلَتْ But when she grew heavy, she became heavy (ذات ثقل) due to the growth (كبر) of the child in her womb دَعَوَا اللهَ رَبَّهُما لَئِنْ آتَيْتَنا صالِحاً they both supplicated Allah, their Lord: 'If You give us a sound [child], a sound (سويّاً) child, free (بريئاً) from defect (الآفة) لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ. we will surely be among the grateful.'

(١٩٠) فَلَمَّا آتاهُما صالِحاً جَعَلا لَهُ شُرَكاءَ فِيما آتاهُما ** (190) But when He gave them a sound one, they ascribed partners to Him in what He had given them. And it was read as شِرْكاً (partnership/association) using the verbal noun (المصدر). فَتَعالَى اللهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ.** So exalted is Allah above what they ascribe as partners [to Him]!

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are Adam and Eve (حوّاء), and their association (شركهما) was merely an association in obedience (شرك طاعة), not an association in worship (شرك عبادة).

And al-Qummi added, saying: They assigned a share (نصيباً) to al-Harith in the creation of Allah, but they did not associate Iblis in the worship of Allah, after mentioning a detailed narrative (حديثاً مبسوطاً) concerning this, which he narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), conforming to what the general public (العامّة) narrated regarding it, which is unfitting for the Prophets.

And what is understood from that narrative is that the meaning of their association فِيما آتاهُما ** Allah is their naming their children عبد الحارث (Servant of al-Harith), and al-Harith (الحارث) is the name of Iblis, and Iblis had induced them to do that through his deception (تغريره).

And it was said that its meaning is the naming [of children] as عبد عزّى (Servant of Uzza), عبد مناة (Servant of Manat), عبد يغوث (Servant of Yaghuth), and similar names of idols (أسماء الأصنام).

And the meaning of جَعَلا لَهُ ** is that their children (أولادهما) made شُرَكاءَ ** (partners) in what came to their children (أولادهما), based on the omission of the annexed term (المضاف) and the substitution of the term annexed to it (المضاف إليه) in its place in both instances.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): al-Ma'mun said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, is it not your saying that the Prophets are infallible (معصومون)?" He replied: "Yes." He said: "Then what is the meaning of the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَمَّا آتاهُما صالِحاً جَعَلا لَهُ شُرَكاءَ فِيما آتاهُما?"

Al-Rida (peace be upon him) replied to him: "Eve (حوّاء) bore Adam (peace be upon him) five hundred pregnancies (خمسمائة بطن), in every pregnancy a male and a female. And Adam and Eve made a covenant with Allah, the Exalted, and supplicated to Him, saying: لَئِنْ آتَيْتَنا صالِحاً لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ فَلَمَّا آتاهُما صالِحاً ** (189) If You give us a sound one, we will surely be among the grateful. (190) But when He gave them a sound one, of the offspring, a sound creation (خلقاً سويّاً), free from chronic illness (الزمانة) and defect (العاهة). What He gave them was two types (صنفين): one type males (ذكراناً) and one type females (اناثاً). So, the two types (الصنفان) ascribed شُرَكاءَ (partners) to Allah, the Glorified, in what He gave them, and they did not thank Him with the gratitude of their parents to Him, the Mighty and Majestic. فَتَعالَى اللهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** So exalted is Allah above what they ascribe as partners [to Him]! al-Ma'mun then said: "I bear witness that you are truly the son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

(١٩١) **أَيُشْرِكُونَ ما لا يَخْلُقُ شَيْئاً وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ ** (191) Do they associate [with Him] that which creates nothing and are themselves created? It means the idols.

(١٩٢) وَلا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ لَهُمْ لعبدتهم نَصْراً وَلا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَنْصُرُونَ ** (192) And they are not able to grant them any help, nor can they help themselves. لَهُمْ ** (for them) refers to their worshippers (لعبدتهم). نَصْراً ** (help) وَلا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَنْصُرُونَ ** (nor can they help themselves) meaning they cannot repel from themselves whatever afflicts them.

(١٩٣) إِنْ تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدى لا يَتَّبِعُوكُمْ ** (193) If you invoke them for guidance, they will not follow you. It is the same to you whether you invoke them or remain silent. إِنْ تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدى لا يَتَّبِعُوكُمْ ** (If you invoke them for guidance, they will not follow you) bears two possible meanings. The first is that the address (الخطاب) is directed to the Muslims, and the pronoun هُم refers to the polytheists (المشركين), meaning that if you call the polytheists to Islam (الإِسلام), they will not respond to you (لا يجيبوكم). The second is that the address (الخطاب) is directed to the polytheists (المشركين), and the pronoun هُم refers to the idols (الأصنام), meaning that if you call upon the idols to guide you (يهدوكم), they will not follow you (لا يتّبعوكم) toward your desired aim (مرادكم) and will not respond to you (يجيبوكم) as Allah responds to you. And the word يتبعوكم was recited with takhfif (بالتخفيف) (lightened form). سَواءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ أَدَعَوْتُمُوهُمْ أَمْ أَنْتُمْ صامِتُونَ. (It is the same to you whether you invoke them or remain silent.)

(١٩٤) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (194) Indeed those whom you invoke besides Allah meaning, you worship them and name them gods (آلهة) besides Him, glory be to Him, عِبادٌ أَمْثالُكُمْ ** are servants like yourselves. owned, subjugated. فَادْعُوهُمْ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكُمْ ** So invoke them, and let them answer you in your important matters, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful. that they are gods (آلهة).

(١٩٥) أَلَهُمْ أَرْجُلٌ يَمْشُونَ بِها أَمْ لَهُمْ أَيْدٍ يَبْطِشُونَ بِها أَمْ لَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ يُبْصِرُونَ بِها أَمْ لَهُمْ آذانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِها قُلِ ادْعُوا شُرَكاءَكُمْ ** (195) Do they have feet with which they walk, or do they have hands with which they strike, or do they have eyes with which they see, or do they have ears with which they hear? Say: 'Invoke your partners, and seek help from them against My enmity, ثُمَّ كِيدُونِ ** then scheme against Me, so go to extremes in what you and your partners are capable of regarding what is hateful to Me, فَلا تُنْظِرُونِ ** and do not grant Me respite. So do not grant Me respite, for I do not care about you, due to My reliance upon the guardianship (ولاية) of Allah and His protection (حفظه).

(١٩٦) إِنَّ وَلِيِّيَ Indeed my guardian is my helper (ناصري) and my protector (حافظي) اللهُ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ الْكِتابَ Allah, who has sent down the Book, which is the Quran (القرآن) وَهُوَ يَتَوَلَّى الصَّالِحِينَ and it is He who guards the righteous, meaning He helps them (ينصرهم) and protects them (ويحفظهم). (196) Indeed my guardian is Allah, who has sent down the Book, and it is He who guards the righteous.

(١٩٧) وَالَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَكُمْ وَلا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَنْصُرُونَ. (197) And those whom you invoke besides Him cannot help you, nor can they help themselves.

(١٩٨) وَإِنْ تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدى لا يَسْمَعُوا وَتَراهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لا يُبْصِرُونَ If you invite them to guidance, they will not hear. And you see them looking at you, yet they do not see. They resemble those who look at you (يشبهون الناظرين إليك) because they were fashioned in the form of one who looks at the person facing him (يواجهه). (198) If you invite them to guidance, they will not hear. And you see them looking at you, yet they do not see.

(١٩٩) خُذِ الْعَفْوَ Take to clemency, meaning: take what is readily available (ما عفا لك) to you from the actions and manners of people, and what they bring forth without difficulty (من غير كلفة) and with ease (وتسهّل). Do not demand what is hard upon them (ما يشقّ عليهم), and do not scrutinize them (ولا تداقّهم). Accept what is easy (الميسور) from them. Similar to this is His saying: "Make things easy (يسرّوا) and do not make them difficult (ولا تعسّروا)." The word الْعَفْوَ (clemency) is that which is the opposite of hardship (الجهد).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah disciplined His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with this (command), meaning: "Take from them what is apparent (ما ظهر) and what is easy (وما تيسر)." He said: "And al-'Afw (العفو) is the moderate path (الوسط)."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He said to a man from Thaqif: "Beware of striking a Muslim, a Jew, or a Christian over a single dirham of tax (خراج), or selling an animal used for work over a single dirham. For we have been commanded to take the easy portion (العفو) from him."

وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ and enjoin what is good, meaning: the beautiful known (بالمعروف الجميل) of actions and the praiseworthy (الحميد) of manners وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِينَ and disregard the ignorant, and do not argue (ولا تمار) (1) with the foolish (السفهاء), nor recompense them with the like of their foolishness (سفههم). (199) Take to clemency, and enjoin what is good, and disregard the ignorant.

In al-Majma', it is narrated: When this Ayah was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked Gabriel (جبرئيل) about it. He replied: "I do not know until I ask the Knower (العالم)." Then he came to him and said: "O Muhammad, Allah commands you to forgive (تعفو) him who wronged you, to give (تعطي) him who deprived you, and to maintain ties (تصل) with him who cut you off."

And in al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah commanded His Prophet with noble manners (مكارم الأخلاق). There is no Ayah in the Quran more comprehensive of noble manners than this one.

And in al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Allah commanded His Prophet to deal gently (بمداراة) with people, so He said: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجاهِلِينَ (Take to clemency, and enjoin what is good, and disregard the ignorant).

**وَإِمَّا يَنْزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ نَزْغٌ ** (And if a temptation from Satan tempts you)

He pricks you (2) with a prick (نخس) from him in the heart, whispering to you contrary to what you have been commanded, such as the onset of anger. And النزغ and النسغ and النخس and الغرز are synonymous. He likened the whispering (وسوسة) to people—enticing them toward disobedience (المعاصي) and disturbing them—to the pricking (غرز) of the driver (of the animal) what he drives.

In al-Majma', when the preceding Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "How, O Lord, and anger?" Then was revealed:

**فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللهِ إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ ** (seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is all-hearing)

He hears your seeking of refuge (استعاذتك).

**عَلِيمٌ ** (all-knowing)

Of what contains the rectification (صلاح) of your affair.

footnote (١) الممارة is disputation (*المجادلة*).

footnote (٢) *نخس الدابّة* (pricking the animal) is like (*نصر*) and making a prick (*غرز*) in its rear or its side with a stick or the like. The origin of *النخس* is pushing (*الدفع*) and movement (*الحركة*).

(٢٠١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا إِذا مَسَّهُمْ طائِفٌ مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ ** (Indeed, those who are Godwary, when a visitation from Satan touches them) A touch (لمة) (1) from him, as if it circled them and revolved around them, yet was unable to affect them. And it was read (طيف) without the alif. تَذَكَّرُوا ** (they remember) what Allah commanded and forbade, فَإِذا هُمْ مُبْصِرُونَ ** (and behold, they perceive) the places of error and the snares of Satan, so they guard against them. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the servant who intends to commit a sin, then remembers, and thus restrains himself (فَيُمْسِكُ). And in one narration, he abandons it (فَيَدَعُهُ). And in another, he perceives and holds back (فَيُبْصِرُ وَيَقْصُرُ). And al-Qummi said: When Satan reminds them of acts of disobedience and incites them to commit them, they remember the Name of Allah, فَإِذا هُمْ مُبْصِرُونَ.** (and behold, they perceive).

(٢٠٢) وَإِخْوانُهُمْ ** (202) And their brethren) And the brethren of the devils (الشياطين), meaning those who did not fear [Allah] (الذين لم يتّقوا). The devils (الشياطين) extend/supply them (يَمُدُّونَهُمْ). And it was read with a ḍamma on the yāʾ and a kasra on the mīm [i.e., يُمِدُّونَهُمْ]. فِي الغَيِ ** (in error) through adornment (بالتزيين) and urging them towards it (والحمل عليه). ثُمَّ لا يُقْصِرُونَ (then they do not cease) They do not hold back from their temptation (اغوائهم) until they persist (يصرُّوا) and do not return, so they perish. Or the brethren (الاخوان) do not cease from the error (الغيّ).
