(203) وَإِذا لَمْ تَأْتِهِمْ بِآيَةٍ ** (203) And when you do not bring them a sign (a sign) from the Quran or a sign from what they proposed, قالُوا لَوْ لا اجْتَبَيْتَها ** they say, 'Why do you not choose it?' Why did you not gather it (جَمَعْتَهَا) as a fabrication (تَقَوُّلًا) from yourself, like the rest of what you recite, or why did you not request it from Allah? قُلْ إِنَّما أَتَّبِعُ ما يُوحى إِلَيَّ مِنْ رَبِّي ** Say, 'I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord.' I am not one who fabricates (مُخْتَلِق) the signs (الآيات) or I am not one who proposes (مُقْتَرِح) them. هذا ** This This (the Quran) are insights (بَصَائِرُ) for the hearts, by which they perceive the truth (الحق), مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةٌ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ. from your Lord, and a guidance and a mercy for a people who have faith.'

(204) وَإِذا قُرِئَ الْقُرْآنُ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ وَأَنْصِتُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (And when the Quran is recited, listen to it and be silent, so that you may receive [His] mercy.) It was said that it was revealed concerning the prayer (الصلاة), as they used to speak during it, so they were commanded to listen to the recitation of the Imam (الإِمام) and to be silent for Him.

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): If you are behind an Imam (إمام), do not recite anything in the first two units (الأوليين), and be silent for his recitation. And do not recite anything in the last two units (الأخيرتين), for Allah says to the believers: وَإِذا قُرِئَ الْقُرْآنُ (And when the Quran is recited)—meaning in the obligatory prayer (الفريضة) behind the Imam (الإِمام)—فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ وَأَنْصِتُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (listen to it and be silent, so that you may receive [His] mercy). And the last two units (الأخيرتان) follow the first two (الأوّليتين).

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If you are behind an Imam (إمام) whom you follow and trust, then his recitation suffices you. But if you wish to recite, then recite in that which is recited silently (يخافت به). If he recites loudly (جهر), then be silent. The Almighty Allah said: وَأَنْصِتُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (and be silent, so that you may receive [His] mercy). And al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both) who said: If you are behind an Imam (إمام) whom you follow, then be silent and glorify [Allah] (سبّح) within yourself.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Silence (الإِنصات) for the Quran is obligatory (يجب) in the prayer (الصلاة) and outside of it. And if the Quran is recited near you, silence (الإِنصات) and listening (الاستماع) become obligatory upon you.

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who leads the people in prayer (يؤمّ القوم)—and you are not pleased with him—in a prayer where the recitation is loud (يجهر فيها بالقراءة). He said: "If you hear the Book of Allah being recited, then be silent for it." It was said: "But he testifies to my polytheism (الشرك)." He said: "If he disobeys Allah, then obey Allah." [The narrator said:] I responded to him, but he refused to grant me a dispensation (يرخّص لي). It was said: "Should I then pray in my house and then go out to him?" He said: "That is up to you (أنت وذاك)." And he said: Indeed, Ali (peace be upon him) was in the Dawn Prayer (صلاة الصبح), and Ibn al-Kawwa, who was behind him, recited: وَلَقَدْ أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ (Certainly, it has been revealed to you and to those before you: 'If you ascribe partners to Allah, your work shall certainly fail and you shall surely be among the losers.') So Ali (peace be upon him) was silent, out of reverence for the Quran, until he finished the verse. Then he returned to his recitation. Then Ibn al-Kawwa repeated the verse, so Ali (peace be upon him) was silent again. Then he recited, and Ibn al-Kawwa repeated [the verse], so Ali (peace be upon him) was silent. Then he said: فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌّ وَلا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ الَّذِينَ لا يُوقِنُونَ (So be patient; Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let those who are not certain make you unsteady.) Then he completed the Surah and bowed (ركع).

I say: These two narrations and those similar in meaning, which conform to the apparent meaning of the Quran regarding the general obligation (عموم وجوب) of listening (الاستماع) and silence (الإِنصات), are interpreted by our companions (أصحابنا) and the general body of jurists (عامة الفقهاء) as referring to recommendation (الاستحباب) and its emphasis. Rather, the command has been narrated to recite behind the opponent (المخالف), even if you hear his recitation, provided there is no dissimulation (تقيّة).

footnote (1) And in the narration of Ibn Mas'ud regarding the son of Adam: There are two impulses (*لمّتان*): an impulse (*لمّة*) from the angel (*الملك*) and an impulse (*لمّة*) from Satan (*الشيطان*). The impulse (*اللّمة*) is the concern (*الهمّة*) and the thought (*الخطرة*) that falls into the heart. He meant the visitation (*إلمام*) of the angel or Satan to him and their proximity to him. Thus, whatever thoughts (*خطرات*) are of goodness are from the angel (*الملك*), and whatever thoughts (*خطرات*) are of evil are from Satan (*الشيطان*).

(205) وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ ** (And remember your Lord in your soul) This is general (عامّ) for every remembrance (ذكر). تَضَرُّعاً وَخِيفَةً ** (humbly and fearfully) Meaning: being humble (متضرّعاً) and fearful (خائفاً). وَدُونَ الْجَهْرِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ ** (and without being loud in your speech) Meaning: with the tongue, because the remembrance (الذكر) in the soul and without being loud (دون الجهر), which are expressed as secrecy (السر), are more conducive to sincerity (الإِخلاص), further removed from showing off (الرياء), and closer to acceptance. بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ (in the mornings and the evenings) Meaning: in the early morning (بالغدوة) and the late afternoons (وَالعشياتِ), due to the merit (فضل) of these two times. وَلا تَكُنْ مِنَ الْغافِلِينَ ** (and do not be among the oblivious.) Meaning: those oblivious (الْغافِلِينَ) to the remembrance of Allah, who are distracted (اللّاهين) from Him.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of them (peace be upon them both): The angel does not record anything except what he hears. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ تَضَرُّعاً وَخِيفَةً ** (And remember your Lord in your soul, humbly and fearfully). Thus, no one knows the reward (ثواب) of that remembrance (الذّكر) within a man's soul except Allah, due to His greatness (لعظمته). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated marfu‘an from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَاذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ ** (And remember your Lord in your soul) means being submissive (مستكيناً). وَخِيفَةً ** (and fearfully) means fear of His punishment (عذابه). وَدُونَ الْجَهْرِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ ** (and without being loud in your speech) means without being loud in recitation (القراءة). بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ ** (in the mornings and the evenings) means in the early morning (بالغدوة) and the late afternoon (العشي).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Allah [said]: "Whoever remembers Me secretly (سرّاً), I remember him publicly (علانيةً)." (1) And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever remembers Allah in secret (السرّ) has remembered Allah much. Indeed, the hypocrites (المنافقين) used to remember Allah publicly (علانية) but did not remember Him in secret (السرّ). So Allah the Almighty said: يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (They show off to people and do not remember Allah except a little)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: You should say in the evening (عند المساء): "لا إله إلّا الله وحده لا شريك له له الملك وله الحمد يحيي ويميت ويميت ويحيي وهو حيّ لا يموت وهو على كل شيء قدير" (There is no god except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs the dominion, and to Him belongs all praise. He gives life and causes death, and He causes death and gives life, and He is the Ever-Living Who does not die, and He has power over all things). It was said: "[Should I add] 'In His Hand is the good (بيده الخير)'?" He said: "Indeed, in His Hand is the good (بيده الخير), but say as I tell you, ten times. And [say]: 'أعوذ بالله السميع العليم' (I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing) when the sun rises and when it sets, ten times."

(٢٠٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ** (Indeed, those who are near your Lord) It was said that this means the angels, and al-Qummi said it means the Prophets, the Messengers, and the Imams (peace be upon them). لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبادَتِهِ وَيُسَبِّحُونَهُ ** (do not disdain to worship Him, and they glorify Him) And they declare Him free from imperfection. وَلَهُ يَسْجُدُونَ ** (and to Him they prostrate.) And they single Him out for worship and humility, and they do not associate anyone else with Him. Here is the first prostration (سجدة) of the Quran.

And in the Hadith: When the son of Adam reads the verse of prostration (السجدة) and prostrates, Satan withdraws, weeping, and says: "Woe unto me! This one was commanded to prostrate, and he prostrated, so for him is Paradise. And I was commanded to prostrate, but I disobeyed, so for me is the Fire."

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surah al-A'raf every month will be, on the Day of Resurrection, among those who *لا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (there will be no fear for them, nor will they grieve). If he recites it every Friday, he will be among those who will not be reckoned with on the Day of Resurrection." And Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, knows best everything.

footnote (١) قال في الوافي بيان ذكر الله سرّاً يشمل الذّكر في النّفس الذي في مقابلة الغفلة والذّكر على اللّسان بالإِخفات الذي يقابل الجهر كذا ذكر الله لعبده علانية يشمل ذكره بالخير يوم القيامة على رؤوس الأشهاد وذكره بالجميل في الدنيا على السن العباد. (1) He said in al-Wafi: The explanation of remembering Allah secretly (ذِكْرُ اللهِ سِرّاً) includes the remembrance in the soul (الذّكر في النّفس) which is the opposite of heedlessness (الغفلة), and the remembrance upon the tongue (الذّكر على اللّسان) through lowering the voice (الإِخفات) which is the opposite of raising the voice (الجهر). Likewise, Allah's remembrance of His servant openly (ذِكْرُ اللهِ لِعَبْدِهِ عَلانِيَةً) includes His remembrance of him with goodness on the Day of Resurrection before the witnesses (رُؤُوسِ الأَشْهَادِ), and His remembrance of him with praise (الجميل) in this world upon the tongues of the servants (العِبَاد).
